Thanks for the feedback here - I think there's a few items I might be able to

Matthew

date: 2021-12-22
to: davehorlick@yahoo.com
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 17de4387eb8e8e5e
---

Hey David,

Hope all is well! We turned off our Algorand accelerator email addresses,
so hope you don’t mind me reaching out here.

I’m in LA for the next month or two, would love to catch up if you’re
around. I’m working on something re launching a token/web3 product for
engineering community based recruitment, and would love your thoughts on
it, as well as to hear how things are coming along with Ockams.

Also Alex Potter mentioned you had a good conversation a while ago - I like
him a lot, and it sounds like the Oasis accelerator will be even more
startup friendly terms than the Algorand one (although certainly doesn’t
have the onchain traction that Algorand does).

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-12-22
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 17de38da24cfd9a3
---

Hi Pat,
Sorry for the delay since we last spoke - I ended up spending an extra week in
Costa Rica, and then have been quite heads down on some work and projects since
getting back.
I really enjoyed our conversation when we spoke a few weeks ago - and also
ordered 12 Seconds of Silence that I'm excited to read once I finish this
fascinating but super slow and dry book about Damien Hurst and the economics of
contemporary art.
I'd love to find another time to catch up and continue our conversation. I've
been putting together a bit of a doc/wiki outlining the metaverse, and have been
finding quite a few opportunities come my way in terms of NFTs and metaverse,
which I'd love to get your take on both.
Hope all is well with you and the family, and that you all have a lovely 
holidays!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-12-22
to: Sheng Huang <shenghuang06@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Ever dealt with speaker contracts?
msg_id: 17de093d711acb1f
---

Hey man - good to hear from you as always :) my mom was just talking about you
yesterday, has  really enjoyed your conversations.
Find attached the typical advisory contract I use - it's cash only, I have
another one I use for equity if you need that. Def a little different but you
can probably just alter the scope and payment items for what you need
I'm in LA through early Jan, and then probably up to Santa Cruz/the Bay for
middle  of Jan - would love to catch up, or if you're back down in LA.
Also have a friend from college who lives in Hayes Valley, super  smart guy,
runs a medium sized family consulting business and we're vaguely working on
launching an NFT fund together (and he's doing a lot of mapping/frameworking -
good at parallel high-level/low-level problem solving). Let me know if you might
be interested in connecting!

---
date: 2021-12-21
to: andrew@optimotive.co
subject: Fwd: TechTree Token overview
msg_id: 17dda965ce626388
---

Hey man,

Hope you’ve been doing well! Looks like I’m going to be starting a full
time job taking a web2 product web3 and launching their token. Would love
to pick your brain on it around the launch/marketing side, and see if there
might be anyone you know I should connect with. Some info attached below,
and let me know if you might know anyone!

Best,

Matthew







About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:


   1.

   Deck <https://brieflink.com/v/bazah>
   2.

   Light paper:
   <https://www.notion.so/techtreedev/Request-for-funding-TechTree-1fb248c8640f45199e9b4f16664a4123>

   3.

   Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1c5BaA39vY8i8DbkXQl63wfxU0MlGs95-dEJyVWb5Wlw/edit#>



ᐧ

---
date: 2021-12-21
to: "Muñeca Diaz" <muneca@voic.es>
subject: Re: Tue
msg_id: 17dda80a3e96eed4
---

Hi Muneca,
Thanks for this - it looks like I've gotten in to  the group, and please find
below a bio and attached a headshot.
Let me know if there's anything else, and any other logistics for tomorrow.
Best,
Matthew

Matthew has worked in technology for more than 10 years and blockchain for more
than 5. He has built companies from launch to exit, consulted some of world's 
largest brands to launch cutting edge products and startup programs, advised
multiple startups on their way to becoming unicorns, and invested in dozens of
seed stage companies.

date: 2021-12-20
to: Kevin Meyer <kfm730@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: TechTree overview - token
msg_id: 17dd525317fbbc04
---

About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:


   1.

   Deck <https://brieflink.com/v/bazah>
   2.

   Light paper:
   <https://www.notion.so/techtreedev/Request-for-funding-TechTree-1fb248c8640f45199e9b4f16664a4123>

   3.

   Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1c5BaA39vY8i8DbkXQl63wfxU0MlGs95-dEJyVWb5Wlw/edit#>



ᐧ

Hi Vincent,

It was great to catch up with you the other day and hear a bit about what
you're up to, and your take on what I'm working on.

As mentioned re: follow up, I've pasted a bit of info about TechTree below,
and have cc'ed Pawel on this message, who is a long time entrepreneur and
mentor/advisor/collaborator of mine, and our CEO. He can answer your
questions around social tokens, executive search vertical, and roadmap
better than I can at this point (and I might join in to listen in as I
continue getting up to speed with things here). He also has some
interesting connections with the art world, so I imagine you'll have an
interesting conversation on a few fronts.

Also, thanks for the tip on the Spatial mints - I got both of them last
week :) - please do send along anything like that if you think of it!

And of course, let me know if there's anything else we discussed that was
of particular interest, or if there's anything you're working on I can help
with.

Best,

Matthew





About TechTree:

A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:

---
date: 2021-12-18
to: Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: Re: Call today/tomorrow? Analytics dashboard
msg_id: 17dcc35bf881f3be
---

Hi Alicia,
Sorry for the delay - I've been trying to compile a thorough update, and look in
as many places as possible to try and surface the data you wanted for the
dashboard.
I've taken quite a few more dives into the data available through various
sources, and think I may have reached a bit of an impasse at this point (or
maybe I'm not seeing something). I've laid out what I'm looking at and options
below, so hopefully this provides a clear view of the situation/understanding of
potential paths that could be taken from here (which also is somewhat timely, as
we're starting to approach the 6 days of work limit we had set for the project
initially - about 5.5 now).
I have set up what I can with the data that is readily available, as well as
created a new tab to attempt to streamline the creation of the slide you shared
with me (and lay the foundation for that as a fully functional report). There's
also the previously mentioned dashboard of charts with what I think will be some
of the more valuable data points of what was available (some of which I have
only been able to pull the most recent month data point for, but should be
useful when there's a bit more entered and some calculations stabilize - and
also happy to add some specific points to these).

I've tried to reformat the data/pull additional data points that you included in
your desired dashboard mockup, and was able to get a couple more, but
encountered some issues: * Total Team Plan Participants: I'm not sure how to
   differentiate between these users and other users. Please let me know if
   there's something you can point me to.
 * Simulator: I am able to see a membership type number
   in column S of the member/user export file. However, for the simulator type
   (20338) I see no results. I have still listed instructions in cell E21 of the
   KPI Input tab for how to pull this easily should that metric go live in that
   field, but it is a very manual pull. I have also created a row to calculate
   New Simulation Users from this field, but again, there are no results thus
   far.
 * Competency Framework: The number I am seeing from the
   LMS Quizzes export file is 4 total all time - this does not seem correct.
   Where can I find this data?
 * I have looked through pretty much every data source
   within your WP backend, and cannot find anywhere to accurately/usably surface
   a count of Affiliate Referrals, Member Referrals, Future CFO participants,
   CFO programme participants, or Mentees (and the related "New" and "Total"
   metrics from your dashboard). Let me know if there is somewhere I can find
   these.


If you might know where I can find a more solid source for the above, please do
let me know. I was hoping the MemberPress user data files would give me more to
work with, but it seems apart from transaction counts and module completion
logs, there's not much I can find that will allow me to benchmark the stats I
mentioned above. Please do let me know if you think I'm missing a more
accessible data source, but I believe I've exported just about every report and
viewed just about every member/user/group/etc summary I can find in the various
plugins in the Wordpress back-end.

From here. there are a few paths I could see pursuing: * 1. Revisit current
   dashboard and iterate with currently collectible data - this is probably the
   quickest solution to at least get the ball rolling, although I know is not as
   comprehensive as what you envisioned or what I had hoped to do. Iterating
   this with available data and polishing it up will likely get us near the 6
   days of work mark we had previously agreed on. As part of this, I could also
   plug in a quick Google Data Studio dashboard which might help with some of
   the slicing you want, as well as create a couple more charts in the Google
   Sheet for some visualization based on the data in the KPI Inputs tab. And do
   let me know if there's anything you think I might not be considering in terms
   of data sources I might use.
 * 2. Focus on Google
   Analytics and attempt to rely on that for tracking - As I believe I mentioned
   when we last spoke, for some of the cohort analysis views (slices) that you
   had wanted re: the last slide in your deck, some of these will probably have
   to be done with Google Analytics reports until a full data integration is
   done (which i can set up a few to get the ball rolling on this).
   Unfortunately, Google Analytics misses quite a bit of data due to cookie
   blockers, but I imagine the usage of this is not particularly high with
   desktop users in your audience. Most of this process would need a decent JS
   engineer for a week or so to deploy. I do also have a rough goal spec I've
   been working on for you that will likely serve as a solid draft of a spec to
   hand off to them, but will likely need a bit of iteration (and I had put on
   the backburner to focus on the more analytics oriented tasts)
 * 3. Set up CDP - likely
   beyond your budget, but probably the only way to really reverse engineer the
   dashboard you want due to the number of different data silos and slices you
   want


Happy to present this information to you, Dan, and Kevin if that would be
helpful. And if you think I'm missing something, please do send a link to where
I can find a relevant report, send a Loom, etc, or suggest a time for a call
next week if you'd like (although Monday doesn't work for me).
Best,
Matthew

About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties

---
date: 2021-12-12
to: "Gabe O'Leary" <oleary.gabe@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: TechTree overview - token
msg_id: 17db0f7f484ef93f
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Pawel Tomczuk <pawel@mytechtree.io>
Date: Mon, 29 Nov 2021 at 13:52
Subject: TechTree overview - token
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hi Matt,

Here's the overview I'm using:

About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:


   1.

   Deck <https://brieflink.com/v/bazah>
   2.

   Light paper:
   <https://www.notion.so/techtreedev/Request-for-funding-TechTree-1fb248c8640f45199e9b4f16664a4123>

   3.

   Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1c5BaA39vY8i8DbkXQl63wfxU0MlGs95-dEJyVWb5Wlw/edit#>




Pawel TomczukCo-founder & CEO
+44 7484146499 <+44%201632%20960511>
pawel@mytechtree.io
https://mytechtree.io/
ᐧ

date: 2021-12-06
to: ross@kontinuous.co, Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: Ross/Grant intro
msg_id: 17d9113549225cd1
---

Hi Grant,
I mentioned Ross was the founder of Kontinuous (which I've invested in/was in
the Algorand accelerator I ran, in process of rebranding to zest), who is
setting up a website-widget based funding solution for consumer brands to
continuously raise funding from their communities. He was interested in
connecting in general, and although might be looking for an intro to someone at 
Coinfirm  as well if you might be able to make it or talk to him a little about
that.
Ross, please meet Grant, who runs Copernic Space, a fundraising site focused on
democratizing space financing by allowing consumers to invest in space. He's
looking at various fundraising options, and I'm also working to plan and execute
 their first sale on the platform, where we're selling cargo space on a Lunar
Rover that will be launched next year as tradable NFTs. He was also a cofounder
and led the marketing for a number of years at Coinfirm.
I'll let you two take it from here.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-12-06
to: Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: Re: GrowCFO SEO Checklist
msg_id: 17d8e0c3af182c48
---

Hi Alicia,
Thanks for sending along.
This is certainly a solid checklist - some parts are quite similar to the more 
full-fledged SEO evaluation that I often run (although I believe we had decided
not to to keep costs down/as quite a lot of it you would have already done
intuitively compared to most of my clients, but I imagine I've shown you this at
some point before).
Most of the points on the checklist are more one-off implementations though (or
even more strategic advice in some cases), quite a few of which had already been
done when we started, a handful of which I set up as we were going along, and
some that would be part of wider technical changes or a matter of ux/layouts.
In terms of what I had in mind when I mentioned a checklist SEO, it would be
focused on the 3 sections around Content, On Page, and Off Page, and trying to
maybe narrow those a little bit more to creating a process for when you launch a
new page in terms of production, seeding cross-promotion/preparing linking,
content launch, initial content marketing, and driving
cross-proportion/linking.
This is the template I typically use to start with - happy to chat on this more
tangibly when we next talk.
Hope this helps, and let me know if you have any other thoughts or questions in
the meantime.
Best,
Matthew

You can see time tracking in this spreadsheet:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1s1BhTuhOY8CDLKFf7A79qTTY_
LYFmI7Em4kOJmrZkEw/edit#gid=1388238213

with probably a half day or so left).
As always, let me know if you have any thoughts or questions.
Best,

---
date: 2021-12-04
to: Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: GrowCFO KPI Benchmarking/Dashboarding
msg_id: 17d872f45b1aeb4b
---

Hi Alicia,
Starting a new thread re: analytics/dashboarding call and getting robust V1/2 up
before the holidays.
I've made some solid headway and am near wrapping up the KPI benchmarking and
setting up a very basic dashboard in Google Sheets that we can iterate from - 
please find linked here.
Please note: on the Dashboard tab, some charts  lines won't be created until we
capture a second data point at the end of the month. Also, I've mapped all the
data collection points and steps on the KPI Input tab (where I'm also doing the
basic benchmarking). You can generally ignore the Chart Staging tab.
Next step from here will be to have a quick call to plan any iteration or
additional data we want to capture here, as well as discuss what makes sense for
next steps (continue building data visualizations, set something up in Google
Data Studio (although thinking that might be overkill), some semi automation for
data import of MemberPress and LMS data, etc).
What's your schedule like Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday this week at the beginning
or end of workday? Let me know and I'll send around a calendar invite.
Hope you're having a good weekend,
Matthew

date: 2021-12-04
to: Ahsan Sohail <ahsansohail22@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: TechTree overview - token
msg_id: 17d86a1279e7d244
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Pawel Tomczuk <pawel@mytechtree.io>
Date: Mon, 29 Nov 2021 at 13:52
Subject: TechTree overview - token
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hi Matt,

Here's the overview I'm using:

About TechTree:



A social network where developers help each other to level up while getting
rewarded for the help they offer. Initially, we’re helping engineers to
help each other find other devs to work with and getting paid the same
amount of money as recruiters for it (over 10k USD per successful
recommendation). We have already paid our users over 300k USD in bounties
for such activities with the biggest single hire being worth 45k USD.

Our partners and clients are some of the biggest VCs and tech companies in
the world like: Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone or Personio, Tessian,
Advent, and many more.

We’re backed by some amazing investors like Frontline Ventures, Notion
Capital, and fantastic angels including ex CEO of Skype, CTO of Workday,
senior execs from Stackoverflow, Uber, Monzo and many more. We’re a 15
people team doing slightly over 50k USD monthly revenue.

Here’s some more information about us:


   1.

   Deck <https://brieflink.com/v/bazah>
   2.

   Light paper:
   <https://www.notion.so/techtreedev/Request-for-funding-TechTree-1fb248c8640f45199e9b4f16664a4123>

   3.

   Blog - why we decided to build TechTree:
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1c5BaA39vY8i8DbkXQl63wfxU0MlGs95-dEJyVWb5Wlw/edit#>




Pawel TomczukCo-founder & CEO
+44 7484146499 <+44%201632%20960511>
pawel@mytechtree.io
https://mytechtree.io/
ᐧ

I’m mot sure if this answers your question, but finally got a response to
my queries.

I would certainly trust the Xero and accountancy answers over my reporting
in the spreadsheet.

Best,

Matthew


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
Date: Thu, 2 Dec 2021 at 02:51
Subject: RE: 2 Queries for US tax filing
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Cc: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>


Morning Mathew



As per the accounts prepared for the period ending 31 December 2020, the
answers to your questions are as follows;



   1. Profit -   £17,433



   1. Salary – Nil



   1. Dividend 17,170



   1. There is no personal loan from the Director to the Vang Advisory as
   per the accounts



I trust this information answers queries.









Kind regards,



Sam

*From:* Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
*Sent:* 01 December 2021 17:33
*To:* Sam Cobbina <Sam@SHIELDACCOUNTANCY.CO.UK>
*Cc:* Kevin Gardner <Kevin@SHIELDACCOUNTANCY.CO.UK>
*Subject:* Re: 2 Queries for US tax filing



CAUTION: This email originated from outside your organization. Exercise
caution when opening attachments or clicking links, especially from unknown
senders.

Thanks Sam,



I believe the essence of what we need for this is 1) How much profit was
generated by Vang Advisory, 2) how much profit did I withdraw from Vang
Advisory through salary, dividends, etc (and in what proportions), and 3)
how much money did I personally loan Vang Advisory (all for calendar year
2020, and we'll need the same again very soon for calendar year 2021).



Let me know if/how I can help get this information,



Thanks,



Matthew

*Welcome to The Founder Spotlight where we highlight the incredible people
behind the companies we’ve backed at The Fund
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJwt0M2OhCAMAOCnGY5GW0Q4cNjLvoZBqA5ZRcPPzPr2i-MmpKRtmuarNZmWPZ762FNmVxjzeZAO9E4r5UyRlURx9E4jtrIDwZzmrpO9ZD6NcyTajF91joXYUabVW5P9Hq4BxQcE9tS9aBGMQGn51A-CFKFDACFbwB55d681xXkKljS9KJ57ILbqZ85HeuDXA77ry0-aS3DNy9aEeQ0tdN0VBoCON9CIAQWgUmriSMqZRvD4-479_ODttkCTypSysT-N3TcW9Waqz6dQu8uF-JSrYaz_VoLP50jBTCu5m5fvI33A40KBYj2eG03WXcVx0Q84cAE3p_o5l0oiB1b3ur1OBf1P-AOaUnuO>.
This week the spotlight is on Parker Mitchell
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJwlkM2OhSAMhZ_msjTyKy5YzOa-hkGoSi6igTqObz94TZrT5DRt8x1nEeYtX2bfCpJbBrx2MAnOEgERMjkK5CF4w3mrKVPEG-GplpqEMkwZYLUhGswHkP0YY3AWw5buhV50nJHFSAGuY94rNmnXu87bUTqlpHdq0lS2z1t7-ADJgYFfyNeWgESzIO7lxX9e7F3rPM8mhvQBH1LjtrVaIVXZbf5AHteAboEYq0OCYS2j9JaOMSoa1qiOK8b7vh8Fh97bRon8d2Y5vUS7zqwpx1jQus99mWSz2gofSqrT-Sb82hVwqH09UsBrgGTHCP5hxyfBbxrDDAlyTdYPFg1VXAslO94JxR7WGo4QutdcMFL_-q1uJYMLTEfy_9xqh8I>,
CEO and founder of Valence
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJwlkMmOhSAQRb_msTQyyLBg0Zv-DYJQ-EgrGoZn-_eNbVKpSm5NOdfZCsueL33spaI7mXodoBOcZYVaIaNWIJvoNaWjxIQjr5nHcpIoFhMywGbjqmtugI42r9HZGvd0LygmKEFvzbESgKUkzoUxSKomBmqiYxAzCCDheWubj5AcaPhAvvYEaNXvWo_yol8v8t3jPM_hY9d7aHB7F1DUZCQY30kQgtlABi4oJ1QpNTMKytuBs_x75im82LgtZChtLtW6n35hQ1lvtjPGknp3uUH-5c5het1aivUykOy8gn8Q62PUP7RZIEHuBnpjq8acSsYnQQXj5EHqHjAmlaSMoP7X730r6fqG0JL_A7tIfhg>
a SaaS company building digital team-based tools that enable the world's
largest companies to transform their organizational culture and improve
performance.*
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJxVUsuO5CAM_JrmlohnIAcOo5H2NyIeTpqdBLJApqf365ckp5WQbWzZVXLZmQpLym-9p1LRaab63kFHeJUVaoWMjgJ5Cl4zhhWhA_Kae6KEQqFMcwbYTFh1zQeg_bBrcKaGFM-GkUtG0VMrykGpEcvZUMo9d8QL72AGwIJi4W9Yc_gA0YGGb8jvFAGt-lnrXh7s40F_ted87MthSzXuq3dpa6mwmQWan6G65-nbmJoe9PPPHbCPJSXf_vM67TktGUoJ39DypQLsreOGoOIEESdMM_ZwX9DquWsMrXWKd9YY1nEmoRsFUZ3xllELUo6K94X1ZjN_UzSvcvNqM-5dXOFFslxhmzYDcaLzyo8dd0Z1VhLZMcKllZbNVrGJYIF_Boz73zssKGiKKSGnkZQS3tN-kGygbBxHyxmM3vQDzz-vLOYHx9tC_1sSynozTcdQYqsup1hXuvGbmt-OGOp7gmjsCv6Wsd7HcAk7LRAhtyPxk6maDEzxQUgm-UBv2ZrOnKtRMU5Rw_WpdUVdnzAf0f8Dr6rFEA>

Every coach's job is to build a team that's more than the sum of the parts.
That's obvious in sports, but it's ever more clear that the best companies
are helping their leaders do this as well. When employees are happy, they
are more productive and stay longer; and a large part of that happiness has
to do with their managers and co-workers. CEO Parker Mitchell
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJwlkM2OhSAMhZ_msjTyKy5YzOa-hkGoSi6igTqObz94TZrT5DRt8x1nEeYtX2bfCpJbBrx2MAnOEgERMjkK5CF4w3mrKVPEG-GplpqEMkwZYLUhGswHkP0YY3AWw5buhV50nJHFSAGuY94rNmnXu87bUTqlpHdq0lS2z1t7-ADJgYFfyNeWgESzIO7lxX9e7F3rPM8mhvQBH1LjtrVaIVXZbf5AHteAboEYq0OCYS2j9JaOMSoa1qiOK8b7vh8Fh97bRon8d2Y5vUS7zqwpx1jQus99mWSz2gofSqrT-Sb82hVwqH09UsBrgGTHCP5hxyfBbxrDDAlyTdYPFg1VXAslO94JxR7WGo4QutdcMFL_-q1uJYMLTEfy_9xqh8I>
founded Valence
<https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJwlkMmOhSAQRb_msTQyyLBg0Zv-DYJQ-EgrGoZn-_eNbVKpSm5NOdfZCsueL33spaI7mXodoBOcZYVaIaNWIJvoNaWjxIQjr5nHcpIoFhMywGbjqmtugI42r9HZGvd0LygmKEFvzbESgKUkzoUxSKomBmqiYxAzCCDheWubj5AcaPhAvvYEaNXvWo_yol8v8t3jPM_hY9d7aHB7F1DUZCQY30kQgtlABi4oJ1QpNTMKytuBs_x75im82LgtZChtLtW6n35hQ1lvtjPGknp3uUH-5c5het1aivUykOy8gn8Q62PUP7RZIEHuBnpjq8acSsYnQQXj5EHqHjAmlaSMoP7X730r6fqG0JL_A7tIfhg>
to help the world work better together by building a set of digital team
coaching tools. Parker has a profound passion for aiding the development of
positive, collaborative work cultures that improve individual and company
performance. This stems from his diverse employment background, which we’ll
let Parker explain later in the interview. Fortunately for him, he worked
for companies that invested a lot in people, but unfortunately, we’re
living through a period of change where managers, leaders, and employees
everywhere need more investment and support. Lucky for us, Valence is
providing just that!
------------------------------

   -

   *Tell us about Valence.*

Our mission is to help the world work better, together. We believe that one
of the most important skills that people can build is the muscle of working
better with a wider range of people, so we create tools that allow team
leadership to improve and for people to work better with colleagues and
teammates. There’s a vast world where people aren't getting the learning
and development opportunities that they should. We thought we could take
some of these principles of teamwork and facilitation, leadership, learning
and growth, and put them into products and be able to scale them and offer
them to millions of people.

   -

   *How does Valence inspire “customer love”?*

People are busy - so busy. We want to make sure we offer the best bang for
their buck, and combine simplicity with insights. So the design
instructions that I give everyone on the product team is that I want to
return value to every user within 60 seconds of using it. We make it
consumer grade and easy to use, knowing that people are super busy. And
second, our insights are the result of thousands of hours of coaches using
the tools and feeding back to us the insights that work - so we have
distilled that and are able to offer hyper-relevant, personalized advice
that people can immediately begin to act on.

   -

   *Do you have a recent milestone you would like to share?*

Our most recent client is a major food and beverage company with a brand
that’s recognized worldwide. We will be working with them globally across
their markets to help them transition from a more hierarchical organization
to a more networked and fluid organization. They know they need to invest
in teams because all types of different people will be working together and
they were looking for a platform that could support that. We’re excited to
help companies such as this scale practices across tens of thousands of
people in many geographies around the world.

   -

   *What makes you a unique founder?*

I started out as a consultant at McKinsey & Co. I then spent 10 years
co-founding and growing a nonprofit organization called Engineers Without
Borders, where we mobilized engineers to tackle the root causes of poverty
and inequality through innovative projects in various African countries. I
also co-founded Significance Labs, a nonprofit that connects tech
entrepreneurs with low-income Americans in order to build great products to
meet their needs. I went from that to working as sort of the right-hand-man
to the co-CEO of the world's largest hedge fund. The thread that really
connects all my experiences and has led to where we are now is that I’ve
learned to care deeply, deeply about culture and it’s something I’ve been
focusing on for a decade and a half. I saw the potential of digital tools
to build culture by helping people build better relationships, and have
better lives at work and make more productive companies.

   -

   *Do you have any advice for other founders?*

Most founders tend to solve the problems they know, which is based on their
own experiences. I think that’s a huge reason for the need for diversity of
founders and supporting people with other backgrounds. I also think it’s
important for founders or prospective founders to expose themselves to
things that aren’t part of the normal path, to atypical environments,
especially if you're a natural problem solver. It’s going to be a fun
experience for you and promises to be helpful for the world.
------------------------------

Hi Milo,
Please meet Pawel, who's been a mentor and collaborator of mine for 5+ years
now. He has a very interesting background in entrepreneurship, technology, and
investment, has been in a few companies that work with senior and technical
engineering talent/teams (including his current one which I've invested in and
will likely be working on full time soon), and is extremely well connected in
tech and startups.
Pawel, please meet Milo, who is one of my close friends from uni. He's been
working in engineering roles at Amazon since we graduated, and is wanting to
start looking at what's next for him. I thought you might be a good person for
him to talk to as he starts to talk to people and see what's out there, and
might have a few interesting connections you could make.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Matthew

Ableton together and lived together for 2 years.

marketing/growth/commercial ops strategy/roles whenever it’s in your focus.

that I didn’t have enough remote experience after 5 rounds….?), or into
SPC, but likely will be launching a small NFT fund as well as PMing the
launch of a TechTree token, and some advisory/consulting gigs to make my
exit from NTT

Hope all is well with you and the fam, and will be excited to hear progress
on Epoch :) I’ll be up in SF early in the new year it looks as well
(potentially with Paweł) so will let you know timing when it firms up.

look to in early 2022.

decision points for me in us deciding  to move forward, I really must insist
that I get 2021 calendar year summaries for Vang Advisory and Capital in early
2022. This is now my 3rd reminder I have sent this year (and have not received
any acknowledgement it will be done or is even in progress) and given the
failure to meet these expectations the past 2 years and the significant stress,
cost, and scrutiny that has caused, I must put extreme stress around this.
I am also still waiting on a confirmation of basic finances relating to 1) how
much profit did Vang Advisory make in calendar year 2020, 2) how much of that
was paid out to me through what methods, and 3) how much I paidin to Vang
Advisory personally. This is my fourth email chasing this, and is essential for
my US filings.
Best,
Matthew

subject: Re: Oasis Accelerator work payout schedule
msg_id: 17d5d042325f47c7
---

Hey Nassim,
Good to hear, and all sounds fine re: timing. I'm fine to do in  writing.
Can you confirm that we are modifying point 2.1 of the agreement linked here to
reflect that for the Oasis MetaMind accelerator, we will use the rate schedule
linked in this document with a portion of payment based on levels of startup
success in the program, with an estimate that I'll spend between 15-20 days (but
this is flexible, and I will provide monthly updates as well as notify if it
looks like we will potentially go over this estimate so we can plan/adjust
accordingly)?

On the potential Program Director front should we need to bring in someone else,
these are the two that I mentioned (in addition to James and the other candidate
Alex had in mind) that are probably worth having an initial discussion with in
the coming weeks: *  https://www.linkedin.com/in/colinallison1
   
 * https://www.linkedin.com/in/pelingonulsahin/
   


Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,

---
date: 2021-11-25
to: dan.wells@growcfo.net, kevin.appleby@growcfo.net, Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: GrowCFO Analytics/SEO Project Update - 25 Nov 2021
msg_id: 17d57f84c36ca9a3
---

Hi Alicia, Dan, and Kevin,
I just wanted to give you an update as we look toward the last stretch of our
project over the next week or two.
We've pretty much wrapped all of our SEO work, apart from me verifying the
changes we make are read properly by Google. In the past week or two,I've
specced our title and description tags for most of the core pages on the site.
I've alsocreated a deck that should serve as a useful guide for ongoing SEO
maintenance and execution (about 5 minutes of work for each new page created),
as well as summarizes the work we've done and links to various documents.
I'm in the process of pulling back data to fill in the KPI benchmarking
spreadsheet we'll be using, and then will work on building more of a full
fledged dashboard, most likely in Google Analytics. I'll also likely create a
document to sspec the various conversion points on the site that we'll want to
track, as it looks like there are a few more than I anticipated, and some will
more than likely require a couple of hours of light development work to get set
up. Alicia and I will have a call to align on this and ensure everything is
accounted for.
It would be good to have a call all together for us to recap the SEO work we've
done so far and finalize the handoff there. Let me know if there might be a time
towards the end of day UK time middle or end of next week that would work well
for you.
Of course, feel free to let me know if you have any thoughts or questions. Also,
it still looks like we'll likely come in on the lower end of our budgeted costs.
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

We've pretty much wrapped all of our SEO work, apart from me verifying the
changes we make are read properly by Google. In the past week or two, I've
specced our title and description tags for most of the core pages on the site.
I've also created a deck that should serve as a useful guide for ongoing SEO

I'm in the process of pulling back data to fill in the KPI benchmarking
spreadsheet we'll be using, and then will work on building more of a full
fledged dashboard, most likely in Google Analytics. I'll also likely create a

and will be in Miami this weekend.

Matthew

---
date: 2021-11-22
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Nova Proposal Review
msg_id: 17d4948695834d01
---

I think we should push them on the idea that email could be a bit of a
competitive advantage, and that they should think about it not as data
collection but as enabling them to deliver value to their community (i.e. what
email is supposed to be in the first place, but the engineering oriented-nature
of this audience makes them very skeptical of marketing)

---
date: 2021-11-22
to: Darcie Tanner <darcie@makk.co>
subject: Re: Nova Proposal Review
msg_id: 17d494457eb3d141
---

Hi Darcie,

This generally looks good to me.

I’d say one thing to maybe note (and maybe that’s the TBD) is that email
marketing is generally pretty neglected in this space, and there’s often
significant aversion to collecting emails (but hopefully will change), and
that using the word dapp instead of app may endear them to you slightly.
Also that Discord, Telegram, and Twitter will likely be their main branded
channels.

Sorry if I didn’t get this back to you in time for your turnaround -
currently minus 6 hours from the UK.

Looking forward to talking tomorrow!

Matthew

first invoice for processing shortly.
Best,
Matthew

Sorry for the email out of the blue, but I’m doing some advisory work with

looking to bring in some more crypto native marketing execution
capabilities, and I thought there might be a fit for some the work you do.

growth agency that is working with one of my portfolio companies that are
also building on Algorand and a few other chains. They get the space and
also have a fair amount of technical marketing and analytics expertise.

Happy to jump on your first call, or for you two to take it from here!

Best,

Matthew

Hi Carl,
Apologies for my delayed follow up - I've been taking a bit of time to work less
and enjoy Costa Rica a bit. I did just  order  Seveneves today, and it's been
interesting to see the wave of my friends starting to read Snowcrash with all
the recent hype around metaverse.
And I very much enjoyed our conversation the other day - it's been nice to be
nudged to think a little bit more long term, macro, and personal level as I look
for what's next for me and decide where to focus my time. I'd love to grab a
bite or drink sometime if you're ever around west LA when I'm back in town in
December as well.
And I'd love to connect with the crypto fund manager you mentioned (Jeremy or
something with a J I think?), and anyone else you think might be interesting for
me to connect with.
Hope all is well, and thanks very much for your time :)
Best,
Matthew

   NFT sales*
   - Games include a *diversified approach of new and established studios*

Fig is the #1 game funding and publishing platform. Fig games have nearly
100 nominations from major award shows. The platform helps both well-known
and up-and-coming indie developers bring their games to life.

With Portfolio Shares Fig is offering a diversified approach where they
will be publishing a range of different game and game console projects
(Portfolio Products), from AAA IP's, to established indie projects.
The games

Recognize some of the iconic partners Fig will be working with?

How it works

Through the sale of Fig Portfolio Shares, Fig intends to co-publish *10
games or game console projects* (Portfolio Products). When those Portfolio
Products are released and become publicly available for purchase, Fig will
collect a percentage of the gross receipts from their sales. From these
collections, *Fig will pay out dividends to investors twice a year.*
<https://republic.intercom-clicks.com/via/e?ob=PSsute0F7LmzaP9GkI5CmG3XAATmyOM3T6jkyA9ubiKDxEQsliDgkI0P5GFz7gUuzruJ%2FBQMsASaCahFBlMaVKiKYGoXC1rjbIwt7VebI70alkwFif5dQg9S0UPsyONQlZR8jPP1dCB25SOn%2Fu1rLAmrOM3XS6xo7hW5OCeqemLsRApWXes96vpej%2FR0UfzMi8cubwjTeUfGO8VGprrOlip30g%2BB9OjVFdsaIdsU9lhUXSFSiCQL%2F4rHY79fgyoI&h=c8be90a8bda39dab02442d1069d03cdb02d5d2d1-ix9imqb3_127400572304126&l=5d2bfc3a895e5bcd1bc5e612e75941ebb0c4b425-12665775>

Fig has the ability to receive NFTs from participating games, should any be
produced, and has the ability to potentially sell them and distribute
proceeds to Fig Portfolio shareholders. This could provide a revenue stream
for investors *even prior to a game shipping*.

*To learn more about how returns are calculated, check out the breakdown
and potential distribution timeline on the deal page. 👇*

---
date: 2021-11-18
to: Mark Masterson <m2masterson@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Before the Q becomes moot...
msg_id: 17d33b7329b715c7
---

At the very least, I’m flattered :)

I think your hunch re tax and defi I think is pretty accurate - I know a
handful of very straight laced tech founders, ex-execs, etc who have
basically resolved to not file crypto taxes for large portions of their
holdings (namely the onchain holdings as opposed to centralised exchanges)
until the US has clearer regulation (and because currently that regulation
basically excludes US citizens from participating in DeFi), or are just
avoiding it all together despite wanting to invest there. It’s a very real
problem for me as I look to move my investment fund to the US as to whether
I liquidate most of my positions to be able to simplify my tax filings.

There’s an interesting crypto-specific tax management tool called Koinly
that also might be interesting to look at for inspiration.

I was actually a big Napster/Limewire user, and switched pretty quickly to
iTunes when the launched the Music Store (as well as the CD ripping
functionality), so definitely resonates with me :)

Definitely happy to chat more. Full transparency from my side, I’m actually
waiting to hear back in the next 2-3 days on a job I will probably accept
if I get, but I also expect it’s a super competitive candidate pool. If
that doesn’t pan out, I’m weighing a few other projects/opportunities, so
would certainly be interested in learning more about what you see in this
role (and happy to make intros if anyone else comes to mind who might be a
good fit).

Does it make sense to hop on a call sometime next week?

Hope all is well, and thanks for thinking of me!

---
date: 2021-11-18
to: sjboyle10@gmail.com, Cristina Vila Vives <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Steven/Cristina Intro
msg_id: 17d338aa12e37eca
---

Hi Cristina,
As mentioned, please meet one of my very close friends from growing up, Steven.
He's been working in sales at Zoom for the past 3+ years, and living in Denver
the past few years as well. He's starting to explore what might be next for him,
and I thought Cledara might be interesting for him to learn a bit more about.
Steven, as mentioned, please meet Cristina, the CEO of Cledara, which is one of
the fastest growing companies that I've personally invested in. They're looking
to ramp up their growth in the US (andbasing  their office in Denver), and
Cristina will be in Denver towards the end of the month, so thought you might be
interested to connect and learn more.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-11-15
to: Alicia Burke <aliciaevelynburke@gmail.com>
subject: Re: SEO Qs 4 Good Soup Social
msg_id: 17d224152964b0ba
---

So this generally makes sense - probably easiest for us to chat through as I
think you're pretty close to the verge of a lot of this stuff really clicking
for you.
If possible, I'd make sure there's also a page at the folder layer for these
pages -  https://goodsoupsocial.com/inspiration/live-on-twitter-spaces/ (i.e.
try and make hub pages for each folder/section at
https://goodsoupsocial.com/inspiration - this could be the first page you linked
maybe, although not sure if that's a different use case (and if so, I'd ask if
it maybe makes sense to be grouped under a different folder name).
Re: image labeling and meta tags - I'd say meta tags is more  important (esp
titles, and descriptions IF there's no text on the pages which I imagine those
will  be pretty light - you could use a copy/paste for the descriptions though).
Images are less important, but also maybe an interesting tactic for you given
the visual nature of what you're doing and that designers and marketers probably
use image search more than almost anyone else
Re: other links, I need to understand a bit more - generally not bad to have
external links (and if you're linking to smaller companies, they may even share
it/notice that you've linked to them if you use UTM tags). For internal links,
generally more is better

subject: Re: Matthew and Zapper

Hi Maxim,
Thanks for the update. The interviews have been a pleasure, and I've been very
impressed by everyone on the team.
I've compiled notes from my conversations into a rough outline of the steps that
I think will be important for a successful app go-to-market campaign (some that
I imagine would be initiatives this role would lead, but others which would
certainly be a collaborative effort or led by others). I've also included a few
remaining questions I have (some of which might be more of wider strategic
conversations or still evolving), other ideas, and a summary of some of my
mobile-specific experience. Do feel free to share it if you'd like -
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1DisI-6YgXVWH8Z1ZDNPZkQ-Lo1R5NRJzWhJGleEuZw8/edit?usp=sharing
I hope to hear from you next week. Do let me know if there's anything else I can
provide in the meantime, or if scheduling an additional conversation would be
useful.
Best,
Matthew

Know anyone who might be/are you potentially interested in this?

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Penny Penati <p.penati@thesupperclub.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2021 at 08:05
Subject: PPC/Paid Social help
To: matteisn@gmail.com <matteisn@gmail.com>
Cc: Oliver Lingwood-Craddock <ocraddock@thesupperclub.com>


Hey Matthew,



So lovely to speak to you this morning. It’s been so long.



Regarding a blurb and what we are after so that you can pass it onto your
contacts, please see the below:

*Areas we are interested in testing:*

   - Paid Social – specifically LinkedIn
   - Google Ads



*Main points*

   - The business has historically tried paid but not had major success
   with it
   - We are looking to revisit this at this point to test/learn/expand on
   areas that impact positively as we are investing to accelerate our new
   member acquisition
   - Areas that the individual can help us with:
      - Review current set-up and ensure the basics are in place
      - Work with me to set-up basic campaigns to test effectiveness with
      our audience of a set of creative/copy ads
      - Help educate me/the team on how to execute on campaigns and support
      in the next phase (of increasing investment in what is working)



Let me know if you need anything else from me,

Penny

Head of Operations

---
date: 2021-11-12
to: Jeremie Daigle <jeremie@zapper.fi>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17d160b97cc2c100
---

Hi Jeremie,
First, I wanted to thank you for your time earlier this week. I very much
enjoyed our conversation, and continue to be impressed by the team at Zapper.
I was thinking a little bit on the "bringing someone into crypto" question you
asked in our call over the past few days, and had a few thoughts that might be
worth mentioning. * I was talking to a friend who I got into crypto via NFTs
   maybe 3-4 months ago (and as of late have been nudging him to DeFi). I told
   him about your question and he told me he's given 3 ENS domains to some of
   our other friends as gifts (all post-airdrop unfortunately) as a way to get
   them into the space, which I thought was interesting. I've actually been
   gifting NFTs to people recently, but I think the idea of gifting a domain
   name (ENS, Unstoppable Domains, or otherwise) could be a powerful way to
   nudge people towards understanding that there is deeper functionality to
   crypto than just trading (or giving them a simple GIF/JPG).

 * For some of our client advisory work at NTT DATA, I put together a short
   presentation based on these notes that I talked through with some executives
   at one of our enterprise telco clients about how they could get involved
   in/gain exposure to crypto and blockchain (and even look towards building or
   contributing to decentralized products). Obviously this is a very different
   perspective than when considering an individual, but I thought it might be
   interesting to see how I would think about onboarding an organization into
   the blockchain ecosystem with more of a linear process (as I believe there is
   less variability in terms of the ways an enterprise might "jump into crypto"
   as compared to an individual, who might take one of many different paths). I
   also put this together about 8 months ago, before I really appreciated the
   potential value of NFTs, so that coupled with the general changes in the
   market over that time might mean it's a bit incomplete/in need of a refresh.


Please do let me know any next steps, or if there's any additional information I
can provide, and thanks again for your time.

Hope you have a good weekend!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-11-12
to: Andrea Bonaceto <andrea@eternacapital.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17d126444809c69e
---

Hey Andrea,
Hope all is well, and apologies for how long it's taken to follow up since we
grabbed coffee.
First, thanks for the tip on Al Goannas :) I wanted to ask, are you guys staking
all of yours for "the Egg"? I have one staked right now, debating staking the
second. And let me know if there's anything else in the space I should be
keeping an eye on!
Following up on a few threads I mentioned when we connected and some others that
might be of interest:
 * Copernic Space  - ex CMO from Coinfirm (who’s also a musician) founded to try
   and allow more accessible investment in space economy, some early use cases
   around NFTs to support satellite imagine for conservation in Africa, and
   they're about to do a sale of payload space on a lunar rover as tradeable
   NFTs in the coming weeks I thought you might be interested or know someone
   who might be (maybe put a trezor with one of your pieces or a physical piece
   on the moon or maybe interesting for one of your investments)

 * Kurt  - runs NFT genius, dev studio that serial launches NFT projects, just
   released Ballerz on Flow, connected via eff Kelley (below)

 * Jeff Kelley  - runs Edge of NFT podcast (Matty Mo, Wall Street Bets guy, Axie
   founders, etc) and MainChain ventures

 * Flaviu Dunca  - I hired him as my junior/eventually to replace me at Touch
   Surgery, spent 3 years there and the last 3 years at TikTok, jumping off to
   start an artist collab high street fashion line with community/perks/proof of
   ownership NFTs, I set him up with Johanna who is now doing the grant
   approvals for Algorand Foundation it sounds

 * Brydg/Alex Kay - founded by an ex-social and gaming VC (invested in esports
   in early 2000s, first startups to do API integrations with Facebook, etc),
   built brydg.com which has serviced over $1bn in real estate loans, thinking
   about a real-estate lending backed NFT/crowdfunding platform (they already
   have the tech for the lending marketplace, crowdfunding, and a bunch of other
   peripheral real estate transaction related services). One of 5 lenders along
   with OakNorth who distributed the government BBB CBILS Covid relief loans.
   Was knocking around an idea to launch a token to buy an EPL club for a while,
   and generally have been exploring blockchain use cases for 5+ years and
   finally gearing up to actually build something, potentially on Algorand (or
   maybe even Oasis)

 * SocialStack / Kristina Tauchmanova  - working on a company called
   socialstack.co - social coin infrastructure for creators and communities to
   incentivise members with tokens and NFTs. I believe I had Andrew reach out to
   Mattia around their most recent round (if he actually did reach out), but
   might be interesting for you as a creator/artist


Let me know if any of those sound like they might be of interest, and if there's
anything else I can help with!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-11-11
to: Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: Re: Content Taxonomy Q
msg_id: 17d0dc7e6c0fd8fb
---

Hi - yup, you are correct that a parent/child/page structure is more ideal
for these pages as you’ve indicated. I think trimming filler words can save
as many characters as the new subdirectory layer adds, and even if not,
generally it’s good practice to have once you’re hitting 5-10 pages in a
“folder”

pretty much the same time :)

Pacific time), if a time then might work for you. Or if another day would
be more ideal, just let me know and I can send you my availability for that
day.

Looking forward to it!

Best,

Matthew

Matthew

---
date: 2021-11-04
to: Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: Re: Feedback on docs
msg_id: 17cebcbb9dde224a
---

Sounds good - if you have a commentable version, I can make some
suggestions that will be a bit easier to copy paste - although some points
I wasn’t exactly sure how to position the messaging

---
date: 2021-11-04
to: Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: Feedback on docs
msg_id: 17cea19c96839330
---

Sorry for the delay on this. If you want to share these with comment or edit
access via Google Drive (or in the future), might be easier for you for me to
make comments in line (or in some cases just make changes)

Tokenized Space Cargo PDF: * Tagline - it's okay but not in love with it - I
   could see maybe tweaking part of it to "own your piece of space"
 * In second line, remove "easily" - devalues
   opportunity
 * In line 4, "ownership of their space" is a little
   vague (although maybe intentionally so)
 * Bold SpaceX Falcon 9?
 * "and own it's future" in second  to last line of first  paragraph is a little
   awkward - maybe something about the permanence?
 * "Whether gaming, luxury, or technology) in the last
   paragraph might pigeonhole us (I could see art being interested for instance)
 * Generally some slightly wonky layout/element
   alignment (price box, alignment of left and right column section tops and
   bottoms, spacing at bottom of right column is very tight, etc) - happy to
   take a crack at polishing if there's an editable version of this
 * Might be worth having a version of this that is
   much lighter on mentioning NFTs for less crypto adoptive orgs (or maybe they
   just aren't target for us right now)


CopernnicSpacedeck - November * Slide 3 - I would make the points underneath
   BARRIERS bulleted lines if possible, the all caps and comma separation is a
   bit tricky to read. Not sure what CAGR is
 * Slide 4 - "with no scalable commercial interface
   with potential buyers" is a bit clunky
 * Slide 5 - I would maybe make the bullets like
 * Investors: statement of problemStartups: statement of problemLate Stage:
   statement of problem
 * Slide 6 - make text thicker, thin white copy on
   black background is difficult to read (esp after white background on previous
   slides), "economic operating system" is a bit vague - I think working
   marketplace in here might be good
 * Slide 7 - try and combine with slide 8 so one
   slide on SpaceMart, copy on supply and demand side tabs of diagram on slide 8
   are inconsistent sentence structure, will be easier to read if we can
   standardize
 * Slide 9 - try and combine with slide 10 so one
   slide on SpacePool
 * I think business model slide (and maybe 1-2
   others) could go in appendix


One Pager * Add checks in boxes in left  column for milestones that are done, try and
   trim milestones to 2 lines each
 * Line 4-5 - change to "investment in space"
 * 3rd line, second paragraph, remove easily?
 * In Team section, capitalize Fortune in Fortune 500


I still have to review the space platform doc

subject: Re: Convertible Debt agreement
msg_id: 17cca204dc372108
---

Hey David,
Thanks very much for this.
Those changes you suggested to ArtSquare sound great. I'm relatively okay about
the points outside of 2 (I lifted the bones of this from a contract Pawel gave
to me actually), but if you could draft up the points in two, that would be
great, although if you do happen to notice glaring issues outside of it, maybe I
should rethink this. And if we need to hop on a quick call to go through any
decision points, more than happy to, just let me know when.
For AngelsPartners, it sounds like: * the  option grant dates are odd and leave me exposed to be terminated without
   payment between longer periods
 * in terms of valuations, my understanding
   is that the company value doesn't affect my personal tax liabilities until I
   actually exercise an option, so that declaring the value wouldn't ultimately
   be an issue until any gain is realized? I might be misunderstanding the point
   here, or sounds like its  more of a tax reporting issues  (certainly not
   helped by my US citizenship).

I should be able to just transition to a different document for AngelsPartners,
so I'll raise that next time I talk to him (and maybe it's actually easier to
use them as the case for you to draft a version up for since there are tangible
terms already on paper there?).
Let me know next steps, and thanks for your help
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-10-29
to: Lindsay Presswell <lindsay@zapper.fi>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17cc9e1250aac745
---

Hi Lindsay,
Thanks for your time earlier. It was great to chat and hear a bit more about
your background and the content/marketing at Zapper. It sounds like there's a
very talented team all around with some interesting (but certainly achievable)
go-to-market and growth challenges on the horizon that would be a lot of fun to
focus on.

I did have a couple of questions - nothing particularly urgent though. I imagine
some might lead to wider strategy discussions as opposed to having a
particularly clean answer at the moment. * What are the current
   marketing/content/community teams structures (sounds like you, 2 community
   managers + another one soon, any others?), vision for future growth?
 * What are major upcoming (or
   recurring) activities, initiatives, marketing campaigns, etc
 * What campaigns, channels, and content
   have been working particularly well up until now?
 * Is the long term strategy to be
   mobile-first or cross-platform with relatively similar features on mobile and
   desktop? Or TBD?
 * What is the current marketing stack?
   What sort of activities are currently track/measure and report/dashboard (and
   can those be attributed to marketing channels via Google Analytics or
   similar)? What sort of marketing/comms is currently automated, if any?
 * We had sort of touched on it (and
   sounds like it's a bit of a WIP), but I'd be very interested in diving a bit
   deeper in terms of trying to pull out some user segments and starting to
   evaluate which may be the most valuable segments we could approach and what
   the USPs and messaging might be for each.



I also had a few thoughts on your question around planning and executing
go-to-market campaigns for mobile apps. I've tried to keep it relatively high
level, but I always find I'm better at sorting through my thoughts on paper when
mapping detailed/multi-stepped processes. Please find linked
here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1O7osL0RED0J9C_GeSXrrG-lY-aOi_5o-7DlU7PgjW_U/edit?usp=sharing

I'd love to continue the conversation, so let me know any next steps from your
side, or if there's anything additionally I can provide.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-10-28
to: Idan Beck <idanbeck@gmail.com>
subject: Following up from last week
msg_id: 17cc54ff8bb2e941
---

Hey Idan,
First of all, thanks again (and to Stacey as well) for letting me stay with you.
It was great to have a bit more time than usual to catch up, and I really
appreciate you being a sounding board for some of the stuff I'm working through
in my career. I think at this point I have some solid options on the table
(between having the 3rd of 4 rounds of interviews with Zapper tomorrow, or
potentially launching Pawel's (and your's? :D ) token.
A few potential connections that might be interesting: * James Palmer: runs a site called Gamerdating that hss  probably a half
   million gamers and often runs promotions/affiliate deals, also has an
   influencer marketing agency that he built off basically scraping all the data
   he could for the past 4 or 5 years, particularly strong in gaming, crypto,
   and software/engineering (could be useful on B2B outreach, launching a token,
   or recruiting devs). Has a bit of an ego, but his database is very valuable
   and he knows it
 * Algorand or Oasis/Rose
   Foundations: throwing around 6-7 figure equity-free grants to build
   blockchain use cases like they're candy right now (as are many others, but I
   know these ones well and can fast track you)
 * Sheng Huang: grew up in the bay, worked for Nianntic pre Pokemon Go launch,
   now doing more content creation and advisory, but very smart and well
   networked
 * Yao Tang/Jack Wang: husband and wife, Yao is ex PMM for Improbable (and
   previously engineering in finance sector), and has worked in gaming
   investment for Tencent and now ByteDance, Jack is very gaming oriented (and I
   think about to jump to a fund that's focused ongaming)
 * Sameer Singh: built up the research/consulting function at App Annie, angel
   investor in startups/marketplaces with significant network effect dynamics
 * There are a few others
   I'll reach out to, i also left most of the more pure investors that might be
   relevant as I imagine that's not really the focus now :) and I also left off
   the more crypto specific ones for now as well (although a few there are quite
   focused on gaming, but pretty much exclusively web3 gaming). And I have a few
   dev friends I'll tease a bit and see if any are interested in jumping out of
   various boring roles at generic YC backed startups/Amazon/etc


I'd also love to connect with your cofounder, as well as discuss the idea of a
token launch 9or even standing up marketing operations),whenever makes  sense.
And I'd also love to pick up any conversation around investment/advisory
whenever makes sense to you (I imagine the round is your focus for the time
being, but whenever makes sense chat around that, very eager to :) )
You had also mentioned a few intros that might be interesting for me (I think
one was an investor you had talked to a few days prior, maybe a few others). No
need to ever opt me in on those, always happy to take an intro from you.
Let me know if I'm forgetting anything, or if there's anything else I can help
with. And thanks again!
Matthew

/parent/child/post is definitely ideal if there will be at least 5-10 items in
each folder (shows more structure to Google) - pretty important but
not absolutely urgently essential
This is managed in the Settings > Permalinks section of Wordpress (at least for
blog posts), although not sure how that works with custom secondary
categories/tags, and likely will be a bit of a headache if there are existing
pages we need to redirect (although redirects only really matter if there are
quality external or important internal links to that page)

date: 2021-10-27
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Info for 2020 US Filing
msg_id: 17cbfe0018f85a9d
---

Hi Brian,
Following up on your questions * 1)      During calendar year 2020, what was the
   maximum value in the following accounts:
 * a. Bunq B.V.: $200b. Revolut Limited account 5343GBP: $5000c. Lloyds Bank
   account 9768: $1500d. Lloyds Bank account 2860: $1500e. Lloyds Bank account
   7960: $1500f. Revolut Limited account 5343MXN: $20g. Revolut Limited account
   5343USD: $200h. Revolut Limited account 5343EUR: $500
 * 2) Please provide your dates of entry to and
   exit from the United States during 2020: None, I was not in the US for any
   days in 2020
 * 3) Were you required to leave the United
   Kingdom (or another foreign country) between February 1 and July 15, 2020 due
   to Covid-19?: No, I was in the UK for the entirety of 2020
 * 4) Please provide a copy of your HM Revenue &
   Customs tax return for the tax year ending April 2021, when available. This
   is due in January 2021 - I have pushed my UK accountants around this, and
   will do so again, but think it will be ambitious to expect it back before the
   filing deadline here given their response times.
 * 5) The payslip from NTT Data shows £ 104,106.60
   taxable gross pay and £ 32,260.80 tax for the year ending April 5, 2021. 
    How much of this was in calendar year 2020, v 2021? - $83,200 of the taxable
   gross pay and $25,800 of tax was for calendar year 2020 from NTT
 * 6) The personal P&L spreadsheet shows £ 29,950
   total payments from Ansible/Vang, but the change in retained earnings from
   the Vang financial statements calculate to £ 17,171.    Did you make a
   capital contribution or another transaction in retained earnings? - I put
   personal money into the company via a directors loan to fund tax payments
   that were coming due, but I will have to check with my UK accountants if that
   matches the difference you're seeing, as this sounds very high
 * 7) Did your stock ownership in Vang Advisory
   Limited (formerly Ansible) remain 100%? Yes
 * 8) Did your stock ownership in Vang Capital
   Limited (formerly Eisner) remain 50%?Yes, as did my Dad's
 * 9) Vang Capital Limited shows a £ 472 balance
   in an account called "Directors' current accounts," which was not on the
   prior year balance sheet. Who is this money owed to?I'm not sure, maybe this
   has to do with a directors loan? I'll have to verify with my UK accountants.


Sorry for the delay on this, and let me know if there's anything else. I'll loop
back shortly (hopefully) with word from my UK accountants and some other threads
I've been meaning follow  up on (including that l likely start taking on my
first revenues and expenses with my US consulting company).
Hope all is well,
Matthew

<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=pIaqY5CeCfODZtYbqd3CFvHiPmFjrAIhUpRCcoczphc.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zaG91dG91dC53aXguY29tL3NvL2M0Tm91ZmFxVT9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6ImIxNmMwMmJhLWQ0MzktNDMyNi1iMzE3LWM4NWQxNGM0ZDg4MCJ9>*



Last Call:  Series Starts Tomorrow



CONNECTION & BELONGING



On Tuesday:

GROUND OPERATIONS:  Battlefields to Farmfields

with Featured Guest,  Dulanie Ellis



Dates:  Oct 26, Nov 2, 9 & 16

First of all, thank-you SO much to my Featured Guest, filmmaker Dulanie
Ellis
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=X0gYvq_MccEioCpa9cYTDWowehXQEq0fUmHMegXyDL0.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3RvbmVzb3VwcmlwcGxlLmNvbS9nZXQtdG8ta25vdy1vdXItZmVhdHVyZWQtZ3Vlc3RzIiwiciI6ImJkMjE5ZGZkLWI0MzktNDVhMS03NTg2LTlmZjlhNjU0NzE0MyIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6ImIxNmMwMmJhLWQ0MzktNDMyNi1iMzE3LWM4NWQxNGM0ZDg4MCJ9>,
who will be with us tomorrow to share her award-winning film with us.
Thank-you too, to those of you who have already reserved your place.  It's
going to be yet another special gathering.



Ground Operations:  Battlefields to Farmfields
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=8L6dZOGnPfVZhvI1cejsDEHSGtiXEaYZ4wevntVKapE.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaW1kYi5jb20vdmlkZW8vdmkzMjIxMDA2MTA1IiwiciI6ImJkMjE5ZGZkLWI0MzktNDVhMS03NTg2LTlmZjlhNjU0NzE0MyIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6ImIxNmMwMmJhLWQ0MzktNDMyNi1iMzE3LWM4NWQxNGM0ZDg4MCJ9>
tells
the stories of 8 combat veterans, after serving in Iraq and Afghanistan,
have found Connection & Belonging running organic farms and ranches.



1. Still on your "to do" list?

Click here to Reserve Your Place
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=y8g-b3SdRI9NHEy5AFftvwda6U8G9nyjH3RiQot1Lrs.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3RvbmVzb3VwcmlwcGxlLmNvbS9ib29raW5ncy1jaGVja291dC9jb25uZWN0aW9uLWJlbG9uZ2luZy0yMDIxL2Jvb2siLCJyIjoiYmQyMTlkZmQtYjQzOS00NWExLTc1ODYtOWZmOWE2NTQ3MTQzIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiYjE2YzAyYmEtZDQzOS00MzI2LWIzMTctYzg1ZDE0YzRkODgwIn0>
.



2.  Already signed up?

Expect an email reminder in a few hours with the Zoom link and the link to
the film we'll be watching together.



As always, the Meet and Greet starts 15 minutes early, at 4:45pm PT /
7:45pm  ET, and we'll get started at the top of the hour.



For the rest of the series, see below and feel free to share,



Christine
*DETAILS, TRAILERS & RSVP*
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=u1TDJ1OEQVHQ4IViYBZI9rjZ0pMBbhvmjzahaxmOFJk.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3RvbmVzb3VwcmlwcGxlLmNvbS8yLWdhdGhlcmluZ3MiLCJyIjoiZmRmY2QzNWMtNmFmOS00NzRiLWNhNGMtYjg1MDQzODlmNjA5IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiYjE2YzAyYmEtZDQzOS00MzI2LWIzMTctYzg1ZDE0YzRkODgwIn0>

To reserve additional salons
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=ENiHzMqibibI_yJA_jtMa6jI7PnMqGzUNdFxSJ3KuUk.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3RvbmVzb3VwcmlwcGxlLmNvbS9ib29raW5ncy1jaGVja291dC9jb25uZWN0aW9uLWJlbG9uZ2luZy0yMDIxL2Jvb2siLCJyIjoiYzY0NDNiYWUtMGM3MS00NGQ2LWFjNDUtNzBjNTRiOWNkYWZlIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiYjE2YzAyYmEtZDQzOS00MzI2LWIzMTctYzg1ZDE0YzRkODgwIn0>
, scroll to the week and then click on the time below the date.  Repeat the
process, for each salon.



- On November 2nd, we'll experience My So-Called Enemy and meet filmmaker Lisa
Gossels and 2 other Featured Guests as well as 2 of the 6 Israeli and
Palestinian teenage girls connected at a leadership program in the U.S.
This coming of age story shows how, when we listen to each other's stories,
without making assumptions or "othering", we can build lasting bridges of
understanding.



- On November 9th, we'll meet actress Meredith Thomas, the on-screen
mom in Jeannette
Godoy's short film, Disconnected, who dares to confiscate the phones of her
daughter and 2 besties for one entire hour... while also dealing with
issues of her own.

*This salon ends 30 minutes early, at 7pm PT / 10pm ET.



- On November 16th, just before heading into Thanksgiving and the December
holidays, we'll gather for a deeper dive into the series theme as we
connect even more with each other.

*This salon ends 30 minutes early, at 7pm PT / 10pm ET.





   - In lieu of a booking fee for online salons, participants may Add a
   Ripple by contributing to support our Featured Guests.



Building connection, community and ripples that expand

ideas, projects, businesses and lives...



Contact us:  info@stonesoupripple.com
*Become a Member*
<https://shoutout.wix.com/so/c4NoufaqU/c?w=BQRncjIeoB_wRdrG_47XIgO-igqmAMFzinyw4E3rrLw.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3RvbmVzb3VwcmlwcGxlLmNvbS9tZW1iZXJzIiwiciI6ImQ1MTVjNTM3LWQyMDctNDljMi1jNWE2LWMxNjY4YmQ3MTY2NyIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6ImIxNmMwMmJhLWQ0MzktNDMyNi1iMzE3LWM4NWQxNGM0ZDg4MCJ9>

date: 2021-10-25

subject: Fwd: Tomorrow: Tips for Better Meditations (Free or On Donation)
msg_id: 17cb957e7b61d9e4
---

Thought you’d find interesting - breath and meditation workshops by an ex
googler, very good at marketing them via email/community


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Rio Otoya <rio@riootoya.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2021 at 13:54
Subject: Tomorrow: Tips for Better Meditations (Free or On Donation)
To:


Hello!


I hope you are had a fantastic weekend.


If you struggle with your Meditation practice or want to get some tips for
a better practice, the workshop tomorrow is for you : )


We will explore a few breathing and concentration tips, workshop any
questions you may have, and end with a guided practice.


Oct 26, 7:30 PM – 8:30 PM London / 1:30-2:45pm Lima

Tips for Better Meditations & Guided Practice.

Meditation can become way easier with a few simple tips and tricks. Info &
Registration here
<https://www.riootoya.com/event-details/mind-calming-hacks-and-guided-meditation-1>


Also, did you hear about the GPS Meditation? It stands for Gratitude,
Purpose & Service. Take a minute to learn about it here
<https://www.instagram.com/p/CVWbLSrA42K/>

Best!


Rio



*Other classes you might like: *

Nov 03, 7:30 PM – 8:30 PM / 1:30-2:45pm Lima

Om: Breath, Vibration, & Meditation
Discover the mystery and power of the Om mantra. Om is the most well-known
and yet most misunderstood Yogic mantra.This is part a theory class and
part a guided meditation practice. Info & Registration here
<https://www.riootoya.com/event-details/om-breath-vibration-and-meditation>

*Free Exploration Calls for Breathwork and Life Coaching. 60 mins*

Let's explore and unpack possibilities for growing or getting unstuck. The
call is 50% mini-coaching session and 50% answering any questions about my
work & approach. Book a call here
<https://calendly.com/riootoya/60min-session>

[image: Meditation at Dojo.jpg]
Photo: Morning Meditation at Dojo Coworking in Bali, circa 2019

-
*_________________________________________________________*

*Rio Otoya | riootoya.com <http://riootoya.com> | +51996538555*

---
date: 2021-10-25
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Taxonomy
msg_id: 17cb64496bbc90bf
---

Hey Yohann,
Following up on this, it looks like there's an issue (or are we just reviewing
taxonomies here?) with the top 2 sets of links potentially where I get "no
investors matching your selection". Also, I think the geo label you might have
intended was "Wyoming" (seriously strange spelling haha). But generally I like
this page layout and when populated with content, should more than exceed the
minimum word threshold we want to shoot for (bare minimum 300 words, ideally
more like 600). We should test that conversion form  pretty well though, as
there's a lot going on in it. We can discuss this in more detail, as I think
there's quite a bit we'll need to think about in balancing data quality and CRO
on startup signup. We might also want a small CTA of "join as an investor" 
somewhere, you "add your fund here" (def also needs some thinking through).
On the taxonomies for the first set, these look good to me. On the second set
(firms), I think we should do
https://angelspartners.com/find-startup-investors/yoming/cryptos/firms, with the
idea that we might do
https://angelspartners.com/find-startup-investors/yoming/cryptos/angels
eventually as well. And then individual element clicks would redirect to
individual firm or angel pages in the 3rd set of taxonomies.
I'm also getting 500 errors on the Reppert Capital and Shea Ventures links.
Although again if this is just about the taxonomies, these look good to me (the
only caveat is maybe we'd want to do ap.com/investors/firms/[INDIVIDUAL FIRM] so
that we could also have ap.com/investors/angels, as per the same logic as above,
to keep these folders a little more organized).
Re: metas - these look pretty solid to me. When they're live, I'll pull them
with ScreamingFrog and take a look to see how they are processed live and see if
we need to make any adjustments - I have a feeling we might end up with some
more characters we can play with (and generally it's better to use close to the
max, so we might add a CTA or "Startup investor Platform" or something as a
suffix at the end of the description). One thought is on the Meta Description,
you might want to capitalize the first letter of important words like
contact/connect (and I don't have a strong opinion on which to use currently -
long term, I'd love to A/B testing it, but A/B testing tags is probably a few
SEO iterations away from where we are, and we need more traffic for it to work).
On another front, I've blasted Wikipedia editing jobs and asked 4 or 5 friends
in marketing roles if they know of anyone. Nothing strong back so far, but will
keep looking. If not, Upwork may be our best bet.
And I have my call with Foti from Growthmentor  on Wednesday, so will set you up
with him after that. And I'll ping Alex Potter about getting the ball rolling
for using AP wth the Oasis Accelerator cohort, although they just started
recruitment so the program probably won't start until January or so. Also have a
few others to reach out to on that front.
Should we also put a call on the book for sometime maybe  early week after next?
Hope all is well, and let me know if there's anything else I can help with!
Matthew

Looking forward to catching up!

date: 2021-10-24
to: Dan Silvertown <dan@lapse.app>
subject: Re: Matthew <> Dan (Lapse)
msg_id: 17cafb1cad3bfbca
---

Hey Dan,
It was great to connect earlier this week and hear a bit about what you're
working on and trying to do with data.
It also sounds like you've made a few decisions (i.e. using Branch, actively
wanting to set up Segment)/have a mentality about this that will make the
process of setting up CDP and analytics/automation a bit simpler than many cases
I'm used to, so I think it would be fun to work on this with you :)
I've linked here a document that outlines how I would see the phases of this
project progressing, the time it would take to complete each from my side, and
some basic information about my rates and how I structure engagements. I can be
somewhat flexible on these, and in some cases am open to a portion of payment
in equity options or performance-based fees (although that's often tricky for
analytics projects as opposed to growth/marketing projects), although there
would need to be a deeper discussion around this.
Please note that currently, the document assumes development/engineering
capabilities will be provided by your side, although I can always bring in a dev
for the project if we need (although it's generally more ideal to have someone
internally for a few reasons).
Let me know if you have any initial thoughts or questions, and when might make
sense to hop on another call to review this, as well as spend an hour or so
talking in a bit more structure/detail about the data silos, tools,
analytics/dashboarding use cases, and other requirements for me to make sure I
have a complete understanding of scope and use case prioritization.
Looking forward to continuing our conversation.
Best,
Matthew

Matthew
