---
date: 2021-10-22
to: Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: Copernic Space marketing outline
msg_id: 17ca6bf6634dfd37
---

Hey Grant,
Sorry for the delay on this - it's been a hectic few days catching up with
friends, taking over some babysitting duty, and catching up on stuff. Next week
should be quite productive for me as I'll be in LA with very little to do, so
likely working a lot.

Month 1: Planning * Solidification of timelines/stages of sale
 * Development of core assets (basic website, sales sheet, sale
   information lightpaper)
 * Planning of tools, systems, initial campaigns, biz
   dev/partner targets, required resources
 * Warm biz dev intros

Month 2: Launch Prep * Pre-launch campaign - content backlogging, channel
   claiming, content calendar creation, initial tease campaign
 * Biz dev outreach, remaining warm intros
 * Initial messaging A/B testing
 * Look for initial cross promotion marketing partners,
   develop/solidify offering
 * Freelancer/agency/junior marketer recruitment
 * Begin email list building

Month 3: * Full pre-launch campaign execution
 * Scaling biz dev and partnership outreach
 * Website iteration to full scale site
 * Begin email newsletters
 * Draft automated transactional and notification message copy

Month 4: * Full launch campaign execution
 * Planning next phases/transition into ongoing marketing execution

Month 5: * Marketing analytics
 * Stabilizing ongoing marketing operations, templating marketing
   processes and assets, building execution team
   


Basic engagement: * I can pretty easily commit to at least 2 days per month, and
   potentially more (up to 5 or so on an all equity option basis) if we have a
   lot of work we want to get through that month. More than that and it will be
   tricky to balance my cash burn rate
 * For up to 3 days per month indefinitely I'm more than happy
   to have a convertible debt for equity options agreement to reserve cash
 * Beyond 3 days per month, it'd be preferable to at least
   partially be paid in cash, but I imagine this would be unlikely until some
   significant revenue or funding events milestones are reached


Let me know if that all makes sense to you, and when makes sense for another
call to start getting plugged into things.
Hope you're feeling better and having safe travels,
Matthew

date: 2021-10-20

subject: Re: 18yr Property Cycles - Review
msg_id: 17c9cd87a7b44397
---

I want to get you feedback on this quickly as I know I said I would, but I
haven’t had time to really dig into it. At first glance, it seems good.

I’d try and get Aysan to read it with you when you sit down with him for 30
minutes and get his sign off. As it’s more macro-markets, he might defer it
to Alex, at which point you’ll have to sit him down to read through it with
him.

In general for those meeting with Aysan, make sure to go in with a document
of bullets of the points you want to cover, any questions/sign offs you
need from him, any info you need from him, links to the files you want to
show him, and record next actions in a notes section at the bottom . Does
have to be exactly like this but you see what I’m getting at.

I’ll try and give this a more through read and actually edit it. I’d also
try as often as possible to point people to a share point document so
people can asynchronusly update without you having to manually merge
changes later, but sharepoint sucks and I know Alex and Aysan are much more
likely to probably give feedback in an email than a doc

Let me know if there’s anything else I can help with, and if you don’t make
headway with Aysan by our one to one call next week, ill go push in Alex.

date: 2021-10-19
to: dan.wells@growcfo.net, kevin.appleby@growcfo.net, Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: GrowCFO SEO & Analytics Update - 19 Oct 2021
msg_id: 17c970711f4564c6
---

Hi Dan, Kevin, and Alicia,
Hope all is well with you since we last spoke. I wanted to provide a quick recap
on how things are progressing - although bottom  line is generallysmoothly.
On the analytics front, I have Google Analytics set up on the site and
collecting data. Generally there's nothing unexpected there so far, although
I'll have a complete view of this in about a week once I understand exactly
what's required to set up tracking for conversion events (i.e. registrations,
payments, account creations, etc) and if we might need an hour or two of dev
work there. We'll also start planning the KPI tracking and dashboarding in the
coming weeks.
On the SEO front, I've started auditing the site and taking notes/actions for
next steps. However, I do need to set up Google Search Console, which requires
me logging in to your GoDaddy account. Alicia has provided me with the login
info, but I'll need to get a 2FA code from an email or SMS that will go to you. 
Is there a chance we could schedule 5 minutes for a quick call Wednesday morning
to tackle this quickly?
Also, to give an update on time spent, we're up to almost a half day of work
done so far. For reference, please find linked here a spreadsheet where I'm
keeping track of everything I'm working on (as well time tracking and expected
completion dates for various
activities): https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1s1BhTuhOY8CDLKFf7A79qTTY_LYFmI7Em4kOJmrZkEw/edit#gid=1799134040
And of course, if you have any comments, thoughts, or questions, please don't
hesitate to let me know. I'll look to send along another update towards the
middle to end of next week.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-10-16
to: Alicia Burke <alicia@growcfo.net>
subject: SEO and Analytics pre-update
msg_id: 17c86b6569b7ac5e
---

Hi Alicia,
Wanted to send you an update here before I send anything to the wider group
(which will be a much much simplified version of this, maybe 20% of the length).
I have a pretty solid understanding of what needs to be done now. I do have 2
particular things to raise, 1 of which is a bit of a roadblock I'll need your
help on: * 1) I was having a little trouble setting up Google Analytics, but I
   think i have  it working. I'm using the old version (i.e. not the version
   they released about 5 months ago). I don't think we particularly need the new
   version, but if we decide we do we might have to spend a bit more time
   finding a way to add the JS tag across the site header with your dev (as the
   little tag-entry box in the Custom Code menu of BuddyBoss plugin didn't work
   with the new code). But I think I have what I need for the time being, and it
   shouldn't prevent us from accessing any of the tracking we had in mind
   (although I will be testing this to double check over the coming days/weeks).
   I've shared admin access to this GA account with you as well.  One other
   thing to note here is that we probably need to make sure you have a cookies
   accept button and GDPR/data protection statement on the website if there
   isn't one now.

 * 2) The roadblock is that for  Google Search Console setup. I need the login
   credentials for the GoDaddy account to verify domain ownership (or to have
   you log in to Google Search Console, click "Add new property", enter
   "growcfo.net" in the domain property option, follow the SSO verification
   login to GoDaddy to approve/verify the connection, and then share access to
   the Google Search Console property with me). There are also some other ways
   we could do this, but this is definitely the simplest. Happy to jump on a
   call if it's easier to do this together, should take about 10 minutes tops.


Apart from that, I'm about halfway through mapping SEO improvements (the rest
are just waiting on Search Console access). I'm going to get started mapping out
conversion tags and implementation actions for Google Analytics, and plan to get
most of that done next week.
It'd be good to plan a call to discuss some of the SEO questions and actions
I'll have as well, so let me know when might work for you there (either early
morning or late afternoon/evening UK time is ideal for me in the coming weeks).
All in all, assuming we can get Google Search Console set up early this week, we
should be right on schedule.
And as always, I'm collecting pretty much everything
here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1s1BhTuhOY8CDLKFf7A79qTTY_LYFmI7Em4kOJmrZkEw/edit#gid=1388238213
Let me know if you have any feedback, thoughts, or questions, and when might
work for a call this week to get GSC setup over the line and discuss some SEO
strategy questions (shouldn't need more than 30 or 45 minutes max).
Hope you're having a nice weekend,
Matthew

date: 2021-10-15
to: David Willbe <David.Willbe@lewissilkin.com>
subject: Re: Convertible Debt agreement
msg_id: 17c854a75aa6afe9
---

Hi David,

Thanks for this - please find attached the two documents I mentioned.

The first and higher priority one (ArtSquare) is a convertible debt
arrangement, but I imagine I am missing quite a bit from this agreement to
ensure it's enforceable. I also plan on taking this agreement and copying
it for another client I'm in negotiations with.

The second (AngelsPartners) is actually not convertible debt, but we are
planning to sign updated agreements via SeedLegals every few months or so
basically updating the amount of equity I've earned, which will then start
the vest-and-cliff period. It sounds like this is how this founder is also
managing another advisor. Let me know if this sounds like it will present
issues (I do trust this founder and have known him quite a while).

Really appreciate your help on this, and of course let me know any costs
and when I might be able to turn around the ArtSquare agreement.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-10-15
to: Flaviu Dunca <duncaflaviu@gmail.com>, Johanna Moran <johanna@algorand.foundation>

msg_id: 17c8260ef9005059

Hi Flav,
As mentioned, please meet Johanna from the Algorand Foundation. She leads their
grant program, and can tell you a bit more about how it works and what they
would be expecting from you were you to be accepted.
Johanna, please meet Flav, who once upon a time many years ago was the first
employee I hired, and now has been leading UK growth at TikTok for the past.3
years. He's looking towards what's next for him, and is planning the launch of a
premium street fashion brand in collaboration with some artists, and wants to
use NFTs as proof of authenticity and to facilitate digital community/secondary
market.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew_____________________________

---

Hi Johanna,
As mentioned, please meet Alex Kay, the CEO of Brydg, who have  been building
real estate financing solutions for 8+ years. They've been considering
blockchain solutions for quite a few years now, are looking to move forward with
building some, and are thinking Algorand might be a good fit.
Alex, please meet Johanna, who is leading the ecosystem grants for Algorand
Foundation, and can tell you more about what expectations and support provided
in their grants are. She'll want to hear some of the details of what you're
planning/how you'll use the Algorand blockchain.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

and will feel out an intro to Alex from RELAYTO/ as well to ensure they're
actively looking to build a blockchain solution.

date: 2021-10-15
to: Marina Gurevich <marina.gurevich@wintermute-trading.com>
subject: Re: FW: [Ext] Following up
msg_id: 17c81a36ad88b3dc
---

Hi Marina,
I think I'd be more interested in the investment role than the marketing one
from what I'm hearing. If there's a vision to rapidly grow a marketing team of
say 5-10+ people in the next two years, then I might be mistaken, but it sounds
like the idea is more incrementally building a 2-4 person team in that time
frame.
I'll give a few notes on investment experience I have in the space, and if it
makes sense to continue our conversation, happy to do so - I'll also fill in the
application on the site later this evening. * I have about 18k on chain
   transactions across Eth, Avalanche, Fantom, Polygon, xDai, Celo, Algorand,
   Arbitrum, BSC with a Degen Score of about 260 (which is a bit misleadingly
   low as I split my assets across multiple wallets). I got on ETH defi in about
   August 2020, BSC in October 2020, Polygon in Jan 2021, Avax/FTM/xDAI last
   spring, and Algorand/Arbitrum/Celo in the past few months. I'm up well over
   20x overall, although I generally avoid any sort of borrowing or leverage
   (but have started to play around with it a little on Tarot on Fantom).

 * I have deposits in YFI, Badger, Curve (across multiple chains), Nexus Mutual,
   Pickle, Alchemix, Balancer, Harvest, Aave, as well as plenty more alt-chain
   copycat/experimental protocols, and have used most DEXs (Uni, Sushi, 1inch,
   Pangolin, Quick, Spooky, Spirit, Pancake, julswap, Traderjoe, Honeyswap,
   Jetswap, etc). I frequently use DefiLlama, Dappradar, Dextools, Dune, various
   trackers/scanners/etc and have been wanting to buy Nansen. In terms of CEXs,
   I've used Binance, Kraken, Coinbase, Kucoin, Uphold, Houbi, LAtoken, and FTX

 * Some notable private token sales I've participated in are ECOMI presale, FLOW
    Coinlist Sale, Sovrin/Verim/Cheqd presale (currently in Outlier Base Camp),
   various parachain auctions, and a few launchpad dapps presales (Polystarter,
   Avalaunch, ADAPad, ETHPad, etc, as well as some subsequent sales on those
   platforms). I had access to quite a few others during the 2017-2018 bull run,
   but I didn't really have access to liquid capital at the time.

 * I also have equity stakes in about 18 startups, about 5 of which are in the
   crypto/defi space (which I've directly facilitated about $2 million in grants
   for from  Algorand, not to mention follow on funding  from other investors).
   I've mentored hundreds if not thousands of startups and founders, have a
   4.9+/5 rating on Growthmentor with over 150 sessions there, and have mentored
   for at  least 30 accelerators/funds.

 * I've worked closely with Eterna Capital helping them set up an accelerator
   business line that they whitelabel to projects (Algorand and Oasis most
   notably) that creates very favorable investment terms. Through this, I get
   equal access and terms as Eterna in those accelerators due to my role in
   standing up operations, as well as access to their other deals in the main
   fund. I also served as program director for the Algorand Europe Accelerator,
   where I worked hands-on with 11 projects, about half of which were DeFi, and
   managed an investor/mentor network with participants from Coinbase, a16z,
   Gemini, Coinlist, DCG, etc.

 * If the portfolio companies on your website are the page here  that includes
   Zerion, I'm a daily active user (although prefer Zapper), and am friends
   with/was hired at Startupbootcamp by Alex Guy, their new head of growth (and
   I was the other 1 of 2 final candidates for that position at Zerion according
   to Evgeny and Vadim, although I was not sure I would accept it if offered). I
   also have good friends who work at various projects/funds in the
   crypto/blockchain/defi space, although hesitate  to name drop.


Best,
Matthew

Hi Mauro,

For equity - let's do $5k now, $5k by Dec 31 2021. I'll likely send via
blockchain (actually given that's okay, I'll likely make the full payment a bit
early, once I'm able to dump some tokens from the FLOW presale unlock at a
decent price).
More than happy to take a look at the deck as well, send along whenever you'd
like. I'm also prepping an email on behalf of another startup to reach out to
Sheel Tyle (who I have a lukewarm connection with, but who I used to do some
event speaking for, and was an early investor in Robinhood). It's a longshot,
but I'll look to include some info about ArtSquare (btw, is branding A-S capped,
or just A?) and see if we can get him interested.
Should we plan on a call (as well as look to set something recurring) middle to
end of next week to kick things off?

Let me know if there's anything else,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-10-09
to: join@dex.guru
subject: Head of Growth at DexGuru on Cryptocurrency Jobs
msg_id: 17c6555c2f68e032
---

I'd love to chat about the role you're looking to hire for and if I might be a
good fit.
I first discovered cryptocurrencies in late 2009, when my roommate mined 25 BTC
on his MacBook. I subsequently worked managing the marketing for a cash in/out
platform in 2013, but never got paid and it soured me on the space for a while.
In 2017, I started working on marketing and growth consulting projects in the
space, including helping with the Coinfirm ICO, their SEO, and rebuilding their
website, as well as planning the marketing for the Radix TGE campaign. In 2020,
I helped Eterna Capital pitch running accelerator programs for Algorand
Foundation, and subsequently designed and served as Program Director for the
Algorand Europe Accelerator, where we generated nearly 200 applications and our
projects have raised more than $25 million in venture funding post program.
After that, I was brought in to plan the commercial operations and marketing
launch for the Algorand-backed NFT platform Aorist, which soft launched in early
September, as well as another accelerator program for Oasis Foundation. Less
formally, I have more than 16,000 wallet transactions across Ethereum, Polygon,
Avalance, Fantom, xDai, Algorand, BSC, and other blockchains, lots of NFTs, etc.
Outside of crypto, I have a great deal of experience building commercial growth
using data driven, product-led tactics, and building and managing marketing
operations teams to scale growth. I was the first hire at Hackster.io, where I
managed all marketing, analytics, community, and product on the way to an 8
figure exit to Avnet. I also was the first marketing hire at Touch Surgery,
where I more than 10x’ed user growth and built a 4 person marketing team on the
way to a 9 figure exit to Medtronic. I led marketing for Startupbootcamp and
managed a team of 20+ marketers as we grew from 8 to 20 programs, and more than
2’xed applications per program and 3x’ed our referrals to nearly 10,000 startups
per year. I’ve also served as a consultant for a number of scale ups to build
analytics, marketing, growth, and sales capabilities and teams, including Quit
Genius, Codility, Vortexa, Jobbio, and many others. More recently, I’ve managed
marketing, operations, and analytics for the NTT DATA UK R&D and New Ventures
departments, predominantly focused on scouting, due diligence, and modeling to
secure management approval for 6 figure strategic investments in enterprise
startups.
I'm looking for something a bit more face paced and challenging, with a very
high calibre and harrdworking team, where I can make meaningful contributions to
scaling the commercial growth of a promising product.
I'd love to learn a bit more about what you're building. Please find attached my
CV.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-10-09
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Taxonomy
msg_id: 17c65235d336972b
---

Looks like 50k URLs or 10MB is the max sitemap size - multisitemaps  is big for 
multilanguage, and also particularly useful for sites where certain pages will
be updated a lot and others not very much (i.e. have sitemaps for each section
of the site and prioritize refresh/updates based on each one):
https://dynomapper.com/why-should-you-use-multiple-sitemaps

Re: firms - my thinking is whatever we pick should correspond to how people
search for it. I could see firms/angels/[individual angel] or
firms/vc/[individual vc], as well down the line. But all the firms we will have
will be investors, correct? Whereas there are many other types of non-investment
firms (and I could even see a long term revenue stream of getting legal and 
finance firms on the platform to offer supplementary services)
Again, I'm not rigid on this, but just trying to explain my line of thinking (as
most of it is really just trying to match behavioural economics concepts/user
search patterns against website infrastructure within Google's ranking
framework, so when it clicks for you, you'll likely start seeing potential SEO
improvements I would never be able to find that are more web development than
SEO).

---
date: 2021-10-09
to: web3-jobs@a16z.com
subject: My Web2 Corporate Tech Consultancy hates Web3
msg_id: 17c651567a3bf099
---

Hi,
I currently work running quant analytics, deal sourcing, internal ops
management, and some client advisory for the R&D/New Ventures department of NTT
DATA in the UK (i.e. a startup studio, 5-10 six-figure strategic investments in
startups per year, and other startup ecosystem-facing/market research
activities).
Most of our UK arm detests web3, to the point that I have been thrust forward as
a blockchain expert into some very senior discussions with our government,
telco, and banking clients, and even successfully got one's buy in for a large
PoC project but the idea was killed by our country leadership team due to
"feeling too high risk". Not to mention, it is discouraging to be one of the
hardest working people in an organization, and I'd like to find a place where
there are people who are passionate about their work and enjoy learning and
building.
I'm in the process of moving back to California (I fly back October 13th), and
would love to connect with anyone who you might think interesting  or chat about
roles within a16z. I'm also interviewing with some more traditional funds, but
would frankly much prefer to move to a more forward-thinking investment
organization (and with a higher likelihood of solid returns due to the likely
overall growth on the horizon in the space).

I have a wealth of experience in investment and crypto/blockchain: * I recently
   designed and directed (including managing a team of 5) the Algorand Europe
   Accelerator program, where our portfolio companies have raised $30 million
   since the program ended. i also designed and recruited the team for the Oasis
   Foundation accelerator, have been working with my friends who started Eterna
   Capital to set up a blockchain-accelerator-as-a-service business line, and
   consulted with Algorand and Eterna to plan the go to market and build the
   commercial operations for aorist, their premium art/NFT auction platform.

 * I served as Head of Operations and Marketing for Titanblock up until the 2018
   bear market hit, a boutique crypto advisory/investment fund, who is a lead
   advisor/managing the fundraising for Sovrin/Verim/Cheqd (and which I was able
   to invest in, currently going through Outlier Basecamp), ECOMI (also an
   investment of mine), Origin, and others

 * I consulted on go-to-market, marketing, and growth for Coinfirm (ICO
   marketing, SEO, built them a headless/serverless website), Radix (TGE
    marketing campaign), and others, I also run an small consultancy that works
   with scale ups (Quit Genius, Codility, Vortexa, Jobbio, heartbeat, etc) to
   solidify KPI/metric tracking, build automation and business intelligence
   capabilities, and scale marketing and growth teams/operations/metrics.

 * I serve as Head of Due Diligence for an Bridge Angel Investors, a Florida
   based and focused angel syndicate that participates in regional A rounds

 * I ran all global marketing and data across Startupbootcamp for 2 years,
   including managing the launch of 15+ accelerators, onboarding of 80+ staff,
   oversight of 20+ marketers, all ecosystem partnerships, creation of internal
   data and analytics tools, and drove a 2x increase in applications per program

 * I was the first employee at Hackster.io (acquired by Avnet), and the first
   marketer Touch Surgery (mid-9-figure acquisition by Medtronic) where I scaled
   growth from under 10k downloads per month to over 130k and built a 4 person
   marketing team

 * Before that, I worked for an SOSV/SVA/WTI backed startup in SF that failed
   (Incident Technologies - the gTar), in the research departments at University
   of Michigan, and in web1.0 SEO and social media for Car and Driver Magazine,
   the UMichigan B-school newspaper, my own (now defunct) music blog I grew to
   about 10k hits per day and a staff of 4 at its peak, and as an intern for
   Ghostly International records. I also have over 1,000 academic citations on
   peer-reviewed Psychology/Consumer Behaviour publications I co-authored
   on decision-making biases and heuristics

 * I love web3 - I have properties in most metaverses, hundreds of NFTs, 16k+
   on-chain transactions across Eth, FTM, AVAX, xDAI, Polygon, BSC, THORChain,
   and Celo. I first learned of BTC in 2009 when my roommate at Michigan (now a
   senior dev at Apple) mined 25 on his MacBook in our dorm. I then worked for a
   on/off-ramp  site in 2013, but never got paid, so it took me a while to get
   back into the space.

 * I run my own very small pre-seed and crypto investment fund, and have seen
   significant recent success with Cledara (about to announce a large A round),
   Yummy (just graduated YC), and TechTree (about to raise a large seed round, i
   introduced their cofounders and set them up with a 6 figure grant from
   Algorand), as well as in DeFi/crypto. I also levered and flipped a house in
   Ann Arbor for a 50%+ ROI in 18 months when I was in college during the
   housing market crash.


Any help is greatly appreciated!
Best,
Matthew

another benefit of the smaller investment is that I'll probably want more if
things go well haha. Pawel is having trouble convincing me to take over their
marketing/growth because I already have a decent equity stake, and feels like a
lot of consolidated risk to then go work there largely for equity. So were that
to ever become something interesting, probably a bit easier of an alignment.

Re: Memgraph, I think it might be a different story if they were a little
farther along getting to market. It sounds like they  enterprise model has been 
workiing ok for them, but they're offering is extremely  high price point, and
they want to try and take a more  Segment/Twilio. But to me, Segment was such a
huge use case driven by other areas of the business (finance reporting,

use case seems like it might have less urgency/commercial value

will hold off for now.
Have a good weekend!

date: 2021-10-09
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Taxonomy
msg_id: 17c64d5caa775e1d
---

Hey man,
Re: having firms first - it's probably not 100% ideal from a URL structuring
standpoint, but sounds like it's most sensible from a UX and technical
standpoint, would agree with your suggestion. I might change "firms" to
"investors" though, as I have a feeling it's more aligned with how people will
search (I haven't dug into Thomas's keyword research, but we could verify
there).
Re: the layout - often times the text in elements with a carousel or
click-to-expand UI can be invisible to Google crawler. And the pages feel a bit
short - any chance we could just have a 4x4 grid of investor photos/profiles, as
opposed to the carousel? We can always go ahead and deploy and then see how
Google actually crawls the page to decide if you prefer the single row +
carousel UI.
Re: - vs _,  "-" used in a URL is a word separator; "_" is most commonly used to
separate tracking tags and is not processed by Google as a word separator -
https://developers.google.com/search/docs/advanced/guidelines/url-structure 
(although they don't explain the why there)
Also just wanted to follow up if you had any changes/feedback/were ready to move
forward with the contract (or if there's anything you need from my side).
Looking forward to getting plugging  in!

On more random notes: * I have a call with the founder of Growthmentor
 * next week (where I connected with the startup that's
   raising on your platform serendipitously) - I think there's a pretty solid
   pool of founders there that have no idea how to raise funding, and they're
   pretty keen to try and open up some new revenue streams - he already has said
   he's open to talk partnership and rev shares, I'll feel him out and then
   likely loop you in for a call if it seems interesting so I can get a feel for
   how you'd want to position for partners (and of course we can plan that ahead
   of time, but I'll need to see you handle those client/partner development
   conversations a couple of times before I can really pick it up and run with
   them myself.

 * Also I got some feedback from my friend Idan who has sold some tech to Yamaha
   and NTT Docomo, and he had this to say: ""think  they'll be angling for an
   acquihire, because the tech is going to be limited appeal without
   use/community/users etc.  With that said, at the scale I'm assuming, it might
   just make sense for him to go find job postings and try to convince them to
   acquihire the company that way - or just pack up shop and move on.  I know
   it's a difficult choice to make though.  There are brokers, and also there's
   this recent start up acquisition platform thing: like
   https://microacquire.com/https://flippa.com/ etc - there are brokers, but
   it's a big effort and it's easier if you have concrete IP (like patents or
   something)."" -- I would probably suggest mentioning this to Ahdrita though -
   she might have some ideas as she has experience in more later stage capital

 * I also have been talking with Wintermute about a VC/M&A role, and sent your
   info to them in a follow up as a potential example of an interesting
   strategic investment to support their MM and HFT activities.

 * Also, i've  been thinking, I think we need to try and provide some unique
   support to startups at scale. I could see this being as simple as us
   providing some basic feedback to founders who come on the platform (probably
   largely manually at first but maybe with an angle of building up rules over
   time). I think for most founders, if we can find one single glaring mistake
   in their fundraising pitch (the one I spoke to has at least 2 - valuation and
   min ticket, not to mention just not enough justification as to why they could
   build it or financial planning) ,we probably build a huge amount of goodwill
   and engagement and drive activation. Just a thought. I think we probably need
   a similar quick win hook/aha point to drive investors to on their side (and
   it's likely something like a sourcing list to find them one good investment
   opportunity, or getting them to have one call with a founder that they
   actually find interesting to validate we can provide them with good deal
   flow0


Have a good weekend! And let me know if you might want to catch up Monday
afternoon/evening (otherwise most likely will have to wait til  Thurs or Friday
when I get to California).

with some more detailed questions following our conversation. Could you please

Matthew



Hi Marina,
It was good to talk to you earlier today. And apologies for my issues with
connection.
Following up on some more specific questions for each of the two roles that are
of interest:
Marketing: * What does the marketing "department" currently look like? Who are the
   employees, what are the budgets? What is the vision for growing it over the
   next 6/12/24 months?
 * What initiatives/channels are currently working well? Which ones
   that aren't or haven't in the past? Any you want to try in the immediate
   future?
 * Are there particular messages or value propositions that resonate
   with particular audiences that were surprising to discover?
 * Are there particular roles that are high priority to build
   employer branding for (analytics, technical. operations, sales/accounts,
   etc?)
 * Do you have any quantifiable goals from a commercial/growth
   standpoint targeted for upcoming periods of time? Or KPIs you're looking to
   drive growth of?
 * It seems like there is a priority on growthing the traditional
   institution customer base - is this true, are there any other segments of the
   4 you mentioned that are particularly high priority at the moment (and in
   that vein, is there a timeline of the launch for the more consumer-facing
   defi product you mentioned)?
 * It would also be good to do a bit of customer/solution/USP mapping
   to get a better feel for how the 4 segments you mentioned differ in terms of
   buying behavior, use case, value proposition, etc, as that would heavily
   inform content and channel strategy


Investment: * Is there a particular goal of the fund? Strategic investments to
   bolster product offerings? Maximizing investment portfolio returns? Taking
   advantage of favourable terms/deal flow we get access with founder clients?
   Providing vetted early stage investment offerings to institutional clients?
   Some prosocial/ecosystem building angle?
 * Is there a particular thesis/theme of the fund?
   Anything crypto/blockchain? Following on to the "crypto project founder"
   clients we work with? Regional focuses?
 * Any particular investment structures or stages? Seed vs growth
   funding? Convertible debt vs equity? Paired/discounted advisory or market
   making services? A more formal incubator/accelerator program?
 * How much capital will be allocated for investment? Is there a
   timeline for how/when it will be released? Is there an eye of launching
   additional funds if the first performs well?
 * Any vision/restrictions in terms of how we'll look to deploy the
   funds (leading vs following in rounds, portions of funds to follow on in
   successful portfolio companies, DeFi, token pre-sales vs equity, excluded
   countries)?
 * What investments have been made up until now?
 * What will the internal approval process look like for investments?
   Are there LPs on the fund?
 * As a relates side (and also related to employer branding), one of my other
   portfolio companies  has an interesting technical recruitment platform about
   to launch a new version that matches engineers by problems they want to
   solve/tech they want to work on with companies that are solving those
   problems/working with that tech (and helps the companies produce content to
   that end) - there's a wealth of referral-only web2 engineers trying to find
   jobs in web3 through the platform (and I also set them up with a
   substantial grant from Algorand to build a very forward-thinking token-based
   referral compensations scheme). It could be a quick win on employer
   branding/recruitment, and maybe an interesting investment to secure the
   engineering talent pipeline, if the goal of the VC/M&A arm is more strategic.
   Happy to provide a more thorough list of potential investments if that might
   be useful, but this and Omenics jumped to mind in the course of our
   conversations.


Would love to continue our conversation on either or both of the above paths.
Best,
Matthew

I'd love to learn more about the role, or any other similar ones, you're hiring
for.
I’ve worked in numerous roles expanding the capabilities of organizations to
make investments in early stage companies. At Startupbootcamp Global, I
standardized internal data and managed internal scouting tool development to
facilitate startup recruitment efforts and 2x the volume of applications (to
more than 20k per year) and 3x our referral pipeline for accelerator programs,
largely driven through an increase in ecosystem referrals and mentorship
events.
At NTT DATA, I managed quant analytics and product-based P+L modeling for 10+ 6
figure strategic investments into enterprise cloud, SaaS, cybersecurity, and
data startups (such as eXate, Weaviate, Jacada, and Gong). I’ve also designed
and managed accelerators on a consulting basis for Algorand Foundation,
Rainmaking Innovation, Eterna Capital, and others, and have served as a mentor
or EiR for 30+ accelerator programs and funds (including Google, Start-up Chile,
the Austrian Trade Commission, Hatch, London Business School, Forward Partners,
CyLon, etc).
In addition to experience in more alternative investment models, I also have
extensive experience sourcing, screening, and managing traditional startup
equity investments, both independently and in larger organizations. My own
portfolio has centered on startups that are building infrastructure for or
useful applications with data. Some of my portfolio companies include Cledara
(Techstars) and Yummy (YCombinator), which are seeing very rapid growth. I also
have served as interim Head of Due Diligence for the past few years for a
Florida-based angel syndicate, Bridge Angel Investors, where we invest low
6-figures into A round startups, as well as served as Head of Operations for the
setup of a crypto investment and advisory fund, TitanBlock.
Most of all, I love working with startups. I’ve mentored hundreds of startups in
the past decade (and have a rating of 4.9+ after 150+ mentorship sessions on 
Growthmentor  in the past 3 years). I’ve also worked on analytics and growth
consulting projects for more than 50 early stage companies, including companies
now scaling up such as Quit Genius, Vortexa, Codility, Jobbio, Pento, Heights,
and others. And I've also been an early employee at multiple startups, 2 of
which were backed by Balderton, 2 of which successful exits (one of which,

per month sold for 9 figures).

Matthew

date: 2021-10-05
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Taxonomy
msg_id: 17c523aa6c17cb20
---

I would think about it like this:
 * angelspartners.com/
 * find-startup-investors/ * [INDUSTRY]
    * [LOCATION]
   
   

And then the [FRIM] pages should be nested under whichever is cleaner to fit or
higher search volume - there will likely be some duplicates in terms of a FIRM
showing up in multiple LOCATIONs or INDUSTRYs, so we need to ensure there is a
primary parent folder indicated for the FIRM in whichever category the pages are
indexed in (i.e. a FIRM should only show up as a URL in one INDUSTRY (assuming
we decide they should be indexed in INDUSTRY and not LOCATION), but should be
listed on the front end of other INDUSTRY and LOCATION pages (but clicking on
the result would take you to the indexed page which would use it's primary
INDUSTRY as the subdirectory) - I think location might actually make more sense
for investors, and industry probably makes more sense for startups (as we can
apply a very similar strategy to startups as well)
Where capitalized and bracketed words are filled by the taxonomy and generative
pages. Ideally the items in the lists on these pages would link to the
appropriate [FIRM] pages to create solid internal linking
Over time, I would see creating pages like * [INDUSTRY]/[LOCATION]
 * [LOCATION]/[INDUSTRY] (ensuring
   that there is enough difference between these pages and the bullet above that
   they do not flag duplicate content - i.e. reversinng the order, but that can
   only work once - maybe its  a shift down the list and start on the "second
   page" so to speak (and third and fourth pages as we add more pages that could
   half overlapping double taxonomies)
 * [LOCATION]/[SUB-LOCATION]
 * 
   [LOCATION]/[SUB-LOCATION]/[[INDUSTRY] -investors in a specific city in a
   specific industry
 * and basically trying to fit any
   list structure based on a combination of filters into this - i could see
   [FUNDING STAGE], [INDUSTRY]/[PORTFOLIO COMPANY], and a few other taxonomies
   being interesting


I could also see creating generated pages underneath the FIRM pages - i.e. a
list of coinvestors, a list of portfolio companies - so [FIRM]/portfolio,
[FIRM]/coinvestors, etc

Let me know if this makes sense :)  - the key is to avoid duplicating pages
which the individual startup and firm pages are where the most risk of doing
this (as long as we can alter the order/items in the various generated
list pages for each page to avoid duplicating them)

Sounds like some good progress. I was actually talking to a good friend
yesterday who was debating whether to take over a startup wise guys program
in Denmark or Africa from the sound of it

Sounds good re working focused and comp - let me know if the times above
work, or feel free to suggest some alternatives (Im quite flexible in the
evenings as well if you work late).

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-10-05
to: rusu claudia <rusu.claudia@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Have a great day! :)
msg_id: 17c50d9fdea54947
---

Hey Rusu,
Very much agree, and Jonathan will probably be more receptive, I'm just not as
close with him and waiting on a ball that's in his court at the moment - I
think Mike is relishing not being in an agency anymore (he penned a few blog
posts shortly after he left about how difficult the agency model is to make
work) and saying no to as many meetings as possible while he focuses on building
and his child.
But no problem at all if I mention I suggested you try and get in touch with
them - they handle a lot of finance/fintech, so i think it'd be a good
conversation :)
And that's just the way it is with subcontracting - I have plenty else on
keeping me busy though, so not an issue.

date: 2021-10-05
to: William Bainborough <william@doordeck.com>
subject: Checking in on moving out
msg_id: 17c50d4ab5577fe7
---

Hi Will,
Hope all is well. Just wanted to check in as we get  closer to my move out. I
assume you received the photos I sent? A few other items:
1) Let me know when you want to do the viewing/do showings - I'm out and about a
bit more these days, but can try to make sure to be around if you give me a
heads up (and at least make sure everything is tidy). Or if it won't be until
after I'm out, let me know how you'd like me to get you the keys.
2) For the furniture, at this point I haven't really been able to sell any, so
if there's any at all that you'd like to keep (or just want to keep it furnished
for showing/in interim), it'd save me the cost of removals. The bed in
particular would be a shame to let go to waste, and a headache to not have a bed
my last night here.
Let me know if there's anything else,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-10-04
to: rishir@tarsadia.com
subject: Tarsadia Investments role
msg_id: 17c4d8d15c428986
---

Hi,
I'm in the process of moving back to Southern California from London, and
exploring opportunities where I can bring my expertise in investment sourcing,
due diligence, and supporting founders to make a significant impact in managing
and growing a fund.

I’ve worked in numerous roles expanding the capabilities of organizations to
make investments at scale. At Startupbootcamp Global, I standardized internal
data and managed internal data product development to guide startup recruitment
efforts and 2x the volume of applications (to more than 20k per year) and 3x our
referral pipeline for accelerator programs, largely driven through an increase
in ecosystem referrals and mentorship events. At NTT DATA, I managed quant
analytics and product-based P+L modeling for 10+ 6 figure strategic investments
into enterprise cloud, SaaS, cybersecurity, and data startups (such as eXate,
Weaviate, Jacada, and Gong). I’ve also designed and managed accelerators on a
consulting basis for Algorand Foundation, Rainmaking Innovation, Eterna Capital,
and others, and have served as a mentor or EiR for 30+ accelerator programs and
funds (including Google, Start-up Chile, the Austrian Trade Commission, Hatch,
London Business School, Forward Partners, CyLon, etc).

In addition to experience in more new wave investment models, I also have
extensive experience sourcing, screening, and managing traditional startup
equity investments, both independently and in larger organizations. My own
portfolio has centered on startups that are building infrastructure for or
useful applications with data. Some of my portfolio companies include Cledara
(Techstars) and Yummy (YCombinator), which are seeing very rapid growth. I also
have served as interim Head of Due Diligence for the past few years for a
Florida-based angel syndicate, Bridge Angel Investors, where we invest low
6-figures into A round startups, as well as served as Head of Operations for the
setup of a crypto investment and advisory fund, TitanBlock.

Most of all, I love working with startups. I’ve mentored hundreds of startups in
the past decade (and have a rating of 4.9+ after 150+ mentorship sessions on 
Growthmentor  in the past 3 years). I’ve also worked on analytics and growth
consulting projects for more than 50 early stage companies, including companies
now scaling up such as Quit Genius, Vortexa, Codility, Jobbio, Pento, Heights,
and others. And I've also been an early employee at multiple startups, 2 of
which were successful exits, one of which sold for nearly half a billion
dollars.
I'd love to chat about how I could contribute to what you're building. Please
find attached my CV.
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

date: 2021-10-04
to: Ishaq Namajee <ishaq.namajee@wintermute-trading.com>
subject: Venture Investing/M&A Role
msg_id: 17c4c5c0778d3d1d
---

Hey Ishaq,
First of all, just  wanted to say I really enjoyed our call the other day.
Usually a company's recruiters are a pretty good indication of company culture,
so it was cool to see how knowledgeable you were about the sector and
Wintermute's offering (surprising how often that isn't the case).
I just wanted to reach out on another thread - I was scanning some other role
listings at Wintermute, and wasn't sure if it might also make sense to have a
conversation around the Venture Investments and M&A role (but I certainly
wouldn't want to take myself out of the running to learn more/explore fit for
the Marketing role). Quite a lot of my focus over the past 5 years, and general
background is around this space.
Some high level bullets: * Currently, I manage due diligence, market and
   competitor research,financial modeling/forecasting, and the process of
   securing leadership team approval for NTT DATA's UK investments in enterprise
   SaaS, cybersecurity, adtech/martech, and blockchain startups - with about $1
   mil per year earmarked for strategic investments with an goal of
   operational integration and potential future acquisition

 * I also have been working closely with Eterna Capital over the past few years
   to set up an advisory/consulting business unit that designs and manages
   accelerators for their blockchain portfolio companies (notably the Eterna
   Borderless Venture Studio, Algorand Europe Accelerator, and upcoming Oasis
   Foundation Accelerator). I personally served as Program Director for the
   Algorand program after we won the RFP, and we generated 200 applications and
   took 3%-6% stake in 11 startups, which have now raised more than $26 million
   in the 6 months since the program ended

 * I've run my own investment fund for about 4 years, split about 2/3 to 1/3
   between startup equity and crypto. On the equity side, I will likely see
   many-multiple returns on the fund through investments in Cledara (Techstars,
   about to announce a very large A round) and Yummy (just graduated
   YCombinator), despite missing deals with unicorns like Flink and QuitGenius
   due to my tiny ticket size, and not including 10 or so other investments that
   could also grow into significant returns. On the crypto side, I have already
   returned the full fund principal and maintain significant NFT, DeFi, and CEX
   positions, and have reinvested a fair amount of gains into token
   presales/equity in blockchain companies/etc

 * Previously, I built up the startup scouting, portfolio management, and
   databasing capabilities for Startupbootcamp across their 20 programs, and
   managed 50+ scouting staff to improve our investment sourcing capabilities by
   more than 2x'ing applications per program, and 3x'ing our referral pipeline
   by scaling ecosystem and investor partnerships, and managed much of our
   expansion strategy and new market entries as we grew to make over 1000
   startup investments. I served as Head of Due Diligence for Bridge Angel
   Investors, an angel syndicate mainly comprised of  retired corporate
   executives in Florida, to lead low 6-figure A round investments into
   Florida-based tech companies. I also served as Head of Operations for
   TitanBlock, a fund some friends of mine set up that invested in Origin,
   ECOMI, Sovrin/Verim/Cheqd, and a number of other blockchain projects, as
   their knowledge comprised more of crypto markets and trading thann
   entrepreneurship and equity investing,

 * On a more personal note, my dad worked in PE/M&A, so I've been looking
   at investment pitches since I was around 13 years old (whenever he wanted a
   kid's/teen's/digital native take on one of the deals he was looking at). I
   tried to get away from it after graduating (despite coming out of a
   relatively traditional business program in Uni), but have found myself
   gravitating back towards it over the past 5 or 6 years


Let me know if it might make sense to chat around that!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-10-04
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Angels Partners SEO report
msg_id: 17c4bd80d844933d
---

Hi Yohann,
Certainly can draft up a contract - will look to have that to you EOD tomorrow
at the latest.
I'm quite flexible tomorrow afternoon if that would work for you?

These pages are a great start. 2 comments that come to mind at first glance are
1) we probably want to show more results per page if possible, to try and reach
a 300 word threshold per page and
2) we definitely want to tweak the URLs - drop generic words like "for" and
"your", use dashes instead of underscores, and instead of the ?, we should
ideally create another layer of subdirectory folders (or more likely 2 layers,
one for location, and one for industry - I'll have to have a think as to which
should be closer to the root layer, but probably the one with higher search
volumes).
We'll also likely want to have some breadcrumbs (or visual way to navigate
between the layers), as well as maybe a block of links at the bottom of the page
linking to "pages other visitors went to" or something like that to build
internal links across these pages and create a richer user journey.

Let me know if all that makes sense, and can put something a bit more thorough
together later this week if desired.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-10-04
to: Patrick Alpas <patrick.alpas@avalabs.org>
subject: Re: Ava Labs Interview Availability
msg_id: 17c48abfec8fb10e
---

Hey Patrick,
Definitely - well I am a bit of a personal fan of Avalanche, so I'd be pretty
excited to help grow it :) Growth Marketing certainly sounds like it could be a
good fit looking at the role.
In terms of DeFi, I got into Ethereum dapps at the end of last summer due to
lockdown boredom, including Telegram, Cryptotwitter, and lots of other research.
I got on alt-chains (starting with BSC, particularly Pancakeswap
/Beefy/Autofarm/Jetswap/a few others) in October/November 2020 when i saw the
high yields and once I figured out how to set up custom networks on Metamask,
and had a nice little run in the DeFi wave in early 2021 (and the subsequent
alt-chain waves).
I have about 80%+ of my crypto onchain as opposed to on CEXs, and probably 90%
of that split between DeFi and NFTs. I've used pretty much every dapp on
Avalanche (Markr really makes it amazingly easy to find solid projects and track
positions - I think the only dapps on there I have not  used are Frost and
Partyswap), as well as most of the main dapps on Ethereum, Polygon, Fantom,
xDAI, Celo, Algorand, and BSC (and cross chain bridges), with at least 15k
transactions and way too much ETH gas spent. I can send you my degen score or my
wallet number if you'd like (although I'm a little hesitant on the latter b/c
some of the insane hacks that have been happening recently, and the former isn't
really that useful/up to date). I also was a presale backer of Avalaunch and
have participated in one of the token sales since their product launch (as well
as use BSCPad and a few other launchpads), and have at least one or two of most
of the early Avalanche NFTs - Bridges, Bros, LootFish, AvaxPunks, Avalanche
Punks, Cryptoseals, lots of HatchyPockets, etc). I'm actually waiting to
withdraw this week's XAVA presale unlock right now before I go to bed, and I
generally reinvest or reposition my Avalanche defi positions once or twice per
week (even a bit more since the transaction fee drop  a few weeks ago).
All to say, would  love to learn more about any roles that might be a fit -
Growth Marketing, Product Marketing, or otherwise. Happy to book a time for a
call if that'd be an ideal next step.
Best,
Matthew

subject: Re: Fwd: About TechTree

Hey man - haha we definitely need to catch up, and seems  like I might have to
take you up at staying at yours for a night or two given the insane rental costs
in SF and probably just needing the time to thoroughly kick around all these
ideas.
Re: Epoch, sounds fine re: you running the small tickets - I can commit $10k -
I'm in the process of booking some returns, so it'd help if I could put off
about half of the actual transfer of funds until the end of the year if
possible, but I can be flexible.
SPC meaning South Park Commons? That would be awesome to connect with them. I'm
planning to just catch up with as many people as I can when I'm up there and see
what threads are interesting (planning to reconnect with Ethan, and have already
been interviewed/turned down for a role at Maurice's fund).
Funny you should mention incubator - I've had an idea in the back of my head for
a while that would sort of be some hybrid between an
incubator/accelerator/artist fellowship/real estate play, maybe even as a DAO.
Not to mention have seem to have found a bit of a niche setting up blockchain
accelerators (and just in accelerators in general). I actually have a good
friend from college (who I haven't done a great job of keeping in touch with,
but planning to reconnect with when I'm back out there) who's ex-YouTube and
gotten a fair amount of attention for founding https://www.wilburlabs.com/, but
I worry about the startup studio model as being relatively surface level market
plays as opposed to building more foundational tech. Would be curious to pick
your brain on how to make a version that would actually be useful for more
technical founders. Some ex colleagues of mine from Startupbootcamp Amsterdam
are also working on an interesting model, with particular emphasis on the
milestone based follow ons https://www.venturerock.com/. Entrepreneur First and
Antler (who I helped with their UK launch and introed Pawel to them as a Venture
Partner actually) are also some interesting models that against my
pre-judgement, seem to be working pretty well

Hey Matthew,
Sorry for the extreme delay here - I think our messages are particularly likely
to slip my eye in my inbox with our name :)
I'll ping you on WA sometime this week if that's okay, although not a huge
amount of urgency at what I'm looking at - just basically onshoring my
consulting business and investment fund (which is a little more complicated with
some of the crypto investing I do) from the UK.
I've been in the UK pretty much the entire last 2 years apart from a month in
Florida seeing family about 2 months ago, but am moving out of my flat in London
on October 13th and heading to LA, and then traveling a bit from there
(Colorado, SF, Costa Rica, then likely the east coast, a bit of Europe and
potentially Asia/LATAM) until I figure out where to settle a bit more
permanently, if giving up US citizenship makes sense, etc. Are you still in CH
these days? I'm long overdue for a trip there to see family, so hopefully will
be around in the next year or so.
Hope all has been well, and looking forward to catching up!
Matthew

date: 2021-10-03
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Angels Partners SEO report
msg_id: 17c477290fc44fac
---

Cool - this pretty much all makes sense to me.
I think the area's where I could see this expanded a bit in the future once some
of the more quick wins are tackled are: * the more technical areas (i.e. looking
   at ways we can improve the PageSpeed Insights scores - caching, CDNs,
   compression, etc),
 * planning the content hierarchy of the
   site (basically URL subdirectory taxonomy)
 * looking at how to go beyond the page
   creation strategies that competitors are using (as well as how to make those
   pages as useful as possible for linkbuilding purposes)


Not sure where you are with tag writing, but also happy to support on that,
especially when it comes to generated pages where we'll likely use a constructor
from structured data to create tags
I typically run this audit as part of my SEO projects, but given that we already
have the deck here, I'd suggest I more just make notes of any issues I see as
I'm going through other tasks, and then we can discuss them and decide how to
approach, as opposed to me performing the complete audit (which takes between a
half day and a day typically).
Access to Google Search Console would also be helpful, as often technical issues
are flagged with a bit more detail than on third party tools or the PageSpeed
Insights.

---
date: 2021-10-03
to: Idan Beck <idanbeck@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Fwd: About TechTree
msg_id: 17c46724e17fa934
---

Hey man, thanks for following up
My trying to write a bit of a blurb for you and what I'm looking for has evolved
into a bit more of a cover letter version writing exercise (you can see here if
you're curious - still trying to parse it down to a paragraph each, at which
point I was going to send you the non-crypto ones and mention that I had
versions for crypto as well if it was more relevant).
I think in essence, I'm looking for one of two things. On the startup side, I'm
keen to find something where I can build, optimize, and/or scale
marketing/growth/commercial/analytics/automation through data-driven, product
led growth tactics, in an earlier stage company with or very close to product
market fit (probably somewhere between late seed and B round) in an emerging or
deeper tech industry (data, health/biotech, crypto, fintech, AR/VR, dev tools,
certain SME B2B SaaS, etc - somewhere where there's opportunity for new
platforms/infrastructure/significant applications for data to be built).
Basically a company where I could go in and 10x growth, create
analytics/automation/product marketing capabilities, build out a
marketing/growth team, plan expansion, and develop into a senior leadership
role.
On the more investment side, I'm hoping to be able to use my experience creating
tools and analysis for early stage startup investments and market research,
managing my own investments (notable are Cledara and Yummy), leading due
diligence for angel syndicates, corporates, and accelerators, and the network
I've built mentoring and consulting to directly invest or facilitate investment
at scale in startups in emerging markets or deeper tech sectors, either for a
fund (particularly one that sees the opportunity in leveraging data for
sourcing, due diligence, and portfolio management given my background designing
and managing these systems), an investor targeted product (Angellist, equity
crowdfunding products, cap table mgmt tools, etc), or maybe even a corporate but
I'm hesitant there based on my experience at NTT. or an accelerator but I would
be pretty picky there.
My B plans at this point are to pick up more consulting and advisory work, and
then maybe either try to raise a fund to invest in data
infrastructure/applications  or (slightly less likely) build a product around
growth data for companies (think cross platform AppAnnie/Google Analytics plus
industry benchmarking) or community-driven CRM. But I'd probably prefer to find
something to join than start myself at this point in all honesty, as I see it as
less risky/more potential to build something meaningful.
I'm in a bunch of interview processes, but nothing particularly compelling.  And
 fortunately there isn't much pressure on either my workload or exit timing from
NTT, so I can take my time and work on projects on the side until I find
something.
That's awesome to hear about Epoch - sounds like validation from a lot of
different angles! Let me know if you have any of the round  left for small
ticket family/friends, but totally understand if not.  And if you want to chat
around more marketing/growth/go-to-market roadmap, would  definitely love to.
I'm moving out of my flat in London and flying to LA (where my mom moved maybe 5
or so years ago) on October 13. I have a few little trips planned (Colorado,
Utah, Costa Rica), although nothing solid for SF yet - I was thinking maybe
trying to make it up there the week of the 18th, and then trying to come back in
December for 2-3 weeks.
Ha, got a bit carried away here. Will let you know as I confirm my dates
(probably will text my mom today and see if I can steal her car the week of the
18th today, so should  have a clearer idea next week).
Hope all is well!

msg_id: 17c418e2cb207c06

Hi Patrick,
Thanks for your response. Do you have a job description?
If it's a solely PPC/paid advertising oriented position, I probably wouldn't be
the best fit. I do have a great deal of experience with paid advertising, from
working hands on creating campaigns and buying ads on various ad networks as
they were emerging 10-15 years ago, to managing ad teams of 3-4+ people,
multiple outsourced agencies, and 6-figure monthly budgets. However, I would say
I am more skilled in other areas.
If the concept is broader and includes a few other areas such as SEO, email,
affiliates/influencers, marketing automation, measurement and reporting, CRO,
A/B testing, acquisition/retention, app/mobile marketing, quant/product/growth
marketing, and training and managing junior marketing teams, that would
definitely be up my alley.
And more generally, I'd be very keen to explore any roles that might be a fit. I
like working with products I believe in, and I have over 1000 transactions on
Avalanche chain in the past 6 months. I did poke around your Greenhouse and see
a few other roles that could be interesting (Community Growth Director, Growth
Marketing Director, and Product Marketing Director particularly), but not sure
if those are in-date.
Have a good weekend!
Matthew

structure this as convertible debt if possible, to protect against the case of a
token not being released, but let me know your thoughts.

amounts (and assuming Option A), I could do $5k now, and commit now to transfer
a second $5k before the end of the year. Or if that's not workable, I could do
$5k now, and we can revisit additional investment at the next raise. I also want
to check, how would you prefer payment - any preference for onchain vs bank
transfer?
I'd love to chat around equity at some point, but I think it probably makes
sense to start working with the above and see how we progress.
Have a good weekend!
Matthew

California for a while, so have  been pretty swamped myself the past weeks.
If you're open to me having a side project or two on until we hit enough revenue
to get to something a bit more sustainable, I could definitely be interested at
a much much lower cost (or even almost all equity and/or tokens).
Ultimately I want to work on something where the future equity is
potentially worth 10x more than any salary I'd be making, and there are pretty
few areas in platform/infrastructure where there's that potential.
And either way, would  love to learn a bit more about your vision/roadmap here.
And congrats re: the launch - looks cool. The idea of fractional ownership here
is really compelling (I recently invested in/am advising ArtSquare, which is a
very different product but I think similar mechanics/disruption in terms of
making fine art investment accessible through tokenization, but not even close
to comparable in terms of potential future market size). I'm a little hazy on
what a payload could be, and curious how fractional shares could affect the
ability to allocate actual space due to lack of consensus (slash curious what
you have in mind to navigate that), but generally makes sense :)

my accountants here about a month ago.
I also ended up needing to order a new checkbook for the 2019 IRS payment, which
I had to have sent to a US address. I should receive them on October 14 when I'm
in Los Angeles, and will send payment to the IRS within a week or so of
arriving.
I'm also still pushing on my UK accountancy to start the handover of Xero for
you, but again, not very responsive as of late. I also have some straggling tax
balances I need to pay off for my UK companies, so am holding off starting to 
receive revenue into the US company until I have that sorted (as well as I'm
having some slight hiccups with my US business banking).
Do let me know if there's anything else!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-29
to: David Willbe <david.willbe@lewissilkin.com>
subject: Convertible Debt agreement
msg_id: 17c326057a6c08fb
---

Hi David,
Hope all is well with you since we last spoke!
I'm working with a pair of advisory clients where there will be a significant
portion of payment in somewhat complicated sweat equity terms as convertible
debt.
I was wondering if I might be able to have you look over the contracts as
opposed to the more investment contract reviews you had done for me before, or
if you might recommend someone who could help?
Let me know if that might be possible - I should have drafts of the agreements
by the end of the week or beginning of next hopefully.
Thanks,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-29
to: Mauro Piva <mauro@artsquare.io>
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 17c325c0dd542413
---

Hey Mauro,
It was great to chat the other day, and to connect with Eleonora. I'll look
forward to her follow up and intro to Marco.
Re: impressions on Eleonora, I hesitate to use the word impressed as I haven't
really seen what she can do yet, but she seems aware of what she doesn't
know/isn't strong at in terms of marketing, and seems eager to expand her skills
where relevant. And from what I can tell, she seems well aligned for the art
sector/content marketing side of the business, but also appreciates that
content is only part of growth. I guess the best summary would be I'm looking
forward to working with her and think we'll have a good workflow/compliment each
other well :)
In terms of agreement, I can certainly draw something up, but would the
following terms make sense to you broadly? * £1,000 per day effective rate (50%
   markup from my typical rate for payment in equity, 33% discount as portfolio
   company (assuming investment)) accrued as convertible debt, with share price
   fixed to most recent valuation
 * 2 days per month allocated, if we
   go under time in a month, days will be rolled into the next month, or reduced
   pro-rata from equity payment if there are unused hours at end of contract
 * Equity will be subject to 6 month
   vest, 2 year cliff
 * Potential for debt buyout at
   ArtSquare's request during the first 3 months
 * Equal or discounted valuation to my
   investment terms
 * Potential to convert payment from
   equity to part equity/part cash if agreed upon by both parties
 * A summary of work to be performed
   will be agreed upon each month, and an update and hour tracking provided at
   the end of each month
 * If it looks like more time will
   unexpectedly be spent on work in a month than planned at the beginning of
   each month, the Advisor will provide notification as soon as possible so that
   a decision can be made whether to approve budget allocation beyond 2 days
 * If we would like to allocate more
   than 2 days in a month, both parties must agree. The fee will be the same as
   for existing work, but ideally at least 50% would be in cash as opposed to
   convertible debt (although we can waive this until we're post-revenue/have
   raised funds and cash isn't such a friction point, and the day rate for cash
   portion of payments would be £777 per day)
 * 1 month termination notice from
   either side


Let me know if that makes sense and I can draft up an agreement to these points.
In terms of investment, do you have a min ticket? I'm in the process of bringing
some returns back into the fund, but it will take a while to get all of that
processed (hopefully end of October, but maybe more like end of the year). Up
until that point, I still have a small amount of cash in the fund, so I could do
one of the following if either makes sense (and in both cases I'd prefer to get
in inn the Option A you laid out above, as it generally seems favourable/no
downside compared to Option B apart from being a bit earlier/ever so slightly
higher risk, although correct me if I'm missing something): * $10k investment
   with $2k paid immediately, and the rest due by the end of 2021
 * $5k investment
   now, could maybe go a little higher if that would help with min ticket
   (although more like 3k might be a little bit more ideal if that's not a pain
   to go that low)


Let me know any thoughts or questions you have, or when might make sense to hop
on a call to discuss in more detail.
Looking forward to working together!
Matthew

---

to: Dan Wells <dan.wells@growcfo.net>
subject: Re: Following up on call
msg_id: 17c31a32ad4e0b47
---

Thanks very much.

I’ll plan a call with Alicia for next week to get work started, and look to

week.

Glad to be working together! And of course, please don’t hesitate to reach
out if you have any thoughts or questions or want to jump on a call.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-28
to: Daniel Johnson <daniel@wescalestartups.com>
subject: Re: FW: SEO Support Needed
msg_id: 17c2cb27becef0a5
---

If it’s rolling monthly, there probably needs to be someone managing this
full time (or the people writing the content need to pick up tags writing,
which is very simple if they’re provided a framework). Do each of these
websites have their own marketing teams? Is there a marketing team that
works across them all? Or are these individual clients?

Easy enough to set up an infrastructure/process (the part that really
requires an expert) and train someone (either internal team members who are
writing the content, or a full time junior hire just to manage SEO
tagging), or if there’s not appetite for that, you’re probably looking at
poaching from a marketing or seo agency. But generally I think that in
house marketers do a better (and much cheaper job) of tag writing/link
building than an external agency if they’re provided with some keyword
research analysis and are trained for an hour or so.

Web dev might be needed if there’s any UI SEO optimisation that needs to be
done or other technical work. Generally way simpler to use someone who
knows the code base.

Yeah, I met Simon when he was first starting it in 2014 - we refer clients
back and forth. He’s generally one of the only SEO agencies I think
actually does a great job, but they only work on sites with thousands, and
more like tens of thousands of pages



On Tue, 28 Sep 2021 at 14:38, Daniel Johnson <daniel@wescalestartups.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2021-09-28
to: Daniel Johnson <daniel@wescalestartups.com>
subject: Re: FW: SEO Support Needed
msg_id: 17c2c5aeaf0acf62
---

I could do most, if not all of this, but it’s more a question of capacity.
If the work below (not including the stuff explicitly tasked for 6-12
months out) is needed in a month or so, that’s going to be tricky (read:
costly). If it’s more staged over 2-3 months, that could work, or we could
hire a digital nomad freelancer to pick up some copywriting work (and
actually I know someone who might be a good fit).

Do you know what these sites are built on and if there’s in house web
development capacity in their side (or did they use an external
agency/developer and are they still engaged/on retainer)?

Blue array would be way too expensive for this. Apart from that, there’s no
one I’d super recommend - just a few okay agencies and cold freelancer
emails that could maybe be interesting if you made sure to manage their
delivery for quality



On Tue, 28 Sep 2021 at 12:57, Daniel Johnson <daniel@wescalestartups.com>
wrote:

(particularly adding a little bit of email work into Phase 1) here  - when we
get started I'll create a new document and folder where we can keep track of the
work I'm doing and backlog/stage what we prioritize next (or can do this within
Notion).

account accesses. I'm happy to work through an artsquare.io email address and
have you provide accesses  to that if it would be preferable from an
admin/ops/security standpoint. If so, can you please create the account for me
(or if not, just provide accesses to the email I'm sending from here), and

useful to have): * Slack

 * Bitly

signing, and if you need anything from my side (or want to jump on a call). Or
happy to draw up an initial version of an agreement if that would be ideal.

Very excited to be working together :) Of course, let me know if you have any
thoughts or questions in the meantime. And please feel free to
whatsapp/text/imessage me at +447523506891 whenever you need.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-26
to: info@drops.co
subject: Head of Growth at Drops DAO on Cryptocurrency Jobs
msg_id: 17c2318f6733ef06
---

I first discovered cryptocurrencies in late 2009, when my roommate at university
read the bitcoin whitepaper and mined 25 BTC in our dorm on his MacBook (I
turned him down on having him show me how as well). I subsequently worked
managing the marketing for a cash in/out platform in 2013, but never got paid
and it soured me on the space for a while. In 2017, I started working on
marketing and growth consulting projects in the blockchain space, including
helping with the Coinfirm ICO, their SEO, and rebuilding their website, and
planning the marketing for the Radix TGE campaign. In 2020, I helped Eterna
Capital pitch running accelerator programs for Algorand Foundation, and
subsequently designed and served as Program Director for the Algorand Europe
Accelerator, where we generated nearly 200 applications to the program and our
projects have raised more than $25 million in venture funding post program.
After that, I was brought in to plan the commercial operations and marketing
launch for the Algorand-backed NFT platform Aorist, which soft launched in early
September. Less formally, I have more than 15,000 wallet transactions across
Ethereum, Polygon, Avalance, Fantom, xDai, Algorand, BSC, and other blockchains.
Outside of crypto, I have a great deal of experience building customer traction
using data driven tactics, and building and managing marketing operations teams
to scale growth. I was the first hire at Hackster.io, where I managed all
marketing, analytics, community, and product on the way to an 8 figure exit to
Avnet. I also was the first marketing hire at Touch Surgery, where I more than
10x’ed user growth and built a 4 person marketing team on the way to a 9 figure
exit to Medtronic. I led marketing for Startupbootcamp and managed a team of 20+
marketers as we grew from 8 to 20 programs, and more than 2’xed applications to
programs to nearly 10,000 startups per year. I’ve also served as a consultant
for a number of scale ups to build business intelligence, analytics, marketing
operations, customer data platforms, marketing campaigns, and growth teams,
including Quit Genius, Codility, Vortexa, Jobbio, and many others. More
recently, I’ve managed marketing, operations, and analytics for the NTT DATA UK
R&D and New Ventures departments, predominantly focused on scouting, due
diligence, and modeling for 6 figure strategic investments in enterprise
startups.
I'd love to jump on a call and discuss how I might be able to scale your growth!
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn      Skype:
matteisnUK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404) 312-6583

Hi Dan and Kevin,
It was great to chat earlier this week and hear a bit more about what you're
building at GrowCFO. As promised, please find an outline of focus areas, broken
down in broad (and somewhat flexible, especially the latter) phases, linked
here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/11z0KbM3vNm6JtGgrDha4GvFdBYKRQyIs2h8TgxeLyv4/edit
I've also included a draft contract here (which will need to be signed before
commencing work if we will have a pay-after-work-is-performed engagement
structure): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NoR6kI6D-2BJf-4Y3dGk2lvt8SNC-fgQzcOapH-H2n8/edit#
I imagine we may reprioritize some elements as we move into the second phase of
work, at which point I can provide an updated maximum time estimate for phase 2
if necessary. I have also noted in the contract that I will provide time
tracking with each invoice, as well as notify you if it looks like we are on
pace to exceed the maximum estimated 4 days of work in each phase. Please also
note that I've included a 20% discount on my day rate, as I enjoy working with
Alicia and think this will be a fruitful project.
Let me know if you have any thoughts or initial questions, or when might make
sense to have another call to discuss these in more detail and look towards
kicking things off.
Best,
Matthew
