date: 2021-09-21
to: careers@parataxis.io
subject: Research Analyst at Parataxis Capital on Cryptocurrency Jobs
msg_id: 17c071a170211647
---

I saw your post on the cryptocurrenncy jobs board, and would love to chat about
how I might be able to contribute to your team.
I first discovered cryptocurrencies in late 2009, when my roommate at university
read the bitcoin whitepaper and mined 25 BTC in our dorm on his MacBook (I
turned him down on having him show me how as well). I subsequently worked for a
cash in/out platform in 2013, but never got paid and it soured me on the space
for a while. In 2017, I started working on marketing and growth consulting
projects in the blockchain space, including setting up Coinfirm’s SEO and
rebuilding their website, and planning the Radix TGE marketing campaign.
While my early experience in crypto/blockchain was more marketing and growth
oriented, I’ve found myself over time working in more investment focused areas
in the space. In 2018, I helped some friends (ex KLM pilot and founder of what
was once the largest mining company in the Netherlands, and ex Head of EMEA
accounts at SendGrid) set up an investment and advisory company called
TitanBlock, where I served as Head of Operations. This led to me investing in
projects like Sovrin/Verim/Cheqd and ECOMI, and eventually setting up my own
small investment fund. I’ve also helped my friends who founded Eterna Capital
pitch, design, and manage accelerators in mid-6-figure deals for the Algorand
Foundation and the Oasis Foundation, serving myself as Program Director for the
Algorand Europe Accelerator. Our startups in the AEA program have raised more
than $25 million in the 6 months post-program, and I’ve personally invested in 2
of them. Additionally, I have been very active in researching and using on-chain
projects. I have more than 15,000 wallet transactions across Ethereum, Polygon,
Avalanche, xDAI, Fantom, and BSC chains. I also own properties in most of the
main metaverses.
Outside of crypto, I have a great deal of experience setting up and scaling
investment vehicles. For 2 years, I led all data and marketing across
Startupbootcamp as it grew from 8 to 20 accelerator programs. I also served as
Head of Due Diligence for a Florida-based angel syndicate Bridge Angel Investors
that makes A round startup investments. And for the past 18 months, I’ve served
as Analytics Lead for NTT DATA UK’s R+D and New Ventures programs including
managing due diligence strategic investments in enterprise SaaS and data
companies such as Gong, Glassbox, Jacada, Lightico, and Weaviate.
Please find a CV attached, and would love to chat more.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-09-20
to: recruiting@nextequity.com
subject: Job posting on Startup & VC
msg_id: 17c01e9b86a960bf
---

Hi,
I'm currently in London, but am moving back to California in October after
nearly 8 years based here.
I’ve worked in numerous roles expanding the capabilities of organizations to
make investment at scale. At Startupbootcamp Global, I standardized internal
data and managed internal data product development to guide startup recruitment
efforts and 2x the volume of applications (to more than 20k applications per
year) for investment and to accelerator programs, largely driven through an
increase in ecosystem referrals and mentorship events. At NTT DATA, I managed
quant analytics and product-based P+L modelling for 10+ 6 figure strategic
investments into enterprise cloud, SaaS, cybersecurity, and data startups (such
as eXate, Weaviate, Jacada, and Gong. I’ve also designed and managed
accelerators on a consulting basis for Algorand Foundation, Rainmaking
Innovation, Eterna Capital, and others, and have served as a mentor or EiR for
30+ accelerator programs and funds (including Start-up Chile, the Austrian Trade
Commission, Hatch, London Business School, Forward Partners, CyLon, etc)
In addition to experience in more alternative investment models, I also have
extensive experience sourcing, screening, and managing traditional startup
equity investments, both independently and in larger organizations. My own
portfolio has centered on startups that are building infrastructure for or
useful applications with data. Some of my portfolio companies include Cledara
(Techstars) and Yummy (YCombinator), which are seeing very rapid growth. I also
have served as interim Head of Due Diligence for the past few years for a
Florida-based angel syndicate, Bridge Angel Investors, where we invest low
6-figures into A round startups, as well as served as Head of Operations for the
setup of a crypto investment and advisory fund, TitanBlock.
Most of all, I love working with startups. I’ve mentored hundreds of startups in
the past decade (and have a rating of 4.9+ after 150+ mentorship sessions on 
Growthmentor  in the past 3 years). I’ve also worked on analytics and growth
consulting projects for more than 50 early stage companies, including companies
now scaling up such as Quit Genius, Vortexa, Codility, Jobbio, Pento, Heights,
and others.
I'd love to learn more about the role you're hiring for and if I might be a good
fit. Please find attached a CV and linked here my LinkedIn:
https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisn
Best,
Matthew_____________________________

Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

date: 2021-09-14
to: jobs@hyper.com
subject: Chief of Staff position
msg_id: 17be67f80d440910
---

Hi,
I'm looking for my next role in technology, and saw someone in my network post a
link to your job application, and thought it might be aligned with what I'm
looking for.
I've held a number of operations roles in investment firms. I led data and
marketing for Startupbootcamp for 2 years as we expanded from 8 to 20+
accelerator programs, and was responsible for managing 20+ global marketing
staff, 50+ scouts, and driving a nearly 2x increase in applications per program.
I've also consulted setting up founder programs for Rainmaking Innovation,
Algorand, Antler, and many others. I currently serve as the head of due
diligence for Bridge Angel Investors, an A round angel syndicate focused on
startups in Florida, as well am employed full time by NTT DATA leading analytics
for strategic investments in startups and the operations of our enterprise
reselling accelerator program in the UK.
I also have experience investing and supporting founders independently. I have
over 120 sessions on Growthmentor, with a 4.9+ rating, and served as an
EiR/mentor for Google, Finnovista, the Austrian Government, Seedstars, CyLon,
and many other startup programs. I have also been angel investing for the past 4
years, which includes pre-seed investments in Yummy and Cledara, and where I've
already returned principal and am expecting well beyond a 5x overall return
(very conservatively).
However, I'd like to join something rapidly growing where I can make meaningful 
contribution  to an organization larger than myself, and that ideally can
support technology founders at scale. I'd love to talk more about what you're
looking for in the role and if Ii might be a good fit. Please find my CV
attached.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-09-14
to: Duha Al-Sajjad <duha.sajjad@brydg.com>
subject: Re: FW: Brydg audiences
msg_id: 17be5ff2de82bebf
---

See me working through their exercise below - have to say, not very impressed by
the quality of the lists - even the highest quality ones are 90% noise/tiny
individual accounts
Broad US, Real estate, estate * TylerTilleryCRE
 * Savills
 * CBRE UK
 * Property Week
 * Property Management
 * RealShare
 * Site Selection
 * Most results are hyper local


Broad USA, Bitcoin, mortgage - no quality results
FS USA, founders - results all general startup founders or horizontal
funds/investors
UK broad, startups, innovation - mainly VCs and startup founders, none
particularly high target
UK broad, mortgage, finance  * TrackerHub
 * Bridginngbanter
 * Most profiles here seem like direct competitors
   or small brokers as opposed to press, ecosystem, events, influencers, etc. or
   independent brokers who would be good customer targets but not great
   cross-promotion targets


UK broad, financial, chartered * BMInsights
 * Research in Finance
 * InvestmentWeekEvents
 * Seems like most here are general financial and
   wealth advisors


UK broad, property, estate * Agents Giving
 * Agency Express
 * Facilitate Magazine
 * Women Talk RE
 * React News
 * CBRE EMEA
 * CBRE London
 * Expo Real
 * Constructing Excelle
 * Buiilt  EnvironmentNNetworking
 * Proptech UK
 * SolicitorChat
 * Urban Lad  Institute
 * London at MIPIM
 * DiscoverCREtech


UK financial services, startup, innovation * Josh Bell
 * CuLn
 * FinTech Switzerland
 * Upscale
 * Most are too general tech/startups


UK financial services, financial, chartered * Seems like mostly financial
   planners/advisors and investment managers


UK financial services, mortgage, finance * Bridgingbanter
 * AltFinTech
 * Most profiles here seem like direct
   competitors or small brokers as opposed to press, ecosystem, events,
   influencers, etc. or independent brokers who would be good customer targets
   but not great cross-promotion targets


UK financial services, firm, property * C&W UK
 * Proptech UK
 * DiscoverCREtech
 * Seems like most of the relevant ones
   here are in other segments as well


Tech UK feels a bit random - very small property, startup accounts - larger ones
might make more sense_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

date: 2021-09-14
to: jlippencott@a16z.com
subject: Jobs in crypto VC / at a16z
msg_id: 17be516a63ddf044
---

Hi Jane,
Sorry for the cold email - I'm not sure if you remember/recognize the
connection, but you mentored for  the Algorand accelerator that I helped Eterna
design and pitch and that I served as Program Director for.
I saw a few weeks ago you posted a somewhat open offer on LinkedIn to
potentially help/make connections for people looking to break into crypto VC.
And more recently, saw  a posting for a Chief of Staff position at a16z, and
that you had joined there, so hope you don't mind my message.
I'm in the process of moving back to the west coast from London, and ideally
looking for something full time at a bit more high performance of an investment
organization than the CVC, angel syndicate ops, and angel investment I currently
do. I'd also prefer it to be in crypto/blockchain, as I have a fair amount of
experience in the space (marketing/investment ops roles going back to 2013 for
Radix, Coinfirm, Eterna, Algorand, TitanBlock, etc, 15k+ wallet transactions
across 6+ chains, 10+ pre-sale participations, properties in most metaverses,
etc) and am quite bullish on the sector long term.
If a16z or any other funds you know of are hiring for
analyst/portfolio/operations roles, I'd love to potentially talk to them. Or if
you might be willing to chat about what might be best for me to look for, I'd
love to connect. I've attached a resume as well if that's helpful.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-14
to: Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: Re: What I'm looking for
msg_id: 17be464a28a5b6cf
---

Could be open for that (if you’re talking for copernicus, space is
definitely an area I’m very interested), especially if was more of a half
or 3/4 time engagement that could allow me to keep a project or two going
on the side. Options/carry are more attractive than salary for me honestly,
but I do need to make sure i have some cash coming in (probably at least
80K all together per year at the extreme low end)

---
date: 2021-09-14
to: Grant Blaisdell <gb@copernicspace.com>
subject: What I'm looking for
msg_id: 17be2ab5bfff2470
---

Basically I'm looking for something in a high growth/potential, ideally in an
emerging vertical/market (blockchain, data, AR/VR, developer tools, etc), where
I can make significant and meaningful contributions, generally by using data,
analysis, and network to either invest in or scale the growth/marketing of
technology-oriented organizations (probably post-Seed but pre-Series C). I have
a few paragraphs each about my experience for more marketing and more
VC/accelerator oriented roles below, but happy to pull together a single more
unifying paragraph if that'd be ideal. Any suggestions or thoughts (or intros) 
more  than welcome :)

Growth/Marketing:I first discovered cryptocurrencies in late 2009, when my
roommate at university read the bitcoin whitepaper and mined 25 BTC in our dorm
on his MacBook (I turned him down on having him show me how as well). I
subsequently worked managing the marketing for a cash in/out platform in 2013,
but never got paid and it soured me on the space for a while. In 2017, I started
working on marketing and growth consulting projects in the blockchain space,
including setting up Coinfirm's SEO and rebuilding their website, and planning
the marketing for the Radix TGE campaign. In 2020, I helped Eterna Capital pitch
running accelerator programs for Algorand Foundation, and subsequently designed
and served as Program Director for the Algorand Europe Accelerator, where we
generated nearly 200 applications to the program and our projects have raised
more than $25 million in venture funding post program. After that, I was brought
in to plan the commercial operations and marketing launch for the
Algorand-backed NFT platform Aorist, which soft launched in early September, as
well as designed and recruited the Program Director for the Oasis Foundation
accelerator. Less formally, I have more than 15,000 wallet transactions across
Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Fantom, xDai, Algorand, BSC, and other
blockchains.
Outside of crypto, I have a great deal of experience building customer traction
using data driven tactics, and building and managing marketing operations teams
to scale growth. I was the first hire at Hackster.io, where I managed all
marketing, analytics, community, and product on the way to an 8 figure exit to
Avnet. I also was the first marketing hire at Touch Surgery, where I more than
10x’ed user growth and built a 4 person marketing team on the way to a 9 figure
exit to Medtronic. I led marketing for Startupbootcamp and managed a team of 20+
marketers as we grew from 8 to 20 programs, and more than 2’xed applications to
programs to nearly 10,000 startups per year. I’ve also served as a consultant
for a number of scale ups to build business intelligence, analytics, marketing
operations, customer data platforms, marketing campaigns, and growth teams,
including Quit Genius, Codility, Vortexa, Jobbio, and many others. More
recently, I’ve managed marketing, operations, and analytics for the NTT DATA UK
R&D and New Ventures departments, predominantly focused on scouting, due
diligence, and modeling for 6 figure strategic investments in enterprise
startups.



VC/AcceleratorsI first discovered cryptocurrencies in late 2009, when my
roommate at university read the bitcoin whitepaper and mined 25 BTC in our dorm
on his MacBook (I turned him down on having him show me how as well). I
subsequently worked for a cash in/out platform in 2013, but never got paid and
it soured me on the space for a while. In 2017, I started working on marketing
and growth consulting projects in the blockchain space, including setting up
Coinfirm’s SEO and rebuilding their website, and planning the Radix TGE
marketing campaign.
While my early experience in crypto/blockchain was more marketing and growth
oriented, I’ve found myself over time working in more investment focused areas
in the space. In 2018, I helped some friends (ex KLM pilot and founder of what
was once the largest mining company in the Netherlands, and ex Head of EMEA
accounts at SendGrid) set up an investment and advisory company called
TitanBlock, where I served as Head of Operations. This led to me investing in
projects like Sovrin/Verim/Cheqd and ECOMI, and eventually setting up my own
small investment fund. I’ve also helped my friends who founded Eterna Capital
pitch, design, and manage accelerators in mid-6-figure deals for the Algorand
Foundation and the Oasis Foundation, serving myself as Program Director for the
Algorand Europe Accelerator. Our startups in the AEA program have raised more
than $25 million in the 6 months post-program, and I’ve personally invested in 2
of them. Additionally, I have been very active in researching and using on-chain
projects. I have more than 15,000 wallet transactions across Ethereum, Polygon,
Avalanche, xDAI, Fantom, and BSC chains. I also own properties in most of the
main metaverses.
Outside of crypto, I have a great deal of experience setting up and scaling
investment vehicles. For 2 years, I led all data and marketing across
Startupbootcamp as it grew from 8 to 20 accelerator programs. I also served as
Head of Due Diligence for a Florida-based angel syndicate Bridge Angel Investors
that makes A round startup investments. And for the past 18 months, I’ve served
as Analytics Lead for NTT DATA UK’s R+D and New Ventures programs including
managing due diligence strategic investments in enterprise SaaS and data
companies such as Gong, Glassbox, Jacada, Lightico, and Weaviate.

follow up with him and some of his team at NFTGenius, and sounds  like they
might have some projects in their roadmap that might be interesting for me to
get involved in.
Second, I mentioned your podcast and what you do to one of my portfolio
companies, Socialstack.co, and they were keen to connect. They've recently
received grants from Celo, Algorand, and Polygon to build tools to make
community management and engagement easier for creatives using NFTs, crypto,
etc. Let me know if you might be interested in an intro to the founder.
Last, I was trawling crypto twitter today and stumbled on this - I just bought 2
(while doing almost no research), but if you have any funds on Fantom chain, I
imagine it might be up your alley - https://ftmcyberpunks.com/

with, or any of the other potential intros I mentioned were of interest, let me
know!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-09-10
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Feedback from user
msg_id: 17bd0ca7bedc149d
---

Hey Yohann,
So very random, but I did a mentoring session on GrowthMentor with someone who
is using AngelsPartners - I've pasted some of his feedback below that he sent me
after the sessions which I think is useful, and even touches on some areas we
might cover:
There are a couple of things I have found frustrating. Whenever someone looks at
my business profile I get an email that says I should “ping” them. I had to
contact them to find out what a ping was. They said it was just a follow up
email. But when I tried to follow up it wouldn’t let me. So I contacted them
again and they said, “Oh, you only get to contact people in the system once.”
But I still get that email every time. And I don’t know if investors can look at
my profile without an invitation from me? And lastly, the parameters are so
rigid. I can only label my business with 2 categories. But when I do a search
all kinds of people pop up that do not meet my parameters, but might still be
interested. I have to click on the email button to be told that they are not a
good fit. That I am not allowed to contact them. Then why do they even appear?
Very frustrating. It just feels like there are too many limitations. On
AskforFunding(dot)com you can contact any investor in their system, for the same
price. But I do like how the emails can be automated on angelspartners.


Will get the broader outline we talked about together over the weekend/early
next week.
Hope all is well!
Matthew

date: 2021-09-09

subject: Re: Notice of moving out
msg_id: 17bcc2d44ce60cfc
---

Hi Will,
Only because it looks like I'll be traveling the next 6-8 months at least, so
doesn't  make sense to be renting a flat.
Re: Furniture - I'm trying to sell a few big pieces, but honestly don't have
high hopes I'll be able to. It'd be nice to get a couple hundred pounds for the
bed particularly, but ultimately I'd rather leave them in the flat to be used
than pay to have a removal company come and throw them away or resell them.
Re: reference, I'll send over  in the coming week or so.
Re: walk through and viewings, no problem. Could we do the walk through sometime
middle  of next week or later? I'll be out of town this weekend and want to tidy
up a little. For viewings, it'd be helpful if you could give me a day's notice
on them if at all possible (or even try and schedule them for one week in a
block to get them done at once), but understand if that's not possible.
Let me know if there's anything else,
Matthew

for communities to onboard onto their platform.

Matthew

date: 2021-09-08
to: Kristina Tauchmannova <kristina@socialstack.co>, Andrew Berkowitz <andrew@socialstack.co>
subject: Potential intros
msg_id: 17bc42ae76e19b0b
---

Hey Andrew and Kristina,
Sorry it took me forever to get this email to you. Some potential connections
that could be interesting; * Josh Stansfield  - former colleague of mine at MIDI guitar company, has
   worked some pretty major music partnerships (RAC, Kimbra, Skrillex, etc),
   pivoted from marketing to more video production/creative a`few years ago

 * Matt Baggiani - friend based in LA, runs a glitch-hop label (I think on
   hiatus for a few years), used to produce a lot of music (top 40 ghost
   production and touring producer/DJ), runs an ethical makeup ecommerce site
   with his SO, heads up marketing for a music licensing tech company

 * Harry Shotta  - met his manager (formerly worked for The Who) at a VR/AR
   event in London, Harry broke Eminem's guinness record for fastest rapping,
   has a relatively young audience and is very pro-social, generally very
   forward thinking/tech adoptive

 * Asa/KOAN Sound - they're roommates but Asa is the one I'm really friends
   with, also part of a close group of friends with Joker, Culprate, Sorrow -
   helped them with some crowdfunding and web stuff a while ago, they're
   generally very quiet and prefer to focus on their music (and often drop off
   comms for months at a time), but I have a feeling they might hold some
   crypto, and they know they have trouble really engaging with their
   communities, who are quite die hard (apart from KOAN Sound, who are managed
   by OWSLA)

 * Dylan Stone-Miller  - probably my best friend from childhood, 30 under 30 for
   a stem education startup, then learned to code and now working at a YC
   company that does something with phone lines, works on tech for
   social/political engagement in his free time, very tied into political
   activism in Atlanta, I'll be staying with him in Costa Rica in late November

 * Grant Blaisdell  - building a marketplace for space tech (think
   crowdfunding satellites), already doing some stuff with NFTs for satellite
   driven animal conservation, formerly CMO of Coinfirm (Kristina would have
   seen him speak at that WeWork crypto event we both went to near Old Street
   years ago)

 * Jeff Kelley  - runs this podcast - https://edgeofnft.com/ - and a small
   crypto investment fund


We can discuss tomorrow which make sense to prioritize and any others that come
to mind
Best,
Matthew_____________________________

US: +1 (404)312-6583

Hope all is well with you.
I just wanted to send you an email to give you 1 months (well, a little bit more
as a courtesy) notice that I'll be moving out of the flat at 21 Isis House by
EOD 13 October.
As part of that, I just wanted to check: * 1) I have some furniture that's
   generally in quite good condition. Do you want to keep any of it for the flat
   so it's semi-furnished if I can't sell it (which seems unlikely)? Happy to
   send a list, or if not I'll call a removal company

 * 2) i was planning to call a cleaner the day before I move out, but if there's
   one you'd suggest you've used before (or if you'd like to handle that on your
   side), let me know

 * 3) Let me know how you want to handle returning security deposit/etc

 * 4) Is there any possibility we can prorate October rent to subtract the 14th
   and 15th, since I will have moved out by then?

 * 5) It'd be helpful if you could write (or happy to ghostwrite it for you) a
   quick letter saying I paid rent on time and was a responsible tenant for my
   next lease.


Let me know if there's anything else, and thanks for letting me rent the flat!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-09-04
to: Jonathan Brody <jon@ladder.io>
subject: Re: Monzo Analytics Consulting?
msg_id: 17bb22a8c9d3a72a
---

Hi Jonathan,
Sounds good - re a few bullets; * Setup of CDP/Analytics/BI tooling, data
   integration management, implementation of marketing/sales automation, and
   recruitment of growth/analytics teams for corporates and scale ups including
   Siemens Bosch, Codility, Jobbio, Vortexa, Pento, Coinfirm, Radix, etc
 * Analytics Lead for New Ventures/R+D department
   at NTT DATA UK (8k+ employee technology/system integration consultancy, 150k
   employees worldwide), including managing due diligence for partnerships
   with/strategic investments in data/CX/MarTech/AdTech startups (such as
   Segment, Jacada, Gong, Glassbox, eXate, etc) and client advisory for customer
   data/analytics/marketing tech solutions
 * Head of Growth/Digital
   Marketing/Marketing Analytics for scale ups such as Hackster.io (8-fig exit),
   Touch Surgery (9-fig exit), Startupbootcamp (including all
   data/analytics/insight management and overseeing teams of 20+ marketers and
   50+ startup scouts), etc.
 * Researcher at UMichigan Social Psychology
   department and Ross School of Business Behavioural Lab, and the UC Berkeley
   Haas School of Business Behavioural Lab, multiple peer reviewed academic
   behavioural researchpublications with over 1,000+ total citations


Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew

I was talking with a founder who's building a health/wellness/nutrition app that
will look to monetize through corporate HR/benefit budgets. The founder has a
very strong background in healthcare/medtech/finance (as does his wife, who is
very senior in medtech consulting and previously hired me to lead up marketing
at Touch Surgery years ago). They're in a prototyping phase at the moment, but
was  curious if you might be interested in chatting with them (not sure if
you're going marketplace-only from what I see on your website, but thought it
might be interesting given some parallels with Plusguidance).
And either way, would  be great to catch up sometime and hear a bit of what
you're working on. If you happen to be around, I'm in London the  next 6 weeks
or so, before I'll take off on a bit of a nomadding trip for a while.

Matthewe

---
date: 2021-08-26
to: mauricew@westerntech.com
subject: Analyst/Associate Role
msg_id: 17b84b8286042e3e
---

Hi Maurice,
Hope you're doing well, and as always, thanks again for your intro all those
years ago to Idan that led to my first role in tech.
I had actually been planning to reach out to you soon, as I'm in the process of
moving back to the west coast this fall. However, I saw your post on LinkedIn
earlier, and figured I should go ahead now. I was hoping to ask if you might be
able to connect me to any VC funds looking to make some hires around
analysis/portfolio management, or after seeing your post, if I might be a good
fit for what you're looking for at WTI.
I've picked up quite a bit of experience on the more investment side of
technology in the past few years, most significantly through: * Managing due
   diligence and management approval processes for 6 figure strategic
   investments in enterprise startups for NTT DATA UK's R&D/innovation
   department for the past year and a half

 * Serving as Head of Due Diligence for Bridge Angel Investors, a Florida based
   and focused A-round angel syndicate

 * Building the application data systems, portfolio management tools, and
   analytics reporting for Startupbootcamp, which led to a more than 2x increase
   in applications per program, 3x increase in referral applications. I also
   oversaw a team of 20 marketers, new program launches, and ecosystem
   partnerships for 2 years as we grew from 8 to 20 programs.

 * My own independent angel investing, which includes pre-seed investments in
   Yummy (currently in YC), Cledara (about to announce US expansion and 8 figure
   A round), and a number of other companies - have already seen 2-3x multiples
   on 6-figure fund in less than 4 years.

 * Overseeing the pitch, launch, program, and portfolio management for
   accelerators such as Qatar SportsTech, Algorand Europe Accelerator, etc, as
   well as mentoring for 30+ programs (Google Launchpad, CyLon, Start-up Chile,
   Austrian Government, Rainmaking, Finnovista, etc).



I'm hoping to get a bit more experience and learn more about the operations at a
more traditional and high caliber VC fund before potentially looking to raise my
own fund in 5-10 years.
I'd love to chat a bit more, or reconnect when I'm back in SF (looking like
October).
Best,
Matthew

token sale and are ramping up their efforts to onboard communities, and in
parallel are looking to rapidly build out their marketing capabilities.
Hope you two can find a time to connect.
Best,
Matthew

Hi Brian,

the US and to hand off Xero access.
Re: point 2, yes I had meant US  company, not UK.
Re: crypto transactions, please find linked here a spreadsheet with tabs broken
out by each crypto currency, with date, conversion price, debit/credit amount,
and USD/native cryptocurrency balance. I'm not 100% sure how to calculate gains
on a per transaction basis (should I use FIFO or LIFO, etc - it's been a long
time since college intro to accounting). If this is enough information, let me
know, and if not, I any guidance would be helpful if you need something beyond
top level summary gains/losses for the year (unrealized and not) - that's easy
enough for me to calculate, but again, a bit confused on how to calculate per
transaction gains/losses.
Please find attached the signed 8879 form. For the IRS payment portal, 2
questions - do I select "Balance Due" as reason for payment after clicking "Make
Payment", and it doesn't appear there's anywhere for me to upload the 1040 form.
 i also  seem to be getting a "Your Identity could not be verified" when I
entered my SSN, name, and address on the subsequent screen, and I tried both my
Worcester close and Wapping High Street addresses. Does this mean I should send
in the hard copy, or is there something I might be missing here.
Thanks,
Matthew

Hi Sam,

to my US accountant and introduce you two to coordinate handoff as I wind down
Vang Advisory in the UK and transition it to the US - from this point forward, I
think most, if not all revenue should be routed through the US company to reduce
double taxation under the US GILTI regulations.
Let me know if you need anything from me, and when I can expect calendar year
2020 balance sheets/P+Ls for Vang Advisory for my US tax filing (which I need by
beginning of September at the latest, and are significantly delayed from the
date I said I initially needed them in early April).
Also, let me know if we need new terms/additional fees to wrap up Vang Advisory
and subsequently capital, as well as handling any remaining personal filings
until I am no longer a resident here (which will likely be when I get a
healthcare plan figured out).

---
date: 2021-08-18
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Vang x Eterna terms follow up
msg_id: 17b59d071d16a4c6
---

Hey Nassim,
Following up on our call, I just wanted to summarize the basic payment/other
terms from our call Monday before I pull together a light contract.
 1. 5% Alex's salary (which I suggest being paid after each of the two main
    program phases complete)
 2. 6% of any other hires I recruit for 1st year of employment assuming employed
    longer than 6 months (which I don't expect us to have any for the Oasis
    program)
 3. £1,000/day rate for consulting work during the program, agreed upon before
    starting any work (and which I'll likely discount in some cases/depending on
    the work) - the fee for all time up until now will be waived in exchange for
    being listed as a program partner on website/decks/etc. -- we could also
    have this cover future work pitching/developing other programs, in which
    case I'd be willing to drop the rate by 10%
 4. A basic MOU clause that states that if applicable, I will be given equal
    discount/access to investment terms for companies in the cohort
 5. A very basic MOU clause that we will collaborate, look for deeper alignment
    in the future, and not circumvent the other party regarding contacts they
    bring to the table
 6. Eterna will write a reference/recommendation regarding accelerator
    design/setup (happy to ghost write this if it'd be preferable)


Let me know how that sounds or if there's anything you'd like me to adjust. From
there, I can draw up a short contract and send along.
Also, just a heads up that I'm going to ping Yasmine and get her to send me the
most recent version of the AEA  analysis report she was working on. I think it
should only take an hour or two to finalize the copy for me. From there, it
might be good to have Jonathan, who is doing design for Vinapa/Aorist, and did
the design for the SBC report ours is losely  based on, to do a day or 2 of
polishing of this report. Or I can even just make some quick fixes, But I think
it'd be good to get this out the door just to be able to reference, and I think
it's pretty close.
Hope all's going well with your move!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-08-18

got moved on top of ours. Can you do an hour later by chance?

Or if not, let me know if you want an intro to the PPC agency, and I’ll
send a few potential communities along (although there’s nothing that seems
like it’d be a super great fit that I have a strong in to, but can
certainly try and make some intros and see where they lead)

date: 2021-08-16

---

---
date: 2021-08-16
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 17b4e37293909a17
---

Hey Yohann,
Hope all is well with you. Just wanted to touch base and see if you might want
to catch up in the coming weeks or so, partially as I have a few intros that
might be interesting but what to  check what you're focused on, and partially
just to hear where things are with building Angels Partners.
I'm in London, although in isolation for another 8 days or so, but could meet in
person any time after that, or could do a  call in the meantime.
Let me know when might  work for you!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-16
to: Andrew Lecocq <andrewlecocq@gmail.com>
subject: Re: checking in
msg_id: 17b4e3433377a898
---

Hey Andrew,
Good to hear from you, and great to see this coming along.
I took a quick look, and don't have a ton of really actionable feedback, mainly
because it seems you're pre-launch and I imagine most of my ideas would be
somewhat redundant to your roadmap. A few random thoughts though that maybe are
on the more periphery that I think could be useful: * I'd be trying to do
   whatever you can to drive pre-launch signups/leads, maybe by packaging up
   what will become future content/resources into an email (Heights, previously
   Dawn, did a very solid job of this, as did Finimize)
 * If you want an
   intermediate step for your site visually, you might look at Webflow, they
   have some really nice templates for Mobile-first startups like this or these
    - also know a very solid and cost effective brand/graphic/generalist
   designer if you might be interested in talking to him
 * I generally like the idea
   of sort of segmenting activities (and the nod to it in the tagline) based on
   sweat/energy exertion
 * I head Google Analytics +
   Firebase is a very power near-turnkey setup for pretty advanced
   cross-platform user tracking for mobile apps and accompanying websites - also
   highly recommend App Annie and Branch for pretty much all mobile apps
 * If you're looking at a CBT
   approach at all, I could potentially connect you with the founders of Quit
   Genius. Also might be worth connecting with Tim Closs if you haven't, as he
   was working on some algorithmic therapy for post-op otho that might have some
   parallels for a while. Also have a friend who was cofounder of a similar
   product (and looks like you're also going after HR/benefits budgets?) for
   mental health that never really got off the group (more on the product side)


Happy to catch up soon if that'd be helpful, and do let me know if you're
raising pre-seed/seed funding. Give my best to Julia as well :)
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-16
to: Wendi Chapman <wendilchapman@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 17b4da2bcb6395b3
---

Hi Wendi,
Sorry for the delay here - was a bit swamped with meetings, family, and
pre-flight prep before heading back to London over the weekend. And thanks for
the intro to Peter - I have a call scheduled with him tomorrow.
Re: Toolmap, it sounds like they have some solid customer pilots that securing
would have likely be some of the biggest challenges for scaling. My real
question for them is around PMF - ultimately, I don't see construction equipment
as a tool that is probably ordered just in time (and I imagine there is
significant requisition process/paperwork), so I don't get the mobile-first
approach - although maybe during an equipment breakdown this is a big problem.
Also I'm a little unclear on the role structure of the management team/what
Mathieu's long term alignment is. I'm dropping them an email just to mention
wanting to see evidence of support for the mobile approach/inquire if there's a
paired web-app. I'd be happy to take a lead on pulling together DD for them and
wrap that into my follow up with them.
In general could currently probably manage 1 or 2 startups at a time assuming
we're looking at a DD summary every 3-4 weeks or so - so I imagine it
wouldn't cover the full pipeline (although I could also prioritize the rest of
the pipeline based on basic initial info in parallel - getting calls to do
initial vetting with all of them might be a bit trickier).
I might have a bit more flexibility to cover the full pipeline once I move out
of my flat in London/back to the US (mid-October) - and I'm hoping to find 2 or
3 part-time projects to balance after I leave NTT and could certainly see this
being one of them, although I will have to see what exactly emerges as I look a
bit more.
Happy to catch up again or push the process forward with Toolmap towards some
initial information collection to see if deeper DD is merited.
And let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-16
to: robin@koinly.io
subject: Re: Following up from last week
msg_id: 17b4d96d7952a3b1
---

Hi Robin,
Just wanted to follow up one more time on this.
Also, I'm currently facing a massive tax accounting headache as I try and bring
all my finances onshore in the US due to the obscenely predatory GILTI
regulations and foreign earned income in general for US citizens - I basically
need to collect buy prices and calculate gains and losses for 4 cryptocurrency
for with about 300 transactions each, and next year this will be more like 15
cryptocurrencies, fro, Revolut statements. This isn't even looking at the
on-chain/defi accounting I'll have to do when I look to re-incorporate my fund
in the US. Could maybe make for an interesting case study/tutorial.
Let me know if you might want to catch up.
Best,
Matthew

the app mentioning that it is basically unusable and asking for a potential
discount, but due to 0 responses, have lost patience with this. Please cancel my
subscription and/or refund me, or I will be forced to issue a chargeback on
future payments.
Hope y'all can turn this around - I'm a huge fan of the idea of a personal CRM,
but this just doesn't have some of the really basic functionality necessary to
enable those use cases (duplicate/merge workflows, time based views, validated
data fields). Disappointing after 5+ hours of setup/cleaning

---
date: 2021-08-15
to: christian@futuristic.vc
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17b4b9bdce3c541b
---

Hey Christian,
Hope all is well with you and Daria - and sorry for the delay in sending this,
I'd been a bit swamped with family and meetings while I was in the US.
Following up on a few threads from our conversation:
-First, I just wanted to thank you for your time and insight, Coupled with some
of the regulations/taxes coming into play in the US, I've decided to narrow
focus on data infrastructure and applications and drop the defi angle for any
potential external capital I raise. And more generally it was very useful to
hear a bit of the process you went through and thoughts on others who have done
similar
-Second, if any of the intros I mentioned (ZTLment, Cledara, any others I'm
forgetting) would be of interest, just let me know and I'd be happy to try and 
connect you.
-Third, Daria had mentioned she had built a financial model for a fund that you
might be willing to share. If there's any chance you would, it'd be much
appreciated (and no worries if not) - would be good to make sure I'm not missing
anything in planning that would put me in a tight spot down the line

In general, if there are any other types of startups/founders you're looking to
connect with outside of nordics/fintech, just let me know and will be happy to
keep an ear out.
Thanks again! It'd be great to buy you (and Daria) lunch or a drink sometime if
you have some time - I'm in isolation from traveling back to the UK for another
9 days or so, but will be around the following month or two until I start
traveling for a decent stretch.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-14
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Vang Adisory LLC setup and other threads
msg_id: 17b45cef52a0b5a6
---

Hi Brian,
Thanks for this.
1) Regarding GILTI capital gains - it sounds like there is effectively a double
tax on capital gains the same way there is on operating profit in any foreign
companies owned by US citizens re: it being Subject F income?
2) Should I give you access to my Xero account then? I'm concerned that this is
managed by my UK accountancy.- and I will likely have some expenses and final
tax payments to make until the end of this calendar year, and just want to make
sure that it's not double counted anywhere. Should I clarify to them that all
incoming revenue should be processed through my UK company? I know I had to
purchase a premium package of Xero for multi-currency accounting, do I need to
do the same for cross-border? Maybe it'd be good to put you in touch with my UK
accountant, Sam, to talk through how this should happen?
3) Re: crypto - okay - I had gotten summaries of beginning balance/end
balance/gains(losses), but will need to clean it up i imagine for the filing, so
will need another week or two on that.
4) Wasn't able to get the form/check signed/sent before my flight - is it
acceptable to use an electronic signature, and is there a way to make an
online/wire payment directly?
Thanks,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-14
to: Andrea Bonaceto <andrea@eternacapital.com>
subject: Touching base
msg_id: 17b45b59510dfd3e
---

Hi Andrea,
Hope you're doing well and things are coming together to launch Aorist.
I thought it would be good to touch base and maybe have a sort of debrief at
some point. I imagine you're quite focused on getting ready for launch, so no
rush (plus I'm in isolation for 10 days b/c I got my COVID vaccines in 2
different countries, and I'd be nice to meet up in person) - although I do plan
to spend at least 4 or 5 months out of the UK from mid-October or so.
We've known each other probably more than 5 years now, I like you, I enjoy
working with you, Nassim, Vicente (who I have to say, I generally feel like I
have good workflow with and am happy to work with in the future should it be
prudent), etc., and have put quite a bit of time and resource into projects
we've worked on together. All to say, I think it'd be good to catch up at some 
point when you have time.
Let me know if there's anything I can help with.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-08-11
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Re: Terms for Accelerator Projects
msg_id: 17b35927db4c7df6
---

Hi Nassim,

All sounds fine, although I’d suggest some small changes to the terms I had
put forth for Oasis, based on the fact that it would not be the first step
of a more formal partnership, removal of the profit sharing fee, and
removal of guaranteed continuing work through the program:


   - 5% commission on Alex's salary at end of each phase of program
   - 6% commission on any other hires recruited by Matthew at end of each
   phase of program
   - £1000/per day for consulting services before and during the program
   (with about 1.5 days already used in the planning that has gone in thus
   far, aligning Alex, etc)
   - Some assurance of equal access to investment terms


Also in the future, we’ll need to come to terms between us sooner in these
processes. Happy to have an overhead time based agreement in place as we
had done before the AEA program if that makes sense. It also might make
sense to have a “success bonus” for successful proposals. And I’m generally
pretty flexible, so feel free to suggest alternatives.

Let me know thoughts/next steps from your side.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-10
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Client
msg_id: 17b31bf78249f8a9
---

Hi Andrea,

I don’t feel great about where things were left. Certainly, I'm sorry if
the way I raised the concerns I had on the last call was off putting -
however, I think I've been put in a very difficult position from 5 or 6
different angles.

I spoke to Vicente the next day, and apologised for anything unprofessional
or overly harsh, and it really seemed he was more confused by the apology
than anything. Maybe this was him being non-confrontational, but I’m not so
sure - my experience with him is that he's always generally quite direct.

I have to say I quite enjoy working with Vicente - I think he’s very
organised and capable, and frankly had been picking up a bit from him in
terms of better organization and project management. I was maybe slightly
skeptical the first few weeks, but I find my workflow with him is
efficient, and we generally get on well from my feeling.

Regardless, for new onboards, I could see how the maybe panicked way I
raised my concerns to be off putting, and a more detailed discussion should
have been saved for a side bar. And I’m sorry if Pablo was off-put by that
as well. From my side, I was coming from a place of concern that it sounded
like somewhat new requirements were coming into play, and then being
flustered at being put on the spot to explain more about a relatively
technical concept at that moment (which is not my strongest area, and was
unexpected).

However, I certainly didn’t mean to insinuate any blame - and I don’t think
Vicente took it that way. I was really just trying to flag that given some
strategic decisions we had previously made (and I have to say, contrary to
what you've suggested, none of which I've disagreed with once 5-10 minutes
was taken for a conversation /basic alignment around the reasoning behind
the stratgic shifts) would mean we needed to have a deeper discussion
around some capabilities that were much simpler before those changes, and
this needed revisiting.

I did have 2 additional calls diving into this topic a bit more (which I
have no intention of charging time for, as well as the first call with
Vicente), and have provided an task-based outline of potential options for
future analytics/technical marketing work (although have left off anything
around campaign execution for the time being).

The frustrating piece of this to me was that it seems you had a lot of
assumptions about me in our conversation - that I wasn’t looking for or
don’t have time for full time work, that I wasn’t on board with taking a
less data-driven approach in light of launching platforms more
slowly/focusing on Aorist, that Vicente and I haven’t had a good working
relationship, that I wouldn’t be willing to restructure our engagement or
try to find ways to cut down on time on calls/etc to make it more cost
effective (particularly given that in light of strategic shifts, my
compensation was already being reduced due to less potential successful
platform launch fees). And particularly after working relatively closely
with you for the past year or so and spending the past 3 months spending a
significant amount of my time on this, I would hope this would merit more
of a collaborative discussion.

Also, I think you were certainly right in that there has been a a bit of a
lack of communication on two levels - 1) I think it was relatively bad
timing that the week before, Vicente and I had decided to go to cut down to
calls every 2 weeks, as it seems like some significant movements happened
over that time, and 2) I think with you bringing me in to this project and
from what I’ve seen in most recent decks, as the CEO of this, I think 2 or
so months is a long time to go without us having just a quick catch up,
especially in light of some of the changes that were happening. And I
certainly take some responsibility for that for not reaching out to you to
make that happen.

However, it was extremely abrubt from going to vaguely starting to plan a
meeting with Vicente and possibly Pablo in Miami while I was here to being
told I'm out. It was also very weird to be on a call where an all-company
trip was being planned (for the same days I had planned to be in Miami) -
I'm very glad I didn't raise any questions at that point, as it would have
been incredibly embarrassing in hindsight now that I have complete
information. Moreover though, it seems like there wasn't really a point in
me joining that call in the first place, so I am a bit confused why the
news that you wanted me to take a step back was shared after that call on
Monday, as opposed to before it, or ideally on precious Friday when you
initially notified me you wanted to talk and it was clear something was up.

I tell you this because we’ve known each other for 5 years, and I don’t
think we’ve really any clashes outside of some light, productive creative
friction (even not really that much then). I like you, enjoy collaborating
with Nassim and Vicente and the projects you work on, and think we work
well together. While I don’t see myself in a full time marketing role in
the niche of fine arts, I see quite a few ways I can support what you’re
doing in Aorist, and many more for Vinapa as a whole, not to mention our
other threads around Eterna.

While I certainly understand you are under extreme pressure between
strategic shifts, difficulty with KYC/banking/etc, and burning more runway
than planned prior to the launch of a live product (and resulting revenue),
I think this could have been handled better. I hope you appreciate the time
I've spent laying out my perspective on this, and note that it is not done
to lay blame or start an issue - quite the contrary, it is to reestablish
general alignment. I welcome any feedback, response, or general thoughts,
or maybe it makes sense to just grab a drink or lunch when we're back in
the UK (probably well overdue anyways) and talk through this more openly.

To clarify, I am not currently upset about any of the above - but I do
think there’s a potential learning experience on both sides, and that
hopefully it can lead to a better working (and personal) relationship in
the future. Regardless, given our history and potential future working
together, I thought it best to be as transparent as possible from my
perspective to move past this.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-08-08
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Accounting/tax questions
msg_id: 17b279fbd6f6a1b2
---

Hi Brian,

Hope all is well. Have a few questions (one hopefully quick and the rest
probably taking a bit more time to sort).


First, I wanted to check on how cap gains tax on equity portfolio returns
for Vang Capital will currently play out - specifically: one of my
portfolio companies had a liquidity event, and is buying me out for $15k
for shares originally purchased for $6k about a year ago. Are cap gains
into a citizen-owned foreign business taxed differently than operating
profit? If reinvest all of this return into new investments immediately,
would it still be considered a realized profit for tax purposes? Or I be
looking to set up a US company to receive this buyout? The company is
waiting for me to sort this out to make the payment.

Second, I just wanted to double check for the 2020 filing - what do you
need for crypto currency reporting - total gain for each crypto, number of
tokens of each crypto held, anything additional? Max token holdings? Also
for 2020 tax filings, is there anything I should keep track of specifically
for deductions? Rent? Telephone/internet? And how do I make payment for the
2019 filing?

I also wanted to check in on transitioning Vang Advisory to US - what do we
need to start processing invoices/payments/costs/receipts? Is there any
reason to keep the UK company active? I am currently making revenue into
this company, so I imagine it would be good to do this sooner rather than
later. I have US incorporation and banking set up.

And after Vang Advisory, I need to look to incorporate Vang Capital in the
US. Is it still possible/would it make sense to have UK arm for unrealised
gains in crypto investments, and international investments? Is there a
reporting requirement for this company as it holds crypto currency and
participated in DeFi activities?


Let me know if there’s anything else I’m forgetting, or if it would make
sense to hop on a call (or meet in person - I’m in Sarasota another 2-3
days) to figure out how to approach these.

Thanks,

Matthew

date: 2021-08-08
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Terms for Accelerator Projects
msg_id: 17b2671578552c96
---

Hey Nassim,
Good to catch up last week and awesome that the Oasis Accelerator is moving
forward.
Following up on our main loose end from that call, I've laid out below what I
could see making sense in terms of a fee/profit sharing agreement for us running
this and future accelerator programs together.
For the Oasis Program: * 3% commission on Alex's salary at end of each phase of
   program
 * 5% commission on any other hires recruited by Matthew
   at end of each phase of program
 * £5,000 for 10 days of consulting/advisory through
   proposal, setup, and program operations, half at beginning and half at end of
   program
 * 20% of program profit at end of program


For Future Programs: * 35/65 profit split (Vang/Eterna) for future programs,
   45/55 in the case where Vang originates the deal
 * Mutual non-circumvent, non-compete for blockchain
   accelerators/programs (and/or right of first refusal?)
 * Vang will be responsible for program design and delivery,
   team recruitment, startup recruitment, and program operations
 * Eterna will be responsible for deal contracts,
   negotiation, finance, human resources, and legals and will be the contracting
   party with the client
 * Areas such as strategy, client engagement, marketing, etc
   will be shared responsibility


Assuming we come to long term alignment, I think there are some next steps to
get the ball rolling on that front that I can start working on, namely 1)
finishing up the AEA report, 2) polishing up a general info/pitch deck, 3)
creating case studies for the AEA and Oasis programs. I also have a lead that
might be interesting who runs the Adidas family office - they currently have an
incubator but are looking to expand it - still very early discussions but will
look to loop you in if discussions take a blockchain specific turn (which seems
like there’s some high level interest, as well as specific focuses on esports
and fan engagement that could lead us there). Also a portfolio company of mine
building social token infrastructure that might want to do a program for
creators.
There's certainly a fair amount of flexibility, so if you envisioned  something
different or more light-weight, just let me know. And as we've discussed, I'm
certainly willing to reorient for some  of my payment to be in
carry/options/etc, although I think we'll probably have to take that on a case
by case basis for the time being it seems.
Let me know if you have any questions or want to hop on a call in this  week.
And how the call with Alex goes. I'll be back in the UK from late Thursday until
mid-October, so pending restrictive self-isolation requirements, can also meet
up in person (which I think you had said you're back in mid-to-late August until
late Sept I believe?).
Anyways, just let me know what next  steps are from your side, and looking 
forward to running more programs together :)
Best,
Matthew

subject: Following up

Hi Jeff,
It was great to meet last week and learn a bit more about how Ringling students
work with companies and the areas you're looking to build out curriculum.
Following up on a few threads from our conversation:
1) Jeff Kelley - runs a blockchain oriented investment fund, an NFT-focused
podcast, and is based in Sarasota - keen to help
2) Potential NFT speaking series - happy to discuss this in a little more
detail, a few people come to mind who could be interesting (including Jeff
Kelley), but also a friend who works at Dapper Labs/FLOW and runs a podcast,
another who runs a VC and sold a collaboration he did with Sophia the Robot on
Nifty Gateway for mid 6-figures, the guy who did curation for Niftygateway, one
who runs a crypto startup that builds tools for creators, a few other people
like that. And happy to make general intros as well, although some of them are
insanely busy these days with the recent NFT boom.
3) Companies for student projects - I have a few companies I work with that
could be interested in this - do you have any basic information about what these
projects typically work with/examples?
4) I'm working with 2 companies in particular that might be interesting on some
level for your students or if there is a college collection of art so to speak. 
Socialstack  allows creators to create audience incentives, rewards (mainly
NFTs), and other engagement for their communities, and Artsquare  lets art
owners turn their art into tradable shares on an open market (and allows users
to trade these shares like stock) while still maintaining ownership of the art.
These are two of the companies I could also see being interested in student
project work.
Let me know what if  any of the above sounds interesting and I can make intros
or we can have another chat. And if there's anything else I mentioned or you
think of you could use some help with, just let me know!
Best,
Matthew

I'm in the USA for the next week or two, so mornings are a bit rough for me.
around noon on Friday or Monday or 1pm on Wednesday would work well for me, or
if you have any times a bit later in the day, that would be a little easier for
me.
Looking forward to learning more!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-02
to: a.johnson@adifo.net
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17b07873a0216759
---

Hi Allen,
Was great to meet you last week and hear what you’re working on - it sounds very
interesting!
Following up on a few main threads from our conversation:
1) Sports Tech - happy to intro  any of your startups to the Qatar Sportstech
program, or connect you with the managers there - I believe they have a cohort
right now but will have one more ahead of the World Cup. Also happy to intro 
you to the managers of the Startupbootcamp Digital Health Berlin program, or the
Managing Director of the Smart City and Transport program (who is now in an
innovation role at Porsche I believe). The former MD of the Qatar SportsTech
program, Koen Bosma, who is an ex Dutch club footballer, could also be
interesting, although I think he’s more focused on venture building/startup
studio/corporate co-creation projects. 
2) Startup Programs - happy to chat around earlier stage, later stage, or other
more niche startup/innovation programs. I also think I mentioned I have a
friend
 who worked on partnerships for FIFA, EPL clubs, and tech companies, as well as
helped Antler, a network of talent accelerator/launchpad programs launch into
new markets and raise from family offices, who could be useful to loop in on
later conversations. My friend who runs the 10 accelerators for JP Morgan and
the government in the Uk could also be interesting, as could Andy Shannon, the
ex head of Startupbootcamp Global who is now a Venture Partner/advisor to a
number of funds and scale ups. I have plenty of general research, corporate
feasibility studies, proposals, and decks around these topics as well if any
would be of interest.
3) Crypto - happy to also chat about how family offices/other more traditional
investors can get involved in crypto. I actually did a bit of a project around
this for my company focusing on how our banking and telco  clients can
understand, invest in, and develop capabilities around crypto/blockchain.
There’s certainly a broad array of projects in the space (and many of suspect
quality), but happy to talk through concepts like crypto, NFTs/defi/DAOs, crypto
gaming/metaverses, and map how you might approach that with your clients. 
It’d be great to catch up again while I’m in town, or via a call if that’s
easier. Happy to come closer to you if that’d be more convenient as well. Just
let me know what’s ideal from your side.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-08-02
to: Janis Krums <janis.krums@gmail.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17b06bca3931f81c
---

Hi Janis,

Was great to catch up and hear what you’re working on last week. Following up on
a few threads from our conversation, let me know if intros to any of the
following are of potentially of interest:
Socialstack: some friends of mine building a social token infrastructure for
community-driven artists built on ethereum and Celo (and looks like they’ll
likely be getting a decent sized grant from the Algorand Foundation to build on
their chain as well), currently about to start a seed raise for tokens, I
invested in their pre-seed round - https://www.socialstack.co/
AEA cohort: let me know if any of the startups in our Algorand Europe
Accelerator cohort might be interesting to connect with. I’ve already invested
in Kontinuous, and will likely invest in Artsquare as well -
https://www.algorand-europe-accelerator.com/demo-day
Verim: a friend of mine is leading Verim (rebranded from sovrin, and I think
rebranding to cheqd), an SSI solution. They have about $100k left in a pretty
sizeable raise with plans to launch on Cosmos soon (deck attached)
Eterna Fund: some friends on mine run a pretty straight laced crypto fund out of
the UK, and are in the process of their first close on their second fund, would
likely be relatively high minimum ticket - https://www.eternacapital.com/
Cledara: would be a larger minimum ticket, and might be a little too late, but
one of my more promising portfolio companies raising a pretty sizeable A round,
fintech solution for SaaS subscription management - https://www.cledara.com/ 
Andy Shannon: he’s a venture partner for a few funds (I know Notion off hand),
and is an advisor for Stacks - https://www.linkedin.com/in/atshannon1

Happy to continue to share deal flow (and let me know if there are any sectors
that are particularly of interest to you) and if you know of any founders who
will be raising seed or pre seed rounds in blockchain or any data related space,
I’d love to connect with them. It’d also be great to send you the deck for my
fund and connect with anyone who you think might be interesting or helpful when
I’m a little bit farther along with that.
And of course, let me know if there’s any other way I can help or anyone you
think of who might be interesting for me to connect with.
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

Hi Sam,
Figured it's about time we move away from that monster thread.
Just wanted to check in re: Vang Advisory filings, as I need a P+L/balance sheet
from calendar year 2020 for my US personal tax filing.
I just got hit with an extremely surprising $15,000 amount due for my 2019
filing, so am trying to get on top of this as quickly as possible.
I should have word for you re: the Vang Capital cap gains tax then as well (and
hopefully some clearer direction on Vang Capital admin), as well as personal tax
info soon (I have to manually process all my cryptocurrency transactions in
Revolut for my personal US tax filing, so hopefully that should save some time
on your side).
Let me know if there's anything else, as well as actions for starting to wind
down Vang Advisory.
Best,
Matthew

Hi Doug,

Happy to chat. Even for non-engineering roles, finance is a particularly
hard angle to get tech into at the earlier stage, but there are certainly
some roles in more mid-to-late stage and VC that might be of interest (and
even some early stage). My first thought is he should definitely be on
Angellist if he isn’t already. Would be great to hear a bit more about what
he’s looking for, or happy to talk to him as well.

My number is +447523506891 (which also works on FaceTime/WhatsApp, as well
as matteisn@gmail.com on FaceTime). I’m free the next few hours, this
evening, or am pretty flexible Thursday afternoon.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-07-25
to: Andrew Berkowitz <andrew@socialstack.co>
subject: Re: Following up on community intros
msg_id: 17ade41fe52bdd6c
---

Hey man,
Sorry for the delay - just got in to  the US and a bit busy with seeing family
and meetings (a few of which might result in some intros, but still need to
follow up).
A few thoughts going through the deck: * On slide 4 - "the future is" is a bit
   broad/lofty for my tastes, but I'm also much more a quant  than creative.
   Sort of in that vein, I'd love to see some stats/comparisons of how much more
   engaged and profitable incentivized communities are, if there are any
   existing parallel examples (PWYW music releases are similar, but probably not
   enough so, maybe more Toms/Panera Cares? But even those don't get at any
    reward mechanism).
 * There are a lot of slides for
   customers/traction and product - I'd try to merge some, move some to appendix
 * Want to see a bit more of roadmap
   timing
 * Let me know if you want an intro to a
   solid designer to either just polish the deck or if you're thinking about
   doing a branding (not that I particularly think you need a full rebrand, but
   again, quant), although know you're still working on this so don't expect it
   to be polished

Generally looks good though - let me know when you're ready to circulate
something as well :)
Best,
Matthew

Ok. Well it seems to becoming very common that riders are taking pictures
of them delivering meals and stealing them - it’s all over Twitter. It
seems like this is what happened in this case. If you think it is in any
way acceptable to not offer a refund in the future if this happens again, I
am going to continue to not use Uber eats (you can see I have not placed an
order since that day).

I spent hours trying to get this resolved. At this point, more than a
refund is required to make me willing to ever use your product again given
your riders are verbally abusive and your support team doesn’t seem to
think that’s important.

   bathroom), and 7 months (there was some overlap) in my new flat (£1200 per
   month, bedroom, kitchen/living room (again that I work in, probably 10-20%
   smaller proportionally to the apartment than the Tipi Alto room) and a
   bathroom
 * My telephone cost was £240
 * I use Cledara to manage my subscriptions, and have attached a transaction log
   - some are also likely in my receipts folder I shared previously, some may
   get paid by other cards


As per comparing my Xero accounts to 2019 - is the P+L view the best place to
look at this? There's nothing that dramatically jumps out at me as missing, once
we factor in the above items and subscriptions paid through Cledara. Also,
looking at the 2019 report, quite a bit of the marketing and advertising cost is
really subcontractor cost (Alicia Burke, Thiago, Unvanity, Rhys Fisher, Daniel
Johnson).
I'll also follow up with my US accountant re: investment returns, although my
big fish to fry with him at the moment is apparently my US tax liability for
2019 (still trying to catch up on that from delays last year on UK and US sides)
is $15,000, which seems insane so I'm trying to understand why (and to
gauge what the hit for the 2020 filing I'm still trying to get in might be). Is
there any harm in taking receipt of the wire transfer now, or should I wait
until we sort this out?
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-07-18
to: mauro@artsquare.io, francesco@boni.it
subject: Following up from call last week
msg_id: 17ab86a8f5b10c3a
---

Hi Mauro,
Was great to connect with you last week and discuss a little more how I might be
able to help around go to  market and growth.
I've provided below an outline of some areas I think I could help in,
connections that might be interesting (although I'm expecting to think of a few
more while I'm visiting my dad and sister next week given their more current
ties to the fine art world), as well as how I structure more significant
executions to try to and be as startup-friendly to my portfolio companies as
possible (in addition to the general advisory support I expect to provide).
Please find linked
here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1DftHJSKkExKkZOYhx1WOWSA3u9qrH1zjQ-kciEX7aFA/edit?usp=sharing
I'll let you know when I make some additions to this probably middle  of next
week as I think of more contacts. The next step from here is probably to jump on
a call and discuss some of these points in a bit more detail to determine what
might be interesting/highest priority. Let me know if you have any thoughts or
questions in the meantime, and when might make sense to schedule another call.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-07-18
to: Nina R <nina.ricafort@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Looking for marketers for project
msg_id: 17ab77251a42cd8f
---

Hey sorry for the obscene delay
I've actually been in the US for a while, but will be back in August - would
love to grab coffee and catch up then :)
I've been good - focusing on more analytics/data/investment side of things
recently, although still quite oriented around growth and marketing (and even
some pure marketing projects on occasion), and starting to plan my escape from
corporate life at NTT (although I really can't complain too much there).
Probably will start digital nomadding in the fall for a while.
Glad to hear things are going well at thread - what's the new role?
Of course, if you're ever looking for something new ;)

---
date: 2021-07-18
to: Tariq Aris <tariq@motion-app.com>
subject: Re: Catch Up
msg_id: 17ab76ef0e97a4d6
---

Hey Tariq,
Sorry for the long delay on this - I've been ramping up my freelance consulting
and job hunt  as I look to exit my role at NTT, so have been a bit swamped under
. If you know of anyone hiring for interesting roles or projects in
growth/marketing/investing/innovation/analytics, I'd love to chat with them, or
happy to tell you a bit more about what I'm looking for.
I dug up our contract and it looks like we had said £1,000 per month, and I
found invoices for 2 months, so I think it would be £2,000 + VAT. We could cut
it to £1500 if you could pay it in the next two or three months.
Contract linked here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BGbQGXwh-PHzePxRHng1e0LZxGSQzZH0WQ2JByV
BR8c/edit?usp=sharing
I've also been keeping an ear out for marketers, but really haven't heard of
many people I know who are good changing  roles. Although honestly, I'd probably
ask Hannah on this if you haven't (although I imagine you have) - she's quite
good really, and certainly more in the know around mobile marketing than me as
she seems to have carved out a bit of a niche there.
Hope all is well, and would love to grab a coffee or lunch sometime and catch up
(I'm in the US until mid August, but maybe then?).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-07-17
to: Regan Bozman <regan@dovemountain.co>
subject: Re: Following up/potential intros
msg_id: 17ab6e384e1d9551
---

Hi Regan,
Sorry for the delay on this - we went through a pretty significant internal 
restructuring that saw us narrow scope from serial launching 5 NFT platforms to
focusing on 1 oriented around fine art. We're picking up steam ahead of launch
now though and are prioritizing this again.
The Vinapa Group is an organization founded by leaders in the digital space (VC
fund founders, successful artists, career technologists/engineers) who envision
user-focused solutions for sustainable investments in digital assets. Aorist
will be the platform for the most pristinely curated digital art, with cutting
edge technology, and a collector focused user experience unrivalled in the
digital space and at the level with leaders in the traditional art world. We
already have artists with significant name recognition in the NFT and
traditional art space secured to release auctions on the platform when we launch
in September. Currently, we're looking for an ad agency partner to plan and
execute paid digital campaigns in support of our launch and subsequent auctions,
with an initial planned ramp up of test  budget of $10,000 per month in the
first few months (inclusive of agency service).
Hope all is well with you!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-07-17
to: Katherine Erdman <kmerdman@umich.edu>
subject: Saw your facebook post
msg_id: 17ab53d1c82aa02f
---

Hi Kay,
Hope all is well with you! I saw your post on my Facebook wall (I really hardly
ever log in these days) and wanted to say hi!
Also, I thought you should know that I still occasionally stop before I send an
email and look for Porch-Aim-Ladder in it :)  (although this email certainly
doesn't show it)
I've been doing very well - have been in London for the past 7 or so years,
which was a lot of fun, although planning to move back/start traveling quite a
bit more. Work post school has been quite fun for me - I was very close to going
a PhD route largely thanks to Katherine Burson, who is also a large reason why I
didn't switch to an engineering or psych program. But the professors she
connected me with that I wanted to work under told me to take time in the
private sector before they'd consider me, so I went into SF startups for a few
years, and haven't really looked back.
After a year or two there, I moved to London, and I've seemed to be able to
carve out a bit of a niche for myself around growth, analytics, and tech, as
well as set up a small investment fund that's been doing very well and I'll
likely raise a fund with external LPs soon. I did take a day job with a big
corporate tech consultancy doing due diligence on strategic investments in
startups as a bt of a hedge during COVID, but they're nice enough to let me work
on the side and it's quite a light workload so I really can't complain (and I'm
also starting to plan my exit there, as there are some big shakeups/mergers
planned between 100+ sister companies).
Are you still in Michigan/teaching? Would love to catch up sometime, and I'm
actually hoping to come to Ann Arbor in the fall for a week or so, so will let
you know if I do.
Matthew

---
date: 2021-07-17
to: 527fbe3b-cc0e-4e3d-bc04-5fae3a5639e1@mail.smartrecruiters.com
subject: Re: Your Application to Investment Manager, Seed/Series A - Octopus Ventures with Octopus
msg_id: 17ab1edbe5049434
---

1) Depends on stage: * For A round - I was a pre-seed investor in Cledara, which
   has been seeing 30%+ MoM revenue growth for the past year or so, and is
   closing an A round led by a big-name VC to expand to the US - if I had the
   funds, I would follow on, and the founders are extremely talented and
   dedicated (one is a Railsbank cofounder, which just raised quite a large
   round)
 * For Seed - Plutio - mid 6 figure ARR, founder has
   bootstrapped for 6+ years (at my advice during our first meeting), but at
   this point he seems to have PMF and needs capital to really grow (although he
   did just do a one-off lifetime license for a very limited scope package on
   AppSumo and generated £200k+ so probably bought himself a bit of runway). If
   he doesn't raise funding soon, he will likely end up going straight into an A
   round when the time comes, and I introduced him to Atomico and they're keen
   to participate at A round if the founder gets there


2) Anything that allows me to learn or help others learn. I've consistently
turned down "the higher paying job" throughout my career in favour of the one
with more responsibility, growth potential, or where I felt I could simply learn
the most/was surrounding myself with the highest calibre people possible. I've
enjoyed consulting and angel investing, as it allows me to build my pattern
 recognition skills as well as be exposed to a huge variety of industries and
verticals, although I'd ideally like to join and build an organization larger
than myself as I just believe there's more impact and more opportunity to learn
there.

date: 2021-07-15
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 17aac52944acf03f
---

Ah thanks, I thought something like that might be the case.
So I imagine my UK tax liability through Vang Capital would be $15000 (return
payment amount) minus $6000 (principal) = $9000 times whatever the UK corporate
cap gains tax rate is?
In terms of the duality/cross border consideration, my understanding is that
this is for overall annual profit of a business each calendar year, so I'm
hoping immediately reinvesting it will act as a cost on the balance sheet to
negate the revenue. However, it'd be quite easy to loop in my US accountant to
weigh in on this if that would be helpful.
Let me know if there's anything else I should consider.
Best,
Matthew

Hope all is well!

---

Hi Diego,
Sorry for the delay -
I'll go ahead and sign now, but making sure I don't take a hit on cross border
capital gains tax with my accountants, so give me a few days to get wiring
details to you.
Best,
Matthew

and fit).

interested in a partnership/giving a grant respectively were we to look to build

it might be a priority.
