---
date: 2021-07-14
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: cover letter of sorts
msg_id: 17aa658f66cbb80a
---

So I'm intending this for personal  network that knows I bounce around between
stuff. will likely do another 2 versions, one based on each of the main
paragraphs, to focus for  marketing or investment roles so I don't scare off
recruiters. Also overbilling the US angle as I want a role with a US company,
but remote, so I can get US healthcare/salary/tax rate



To whom it may concern,
I am looking for my next role, and am quite agnostic to location (although would 
 like the role to be at least 50% remote ideally). I’m currently based in
London, but in the process of moving back to the US in the fall.
I’m ideally looking for a role in VC or even more innovative CVC, where I can
learn more about leading larger deals as opposed to my existing experience
jumping on to smaller, earlier stage ones. Almost the entirety of my experience
comes from investing at pre seed or seed stages, and while I’ve had some good
returns (20 or so investments including Cledara, BioLucid, Yummy,
cryptocurrencies/DeFi, etc), I know there is much more I can learn. While my
current role at NTT DATA has me managing due diligence for 6-figure strategic
investments in enterprise technology startups, and I served as Head of Due
Diligence for an A round angel syndicate, Bridge Angel Investors, which invests
in tech startups in Florida, and I built the application and portfolio
management data systems for Startupbootcamp’s 50= analysts and 1,000+ portfolio
companies, participating in later rounds is very different than the experience I
have. I believe I would bring a very unique skill set to a fund, given my
background across behavioural research, product analytics, market research,
growth analytics, and due diligence. I spend most of my free time mentoring and
working with founders (for organisations such as Startup Chile, Finnovista,
Google Launchpad, Seedstars, London Business School, and others) simply because
I enjoy it, which given my global reach, could enable the right fund expand
their geographic focus to Europe and emerging markets. I would be very keen to
learn as much as I can in terms of best practices due diligence processes and
managing investments at later stages, and have a number of founders I work with
who would probably make very promising deal flow I can share with the right
fund.
As I recognize just how competitive junior roles in VC are, I’m also looking for
CMO/Head of Marketing/Growth roles in late Seed/Series A/Series B (or maybe
senior marketer/growth/ecosystem/program management/research roles in series C
or D late stage), preferably for a company with products related to data, ML/AI,
developer tools, software, blockchain, marketplaces, deep tech, digital
infrastructure, future of work, social +, consumer fintech, medtech/healthcare,
martech/adtech/customer experience, or similar. I have a great deal of
experience using data and marketing technologies and building teams to generate
initial traction and scale for tech companies. Previously, I have 10x’ed user
growth for Touch Surgery, being the first hire at Hackster.io, and consulting
scale-ups including Vortexa, Codility, Jobbio, Radix, Pento, Quit Genius, and
others to build growth systems, teams, and operations that generated 10s of
millions of dollars in revenue. I’ve also led marketing campaigns for corporates 
 like Siemens Bosch and citizen  M hotels, given talks on marketing and growth
for Google, the Austrian Trade Commission, and the UK government startup support
organization Tech Nation, and managed marketing teams as large as 20 people
spread across 10+ time zones.
I’d love to talk to anyone who might know people hiring for roles similar to the
ones I’ve mentioned above, or who simply might have taken similar paths (or just
have strong opinions about which I should or should not take), or just generally
interesting people, so please do feel free to put them in touch with me -
matthew@matteisn.com
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

subject: Re: Email approval

Just a note that we need to be careful about how much visuals we use - spam
filters use an indicator called image-to-text ratio for filtering emails,
essentially at least  60% of the height of an email should be text vs images

I'll get these to you before the end of the week. Quick responses  is there
should be no advertising cost - also to clarify, what exactly does rental cost
include? Cars, my flat that I use for my office, housing, etc?
I had another more operational question for Vang Capital. One of my portfolio
companies has offered me a buy back (for about $15,000) as they've screwed their
cap table, but have cash on hand. I want to make sure that I handle this in the
most tax effective way possible, as I am planning to immediately reinvest it
into crypto and/or a startup I've had my eye on. Are there any timings around
tax years I should keep in mind/will this be recorded as a profit that the US
would tax as profit from a foreign business owned by a US citizen/or is this
pretty straightforward in terms of not being recorded as profit as long as a
reinvestment transaction goes out in the same period of time thus negating it?
Best,
Matthew

You should check out Firstbase, they just raised a ton of money from
Andreesen Horowitz to do logistics for work supplies (probably only makes
sense for businesses though). I also know there are some interesting groups
of people who will pool money to rent large houses around Europe and
elsewhere for a month or two at a time and use them as coworking spaces,
but maybe an opportunity to offer it as a boutique consulting service -
most companies just send you a laptop and call it a day haha

But yeah my approach will be closer to Airbnb / couchsurfing, which the
latter Im getting a bit old for, but the hope is to find somewhere to buy a
house for when real estate market settles down

So basically there are 2-3 main problems I’m trying to solve - 1 is keeping
my contacts organised and on top of conversations, so very CRM use case
(and just got an invite to Clay so thinking about using that), the 2 is
keeping track of business contacts, both for my investing (so more public
info scraping) as well as client development (so back to a more CRM use
case), and 3 be able to manage/keep track of/make introductions and pull
together lists

I'll be in the US in late July and early August, and then back in the UK until
October when I'll probably digital nomad for a while
So it'd be pretty basic use case - basically recording all my email
communications into a log (ideally with the ability to import or link to
multiple email accounts), attaching new emails to existing contacts, maybe a
basic contact merge functionality (or at least a view that streamlines it), and
maybe enriching with some data from clearbit or linkedin or similar, then just a
few tagging fields for pipeline stages (and maybe views for 2-3 different
pipelines), next action/deadline and other editable fields for basic information
that are typical in a CRM (and maybe a categorization field for personal,
client, investor, investment, and other types of contacts). I'd see it more as a
consolidated and enriched contact database initially, but could see it powering
things like email automation, twitter automation, linkedin automation, etc

I would think of it less as investors and more as stakeholders - anyone who can
potentially go above and beyond helping you source hires, close deals, feedback
on product, and potentially investors or people who can link you with investors
- even particularly high value clients who you dont mind being a bit more
transparent with could go on this list.
The marketing activities sound like the right ideas. Let me know if you need
anyone to manage the paid ads - I know a few agencies and am vetting  some
myself right now to hire for a project I'm working on. Some of the ideas sound a
bit product development or biz dev intensive, so I'd just say try to find some
validation or way to do an MVP approach to things like ecosystems, partnerships,
etc to test the water and see if it's worth scaling in the direction you're
thinking before you put too much resource into it.

Happy to catch up whenever's  useful, just let me know, and best of luck with
the AppSumo launch next week :) Let me know how it goes!

subject: Re: Duha Al-Sajjad shared "Intro to Brydg email draft" with you.
msg_id: 17a81725f14fc579
---

Solid start here - I think everything you need is in there, now it's just a
matter of trimming it down - try and make sentences as concise as possible,
spend less time on how it works and more on how its a benefit to them/improves
their work life - if they don't understand what the email is about and can scan
it to see the main points in about 10-15 seconds, they probably won't keep
reading.

date: 2021-07-07

subject: Re: Fwd: About TechTree
msg_id: 17a80f30d6f2bd47
---

Sent along to Greg, although he's a bit busy with a 1yo daughter and his
project, so may  take a bit to hear back.
In terms of what I'm looking for, very flexible on location (SF certainly
interesting) although somewhat flexible/remote ideal, looking for either head of
growth/marketing in a late seed or A round startup that I believe in, senior
growth or analyst roles in a later stage scale up (would be very picky here -
have talked to some people at Stripe, Seedrs, Revolut - not that I have a strong
interest in FinTech but I like (and use) all their products), or something in
more investment (I hesitate at the idea of going in as an analyst or
associate at a fund, but applied for an analyst role at YC and a fund manager
role at Techstars, applying to a few VCs hiring more senior/cross-functional
hires, even some CVCs/PE firms) and even toying with the idea of trying to raise
a fund (one of my investments is about to announce quite a large A round, and
coupled with crypto returns and 1 or 2 other portfolio companies raising
successful rounds, I may try and ride a narrative about European/emerging market
exposure, analytics capabilities, and stable DeFi returns for undeployed
capital). Would love your thoughts if any of those sound like good/bad ideas.
Although talking to you always does get me thinking about if I could really spin
up the analytics product in my head (I'm just not sure there'd be any real
serviceable market in terms of revenue until I really hit a critical mass and
could power actionable recommendations or data sharing use cases, and scared of
the idea of taking pre-validated investment - maybe I've spent too much time in
Europe though haha).
Congrats on the leave and starting to build! i'll  likely be on the West Coast
in October, so would  love to catch up then.
Hope all is well with you and Stacey and the kids
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-07-07
to: Mark Masterson <m2masterson@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Recommendation
msg_id: 17a80dcc56861a20
---

Hi Mark,
Just bumping this if Thursday between noon and 2pm or 4pm-5pm, Friday after 4pm,
or sometime next week (I'm pretty much wide open Tuesday and Wednesday) might
work for you.
Also, i've  pasted below a draft of a recommendation - please feel free to alter
significantly, as I often find I make them a little bland if I'm writing for
myself :)
Matthew’s analytics and product modelling expertise, coupled with his broad
communication skills, are extremely effective.  As a result of his capabilities, 
 we secured management approval for 6-figure strategic investments in technology
startups over the time he worked for us. He is an essential contributor for our
leadership team’s decision making through the robust, data-driven models and
analytics pipelines he builds to improve business predictability, as well as
better manage our R&D revenue pipeline. Matthew consistently applies his
quantitative skills to rapidly create concise P&Ls by modelling complex deals
involving multi-enterprise licensing and variable software usage. Notably, his
efforts have allowed the UK management team to understand the upside potential
from various partnerships in the clearest financial terms. While working
remotely, he still consistently identified opportunities to leverage his
operational experience to improve organizational workflow and efficiency,
including templating various processes, assets, and reports. Matthew excels at
concisely summarizing critical phases of due diligence and market analysis, and
has the ability to translate his analysis into actionable insight and accurate
forecasts.


Hope all is well!
Matthew

date: 2021-07-07
to: Alex Kay <alex.kay@brydg.com>, duha.sajjad@brydg.com
subject: Segment docs from Greg
msg_id: 17a80c26adc16cfd
---

FYI, these are some documents Greg put together for Segment use cases for us a
while ago - they're scratching the surface for us in terms of functionality (I
think the real value for us comes from automating loan process steps), but a
good idea of what the first month or two after setup might look
like: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1DxCmS9sVMR9TY2Tl5KdSoaatmwWqF3dCA6uIFNGiQEI/edit?usp=sharing
this was the documentation Greg and I made for a Segment project for a virtual
data room company that basically allowed for centralized user data from Google
Analytics (i.e. the website), YouTube (i.e. video watch history), the core
platform (i.e. permission to view data rooms, product usage, account
information), email systems (i.e. ability to track and send notifications), and
ZenDesk (in product support chat), and let us trigger functions in one (i.e.
send an email or respond to a support ticket) from data/usage/conditions in
multiple others (account completion % and if they had viewed a certain YouTube
video).: https://docs.google.com/document/d/148hlc2JWbbN3ZIzty0wug8hFuMCow-B8V89QdLHn9OM/edit#heading=h.pdreh0mq984h
- this was probably about 8-10 days of dev time for him or another solid
backend/full stack dev

date: 2021-07-07
to: cloyd.clark@paideiaschool.org
subject: Long overdue thanks
msg_id: 17a807290d7698ff
---

Hi Clark,
I had hoped to be able to do this in person, but as I don't have family living
in Atlanta currently and am not sure when I'll next be back, I figured I should
send this sooner rather than later.
While this note certainly won't show it, I wanted to thank you for teaching me
how to write. I'm not sure if you remember me from your class (I believe my
Sophomore year, 2006-07 or so? Or maybe it was Senior), but I vividly remember
debating you to try to and raise my grades on pretty much every test/writing
assignment you gave, and that you consistently would push back, but engage in
debate with me. I'm sure I took it a little far - you at one point told me I was
grade grubbing, which in retrospect was probably true, but you also always gave
me constructive feedback on what was missing from my writing, which was
generally that it didn't have a real point to it.
When I got to college, writing was an absolute breeze for me. I could ace pretty
much any writing assignment I put effort into, which a lot of my peers really
struggled with. And beyond that, I could actually develop ideas and arguments,
instead of just regurgitating them. A lot of my early career was built on my
ability to write (articles, academic research, blog posts, etc), which in
retrospect, I largely have you and your class to thank for.
Anyways, everyone always says there are a handful of teachers that make a
significant impact when you're young and learning, and I just wanted to let you
know that even though I'm sure I didn't show it at the time, you were one of
those for me.
Hope all is well with you,
Matthew

date: 2021-07-07
to: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 17a801d2ee463bd5
---

Thanks for the response Kevin, and appreciate you digging up the email, as there
were a couple of details in there I didn't include in my summary (particularly
the point on P60s - I'll look into that or calling HMRC - would this be via the
self-assessment line, or a different one as it's part of automated employer tax
payments?).
From a deadline standpoint, if we have the Vang Advisory filings done by mid
August, I'll be in good shape, as I took a prettylengthy extension. But in
general, considering the complexity of my tax situation, it's very helpful to be
as on top of them as possible (as I'm still trying to get to a place where I can
do a bit of future planning as opposed to retroactive filings).
Makes sense - it looks like I should have quite a good summer for Vang Advisory,
so hopefully I can pay everything up and we can wind it down by early Fall.
It seems like liquidation would be a bit of a hassle - and I assume there's some
negative impacts of what seems to be effectively declaring bankruptcy (I know
much more about how this works in the US than UK)?
One last point is when we've wrapped this up, I do want to revisit Vang Capital
from a strategic level in terms of if I should set up a parallel US company (or
wind down in the UK and set up in the US, or other options). My intuition is
that for access to European startup equity deals (as well as crypto related
investments), it is much more favourable to invest from a UK company, but want
to make sure all my returns above principal won't be double taxed, as well as to
plan for the second fund which will likely include external LPs. I had started a
thread with my US accountant, Brian Leidel and Antonio to figure some of these
cross-border tax implications out, and I think we came to a pretty clear
resolution on Advisory. However, we hadn't really gotten to talking about
Capital's future revenue (let alone the second fund), which Brian had also
offered to set up a call if it might be useful to find a path forward that makes
sense there.

Let me know anything you need from my side,
Best,
Matthew

Hi,
Please find responses below:

1. I don't really have enough information about this position to know the team
size/structure/growth goals, but I've led marketing in organizations ranging in
scale from a solo marketing team/first marketing hire, to hiring up the first
layer of junior marketers in scale ups like Touch Surgery, Jobbio, and Vortexa,
to managing teams of 20+ marketers. In terms of team management, 7 years.
2. Yes, I've managed marketing teams distributed across 15 countries on 5
continents, including managing the setup and launch of new teams and navigating
local regulatory compliance in countries China, Qatar, and Dubai, as well as
general operations teams. I was fluent in French when I was younger, but it is
very rough now.
3. $8000 with equity and benefits, $12000+ without
4. About 11 years, SEO for 25+ sites, social media for 15+ brands, 7 figure
annual ad spends, 10+ product marketing launch campaigns, tens of millions in
both fundraising/crowdfunding and direct sales revenue, etcetc - significantly
more detail can be found on my LinkedIn
profile; https://www.linkedin.com/in/matteisn/
5. I've worked in startups for the majority of my career. I tried corporate
consulting recently but don't find it face paced enough.
6. Yes

Best,
Matthew_____________________________

date: 2021-07-07
to: 09faf190-8573-42c4-99cb-7b62f2f364cf@mail.smartrecruiters.com
subject: Re: Your Job Application with Octopus for ​Principal, First Cheque Fund​
msg_id: 17a7fc0d50a20010
---

Hi Sian,
Thanks for your note. To respond to each:
1. Would need to ensure alignment with the overall LP strategy, but my instinct 
is go  after US fund of funds and successful tech/finance executives who are
envious of the current high European returns, and even growth capital/PE funds.
I have strong links with Western Technology Investment, SOSV, New Enterprise
Associates, CVC Capital Partners, WilliamBlair, Acumen. I would then look to
European FoFs (I've been sharing some deal flow with Atomico, Notion, Oxx, and a
few others), as well as former clients/business partners who have had 9/10
figure exits or raised large rounds recently. The UK government also has capital
ready to deploy through funds via the BBB, who I worked with during the CBILS
program as a consultant for Brydg.com, and I imagine other programs.
2. Assuming this questions means which company would I look to invest in first
within the context of a first check fund and not which of any founder would I
back throughout their career, I would likely say Plutio  - I've watched Leo
build the platform stemming from his own need, and guided him not to take
investment money until he had more revenue to keep more ownership. He's now in
low/mid 6-figure MRR, and while he isn't actively looking to raise funding, a
decent check (larger than the one's I could write as an angel-advisor or he
could secure as a more product-oriented fouder) with more meaningful support
could likely convince him to take outside capital. I also know a 3 time, very
technical founder working on an ML/AI platform for game development QA/testing
(and other use cases) who will leave his senior role at Unity soon to raise.
3. I'll go with the "what energises you" version of this. Learning. Pretty much
every decision I've made for the past 14 years of my life, from doing all my
summer's abroad in high school, to where I went to Uni (a school with big
research budgets and lots of different departments), to my course of study there
(a B.B.A. had the fewest required credits of any degree, allowing me to take
more classes in C++, Hardware Development, Product Design, Psychology Research
Labs, etc), to deciding to go into tech startups in SF instead of pursuing a PhD
post-Uni, to choosing a role running the Global Marketing and Data at
Startupbootcamp instead of a job paying 3x the salary for a generic web-based
learn-to-code platform, has been guided by a desire to learn more. The fact that
I enjoy learning also allows me to work what some might find long hours, because
it doesn't feel like work to me - it's how I'd spend my time anyways.
Let me know what next  steps are.
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583  





On Tue, Jul 6, 2021 at 11:52 AM Sian Cattaneo from Octopus <
notifications@smartrecruiters.com>  wrote:
​​​​​​​​​​Hi Matthew,

Thanks for your job application.

As part of our screening process we are asking all relevant candidates to
respond to the following questions;

Q1. We need to raise the £10m fund before we can deploy it. Who is the first
potential LP you’re going to call, and why will they back to you run First
Cheque Fund? (100 words)

Q2. Which entrepreneur would you back first and why? (100 words)

Q3. What energises you? Or What is your superpower? (100 words)

If you could respond by COB on Friday 9th July that would be much appreciated.

Thanks

Sian

---
date: 2021-07-06
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: ghostwriting recommendation from boss at NTT
msg_id: 17a7c57202474c3f
---

I have to ghostwrite a recommendation for my manager at NTT (who was pushed out
a few months ago so he is no longer at the company, and likely probably feels a
little bad for pulling me in to follow when strategies changed and he was gone
within a year). he's an ex-google exec, so want to make it very
polished/highlight valuable points/not fluffy

Matthew’s analytics and product modelling expertise, coupled with his
communication skills, led us to secure management approval for 6-figure
strategic investments in technology startups over the time he worked for us. He
quickly became an essential contributor to our leadership team’s data-driven
decision making, creating analytics pipelines to help us better predict and
manage our revenue pipeline. Matthew was able to consistently apply his
quantitative skill to a wide range of products in order to rapidly create P&Ls,
modeled on complex multi-enterprise licensing and software usage revenue share
deals. These reports allowed the UK management team to understand the upside
potential in various partnerships in clear financial terms. While in a remote
work setting, he also consistently identified opportunities to leverage his
operational experience to improve organizational workflow and efficiency by
templating processes, assets, and reports. Matthew excels at concisely
summarizing the critical phase of due diligence and market analysis, and has the
ability to translate his analysis into actionable insight and accurate
forecasts.

date: 2021-07-06
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Vang Adisory LLC setup and other threads
msg_id: 17a7c2ae8e6cd563
---

Hi Brian,
Just wanted to compile a few threads here for easier communication:
1) I've completed setup  of Vang Advisory, LLC in Delaware, and have an EIN
number and bank account. Does it make sense to have a call to discuss making
sure my processes for invoices, expenses/invoices, countries receiving payment
from, etc. align with yours? Also FYI, I'm currently using Xero.
2) Do you have a cost estimate of how much it will cost to manage the LLC
filings in. addition  to my personal filings - I can give you historical
transaction volumes/amounts if that might help?
3) Let me know when you can give me an estimate (or confirmation) of my 2019 US
tax when you can so I can plan accordingly/start moving funds
4) It sounds like Antonio has left Shield. As I'm in the process of winding down
Vang Advisory Limited in the UK, I'll likely quickly look to find a new
accountant in the UK (if not structure) for Vang Capital, or even relocate it to
the US if that makes more sense (which would be good to discuss with you).
5) For my 2020 filing, I'm working on getting monthly payslips from my full time
employer in the UK, as they only provide annual ones in the employee portal,
which don't align with the US filing dates. I'll also run all crypto income/loss
calculations on my side. I'll also have 1099s from Robinhood and Acorns in
addition to all of the foreignn accounts I had last year (and I believe one more
foreign account in a savings app called Plum).
Let me know if I'm forgetting anything.
Hope all is well,
Matthew

date: 2021-07-02
to: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 17a6863255080b2f
---

Hi Kevin,
I made a directors loan using my bonus from NTT, and have paid off the balance
through the end of June on Vang Advisory and Vang Capital.
Please let me know how we can proceed with delayed filings for Vang Advisory,
winding Vang Advisory down (frankly I would have liked to explore more options
here, but it just doesn't seem like there is a likely resolution given the US's
extremely predatory policy, and how difficult it has been to get an up-to-date
view on my basic UK business accounts to start to plan how we could navigate tax
in various structures), and when we can pick up threads that have currently
stalled with Antonio and are holding up filings and decisions on my side (I can
pull these together into a single list and send along if that would be
helpful).
I must insist that I hold all further payments until we get these resolved, as
we have been talking about getting on top of this for more than a year now. I do
not like creating a hold-up environment, but at this point this is multiple
times I have been held up on legally mandated filings, despite being assured
this would not continue to happen. And while I do understand the fact it took me
so long to pay my overdue balance may have created some ill feelings, please do
remember that that stemmed from being blindsided by a fee I was told would be a
free service (getting my accounts ported over from my previous accountancy)
nearly a year after the service was provided, and I was willing to pay in full
because you guaranteed we would not repeat these same delays as last year.
Best,
Matthew

---

Hi Pawel and Idan,
I'll keep this quick as I've mentioned you both to each other. I thought you two
would have a very interesting conversation, as Idan is starting to take steps to
start his next (always very technical) venture, and Pawel has been building a
platform around technical recruitment for the past couple of years.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-07-01
to: Idan Beck <idanbeck@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Fwd: About TechTree
msg_id: 17a645cbd96a1c18
---

Will do - he's a really interesting guy, dropped out of college to start a
strategic/crisis/financial comms agency, sold it to Publicis, spent a while
investing and advising, and then jumped back into this. But it sounds like
they're trying to ramp up the technical thought leadership content on the
platform, so I'm sure he'll be very eager to talk to you.
Sound very interesting - my stream of consciousness thoughts: * Like the staged
   development
 * Would be curious
   the economics of an individual deal (how many people-hours go in to  QA
   testing a game before release - how much do you reduce overall cost and what
   % of cost are you taking, how much do you think it would cost to service each
   client, target for how many deals at each stage to progress to the next one
   (is there a critical mass of tests/games/etc that needs to be reached to be
   confident in ML conclusions/driven actions). I see a fair amount of the game
   dev economics down below
 * Sounds sort of
   like fuzztesting  for game development? If at all interesting, we were
   screening a techstars Boston alumn  for NTTDATA  that’s basically enterprise
   fuzz testing called Gaurdara  - happy to connect if at all helpful. Or is it
   more augmentation of human testing i.e. repeating similar processes to ensure
   stability?
 * What are the
   lowest hanging fruit  theoretically in terms of applying QA replay for gaming
   to other sectors?
 * If you haven’t I
   would check out some CX tools like Gong, Jacada, etc. Think you might find
   some interesting applications/use cases
 * For improving
   games industry operating efficiency, is operating margin here a measure of
   efficiency, or go to market success - I imagine these numbers are highly
   dependent on on-market game sales? I know far less about gaming than I do
   music, but my impression is that it’s a similar moonshot/high risk-lopsided
   upside industry, similar to music or venture capital (although maybe that not
   true for highly similar franchise games where there’s an existing audience
   that will play the game as long as it is above a basic level of quality -
   although I imagine similarly to a washed up artist that’s still dependent on
   one of the early franchise releases being a hit)
 * I definitely
   keep thinking of Grandma’s Boy as I read this if you’ve ever seen it
 * Maybe a bit
   niche, but I could see there being a narrative that as more and more digital
   economies emerge in games, devsecops will become increasingly important to
   ensure account security at scale where accounts can contain very valuable
   assets
 * Very interesting
   how near-infitine  potential conditions exist in a gaming environment - never
   thought of the complexity of there not really being fully static states once
   user input starts for basically anything where there’s free movement
 * Would be very
   curious to hear market sizing/company trends in QA outsourcing
 * Way over my head
   technically, but it would seem like a low hanging fruit in cross platform
   games would be to replicate a QA testers  actions across the game on
   different platforms.
 * Could you see
   acquiring a QA house to get input data?

Also, I think I introduced you ages ago to my good friend Greg, and he's working
on a most-of-his-time product with his wife around women's health, but he would
love this - data meets gaming - his main focus early in learning to code was
building games

There's definitely some parallels in terms of how you're planning staged
product/customer segment expansion to an idea I had that was basically 1) give a
really basic automated cross-platform KPI dashboard to startups (i.e. an
automated version of the monthly benchmark we used to run at Incident), 2) add
some reporting and data viewing access management features and provide it as a
tool for VCs/angels to better support their portfolio, 3) add more data sources
to more thoroughly model user journey flows and start category tagging and
aggregating data across companies to look for trends, 4) add computer vision/NLP
to add data about website/product layout, colours, messaging/keywords used, etc
and regress to try to find any trends/tactics that might be dominant for certain
product types/industry verticals/revenue models/etc, potentially even add some
sort of recommendation engine of tactics the company should try or areas of
their user journeys that are showing high user attrition. My approach has
basically been doing consulting project based work to build out the models for
different types of businesses in Phase 1, and starting to work at more
aggregated layers for multi-company portfolio tracking. But I think I told you
about this years ago and you even offered to intro me to some investors, but I'm
just too risk averse to jump off this full time until I see real validation
(which there is some in the Segments, Hulls, Exponeas, Glassboxes  for
enterprise, but frankly I think the much more interest use case which much more
potential for data-based insight is SMEs), which frankly I've haven't really
been doing enough work on it to really have a chance of getting it.
Also just a heads up that I'm starting to look for what's next, and likely in
the US. If you hear of any weird cross functional
investing/innovation/growth/analytics roles, more traditional growth roles in
scale ups, or VCs that are looking for mid-senior level hires or building out 
databasing/modelling/analysis, I'd love to talk to them. Although I don't have
particular urgency, as my job at NTT is still pretty stable, and have  some
solid side projects. I'm also vaguely toying with the idea of launching a fund -
sounds like one of my portfolio startups from the first wave of investments I
made is about to announce a pretty huge A round, so may try and parlay that into
a very small fund if I don't find something ideal

nice email - a stripped down, slightly more high level and transparent version
of this is basically what you'd want to send to stakeholders/potential investors
one other note - there's a metric in email spam detection called image to text
ratio (i.e. the proportion vertically on a page that is made up of images vs
texts, a high proportion is considered spammy) - consider trying to crop images
a little more narrow vertically or adding a bit more copy to bring this down a
little ;)

Random, but any interest in connecting with this founder? He’s been a
mentor of mine for 4 or 5 years, helps startups raise pretty sizeable A
rounds, and I invested in this company - his cofounder Laurence is also a
good friend of mine. basically niche recruitment for devs based on the
tech/problems a company is solving, and showcasing that as employer
branding/developer community building content, with some very strong
referral mechanisms/incentives

I sent him your LinkedIn and he was very keen to connect to get your
feedback on what he’s working on given your technical expertise. He’s also
raising a seed round, which I imagine you could angle yourself as an
advisor/for some carry if you made a few intros that led to anything if you
thought it might be aligned with anyone you know


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Pawel Tomczuk <pawel@mytechtree.io>
Date: Thu, 1 Jul 2021 at 14:21
Subject: About TechTree for Algorand
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hi Matt,

As discussed please see below a short summary about TechTree.

*About TechTree: *

A platform helping companies to access developer audience through 100s of
bloggers, communities & meetups. Companies looking to hire developers,
promote dev tools or educate them can access them through communities and
TechTree would transparently share the revenue with communities and
developers who are active on TechTree, rewarding them for their attention.
Developers on the platform can network, find mentors and participate in
online events with other engineers.

Here's what we're looking to launch in Q4:
https://www.loom.com/share/f80883746cad44788d172ffa7df2f5a8

*Traction:*

- we've been growing MRR 35% month-on-month since September 2020 (from 14k
USD in Sept'20 to over 55k USD in May'21)
- less than 20k GBP monthly burn rate
- our alfa users (meetups, communities, creators, etc.) have already earned
over 80k USD through us
- we've been trusted by top VCs who hosted events with our creators like
Local Globe, Seedcamp, Northzone, EQT Ventures, Kindred any many more. Our
clients include likes of Hopin, Personio, Advent, Peanut, Pennylane,
Accurx, etc.
- we have 70 agreements signed with communities and creators for our Beta
platform launch (October) and an additional 60 on the waiting list - their
total reach is over 500k engineers and approx. 1/3 are coming from diverse
background
- over 1000 engineers signed up on the waitlist and 100-150 people
recommended/applying to jobs every month

*Historical funding:*
- we've bootstrapped and then raised a small angel round from top angels
like ex founders and senior execs of companies like Monzo, Stackoverflow,
Uber Eats, Improbable, NY Times

*Founders:*
- Pawel has sold his previous business to Publicis Groupe. He was also
co-director of Startup Grind London - one of the biggest tech communities
and helped organizations like Google, Amazon and Intel build a reputation
in the dev community through partnerships with bloggers, meetups etc. Spent
last 2 years as a Venture Partner at Antler.
- James has built a peer-to-peer marketplace in fintech and exited to a
trade buyer, he was working with various tech companies as a product &
engineering lead or consultant
- Laurence has been Head of Growth at Jobbio a Balderton backed Series B
recruitment tech business, where he spent over 5 years after starting as
their 7th employee, and before, he was the first employee in an Airbnb-like
marketplace

Best,
Pawel TomczukCo-founder & CEO
+44 7484146499 <+44%201632%20960511>
pawel@mytechtree.io
https://mytechtree.io/
ᐧ

Hi Kevin,

Sorry for the delay on this. Apparently the payment was reverted as Tide
has recently shut down the ability to receive payments in foreign
currencies, and the client is US based. I’m talking to the client and it
should be sorted by early next week, and I’ll pay through the June balance
(can do July as well if it’d be preferable to do them all at once, just
send an invoice).

Probably won’t be able to make it into the office at that point, as I still
will not have had a second vaccine available to me here (and seems likely I
will fly to the US for most of July to get it and see family instead of
waiting around here). But would be great to set up a call to connect and
pick up some threads that were going with Antonio, particularly the Vang
Advisory statements which are currently delaying my US filings.

Another point of note that I emailed to Antonio in the past week or so is
that I need to shut down Vang Advisory once I get a company registered and
bank account set up in the US (which I am in the process of doing), and
check if there was anything I should consider around upcoming tax liability
or existing HMRC payment plans in that regard. It appears the new tax
policies around foreign companies owned by US citizens means I pay a 20%
tax on all profits in foreign businesses, on top of double tax of personal
income above certain levels, and local (UK) taxes. It would also be good to
start planning a transition for Vang Capital for when it starts realising
returns, although I had hoped to keep that entity, or at least a related
entity in the UK to enable some investments that aren’t available to US
companies.

Let me know if there’s anything else,

Matthew

Hi Vicente,
Maybe we should catch up for a call to discuss what you'd like moving forward?
But the outlook for all my investments is more like 4-5 years, hoping for at
least 10x returns, if not significantly higher, before exiting my stake, and
ideally at company acquisition or exit (which I may have mentioned, my dad who
is a partner on the fund is an advisor for CVC Capital Partners and a couple of
other PE funds, previously led M&A for Cox Enterprises, and occasionally helps
companies navigate exit, so potentially a way we could help at later stages).
My shorter term thinking is that the opportunity cost of investing in Yummy was
putting that money into cryptocurrency at the time, so I would frankly consider
a 50% disappointing return. I'd say I would maybe start to consider a $15k-20k
amount to buy back, and would certainly feel much better if I was able to hold
on even a very small stake, as I invest with the idea of high risk and lopsided
upside.
Also still waiting to hear back from Algorand Foundation (their head of
ecosystem is traveling the past few weeks, so not as responsive as usually), but
they have given half million dollar grants to 2 out of 2 companies I introduced
them to (second one just told me this week). However, we should figure out the
above before I make an intro so that I am setting the stage appropriately, and
it would also be helpful to know how immediate your blockchain use case
development realistically is.
Let me know what you think,
Matthew

date: 2021-06-29
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 17a579182a50ece7
---

Okay, any ballpark here would be incredibly helpful for financial planning,
as being taxed on profit in my foreign company is already a huge blindside
to my finances.

For my own reference, is there any documentation or government resources
you can point me to around this change to tax policy for foreign businesses
with US founders? I really can't get over how incredibly predatory it is -
essentially US citizens can no longer be owners in any foreign business (or
else that business takes a 20% hit on their all their profits, effectively
meaning US citizens can't be partners in businesses with foreign founders
and operations unless they force them to incorporate in the US?).
The more I think about this, the more unbelievable it seems - how are US
citizen owners of private businesses with foreign partners and operations
handling this? Forcing their partners to close any arms not based in the
US? Extracting all profits immediately back to the US company? What about
US based venture capital investors who take more than a 10% stake in a
foreign company - is that company now liable for a 20% tax burden to the US
government simply by taking money from a US investor?

On the note about setting up a US company, is this a service you provide,
or is there someone you could point me to who could? Or is it something
that would be simple enough for me to set up on my own?

I'm packed today, but would Wednesday between 2 and 3 work for you? Or any times 
 later in the week?
And sounds good, I'll send something along in the next day or so.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-28
to: lucy.twilley@seedrs.com, david.kirkham@seedrs.com
subject: Re: Updated invitation: Matthew Eisner and David Kirkham @ Tue Jun 29, 2021 4pm - 4:30pm (BST) (matteisn@gmail.com)
msg_id: 17a54e8869c91680
---

Hi David,
Looping you in on this thread as I have not heard back and the call is planned
for tomorrow (Tuesday).
As I indicated last week, this new proposed time does not work for me. Would one
of the following times work for you? * any time after 4:30 PM Tuesday
 * noon to 1:30pm or 2pm to 3pm on Wednesday
 * 1pm to 3pm or after 5pm on Thursday
 * noon to 2pm or after 3pm on Friday


Let me know when might  work for you. As a user both from the fundraising and
investor side on Seedrs, very  keen to learn more about the role :)
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-28
to: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 17a538ebe6229c7e
---

Hi Kevin,
Sorry for the delay on this - I've confirmed with a client I should be
receiving a substantial payment from them in the next 24 hours (US client so
long transfer times) - I should be able to settle this tomorrow. Let me know if
there's an updated balance you'd like me to settle.
Thanks,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-28
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: A few actions
msg_id: 17a53192e80f30e9
---

Hey Antonio,
Hope all is well.
Just wanted to reach out o a couple of fronts:
First, when can I expect the Vang Advisory returns to be completed? It's one of
the only things holding up my US tax filing.
Second, related to my US tax filings, do you know what the best way to get a
summary of my personal income and tax expenses are  for the calendar year 2020?
I know the UK filings are staggered from calendar year, and in the past I've
just tallied up my transfers from Advisory to my Lloyds account and from my
Lloyds account to HMRC, but with a large part of my income coming through NTT
starting April of last year, I'm not sure how to approach the PAYE portion of
this.
Lastly, I need to start the process of winding down Vang Advisory in the UK to
set it up in the US, as there's pretty much only downside to having it here,
although only finalizing the process once the US company and banking is set up.
In the context of some pending payment agreements with HMRC for outstanding tax,
is there any benefit to expediting this process (in that it would eliminate that
liability) or would I still be responsible for that regardless? Is there any
downside to doing so if that is possible?
Thanks,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-28
to: lucy.twilley@seedrs.com
subject: Re: Updated invitation: Matthew Eisner and David Kirkham @ Tue Jun 29, 2021 4pm - 4:30pm (BST) (matteisn@gmail.com)
msg_id: 17a52ee82a1bde55
---

Hi Lucy,

Just wanted to follow up on this -

Would Tuesday after 4:30pm or Thursday from noon to 3pm work? Or feel free
to suggest some alternate times.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-28
to: gilad kahala <gilad.kahala@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Thanks again
msg_id: 17a52c5abf8c197a
---

Hey Gilad,
Sorry for the delay - very hectic period of time for me.
Sounds like an interesting opportunity. You could either structure his equity as
a discount, or give him shares at un-discounted valuation but then have a sweat
equity or performance equity option that either gives him a certain amount of
shares over time based on how much he works or how much value he brings (if he
is raising funds, generating revenue, etc and you can attach to a % of that),
with some vesting and clawback terms. Does that make sense?
Happy to clarify anything in there if it'd be helpful!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Alex Shevelenko <alex@relayto.com>
subject: Re: our pitch (for marketers)
msg_id: 17a4f761a3344635
---

looks very solid to me generally - i'd say the piece that's probably missing
most to me is actually product usage, but the messaging seems right, and think 
the experience is very rich/well augmented :)
let  me know if there's anything else I can help with! Also vaguely starting a
job hunt again if you hear of anything that might be interesting to me around
growth/analytics/VC/accelerators/etc

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Mark Masterson <m2masterson@gmail.com>
subject: Recommendation
msg_id: 17a4f692b306e9a4
---

Hi Mark,
Hope all has been well with you and the family in your move and as you look for
what's next.
It seems like things are likely going to be shaking up pretty significantly in
the coming quarters at NTTD, so I'm putting a little bit more urgency into
finding what's next for me. I was wondering if there's any chance you'd be
willing to write a LinkedIn recommendation for me that I could use as a
reference (and make sure it's okay to potentially give your contact info if I
get to later stages of hiring processes and am asked for reference checks -
although I'd give you a heads up if that was happening).
Let me know if that might be doable, and happy to write up an initial draft if
that would be ideal. And of course, if you know of anything that might be
interesting for me, or just want to catch up, it'd be great to chat sometime :)
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Terese Hougaard <terese@atomico.com>
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 17a4f581b20a3653
---

Great, just introed  you.
A handful of other founders that might be interested in connecting with - let me
know if any catch your eye: * Relayto  - document experience platform, went through Alchemist, Accenture,
   Salesforce, and NatWest accelerators, interesting client list, long-term
   platform and data plays, founder is ex Head of EMEA Sales for SuccessFactors,
   I've invested in this one
 * Yummy  - largest food delivery app in Venezuela but moving into dark
   kitchens/groceries and may make a play as a "super app", founder is ex-head
   of growth from Postmates, I've invested in this one
 * ZTLment - startup from the Algorand Europe Accelerator cohort I was program
   director for, programmable blockchain based payments/invoicing for SMEs,
   going through Danish FCA sandbox
 * Artsquare  - AEA cohort startup, fractionalized and tokenized fine art
   platform, founder is ex-investor at Toucan Ventures, I'll likely invest in
   this
 * Kontinuous  - AEA cohort startup, ability for startups and brands to
   continually raise equity financing through a widget on their website, I've
   invested in this one
 * SocialStack  - some friends of mine are building an infrastructure for
   mission driven creators to launch social tokens, some notable artists are in
   the process of onboarding, I've invested in this one
 * Ledgy  - cap table and equity management for startups
 * Upnext  - content bookmarking, one of my good friends from childhood who is
   an engineering wiz and worked in product for 5+ years at Microsoft is
   founding engineer


L

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Cristina Vila <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Re: Looking for a Demand generation manager/head
msg_id: 17a4f3d316812961
---

Cool, just introd now - and I realized I (or likely autocorrect) accidentally
misspelled her name Teresa, instead of Terese.
And I'm quite flexible this week apart from Tuesday, so whenever :)

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Terese Hougaard <terese@atomico.com>, Cristina Vila Vives <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Terese/Cristina Intro
msg_id: 17a4f3c68144b0e0
---

Hi Terese and Cristina,
I'll keep this short as you've both said you're keen to connect. Terese, please
meet Cristina, founder and CEO of Cledara - Cristina, please meet Terese, who
is.a partner at Atomico.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Mattia Mrvosevic <mattia@eternacapital.com>
subject: Re: Random potential intros
msg_id: 17a4eecbaaa77d25
---

Passed on the info to the socialstack founders.
Re: NTT, our core industry focuses are Telco, Insurance (and to a lesser extent
banking), Automotive and Manufacturing (which is primarily automotive), and
public sector (i.e. government, but includes healthcare). I have Algorand on my
list so to speak, as well as Helium given the IoT focus (one of our recent
strategic investments was into a fleet tracking software startup, which this
seems like a next layer solution). I think our main focus would be on
infrastructure that facilitates enterprise (so Algorand fits this well) or
companies with live projects that might have enterprise use cases in the very
near future that might be interesting for us to help deploy into our clients,
although at the moment we're really just trying to scope what's out there so we
can communicate it and gauge interest with our clients. I've actually been asked
to write an external facing thought leadership article about some topics around
this for NTT, so will likely reach out to you with some specific questions if
you'd be interested in being quoted in it :)  but still very early stages of
that

---
date: 2021-06-27
to: Cristina Vila <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Re: Looking for a Demand generation manager/head
msg_id: 17a4ec4ee31cf2f3
---

Thanks :) I've sent around to a few people, and am doing a bit more interviewing
myself so will keep an ear out. It seems like what I'm finding there are a lot
of very junior and very senior marketers on the market, but less middle
experience.
On a different note, I was speaking to Teresa Hougaard from Atomico  a few weeks
ago, and she sent me a note this morning saying she'd be keen to connect with
you if you'd be interested - just let me know.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-06-25

(or otherwise), I've been doing some work with Algorand recently, and it seems
like a couple of my intros have turned into mid-6-figure grants for companies
 with far less traction than you. Let me know if this is something you're
actively exploring.

date: 2021-06-22
to: mattia@eternacapital.com
subject: Random potential intros
msg_id: 17a33d4e1eb7f1d5
---

Hey Mattia,
Hope all is well! I had mentioned a few points to Nassim a few weeks back when
we were chatting that he mentioned I should ping your way:
1) I'm putting together some resources (which may turn in to  some articles,
etc) and pulling together potential partnership/corporate innovation leads for
my day job at NTTDATA of potential enterprise blockchain projects. Any that
might be interesting to include that Eterna has invested in or otherwise?
2) I invested in the pre-seed round of a company (one of my friends, Kristina
Tauchmannova, is a cofounder) called Socialstack, and are about to start to
raise their seed round. They're positioning themselves as social token
infrastructure for creators, and have some pretty interesting artists they're
working with. Let me know if you might want to connect with them.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-06-21

subject: Algorand/Socialstack connection

Hi Addie,
As mentioned please meet Kristina and Andrew, co-founders of Socialstack. They
were very keen to connect with someone at Algorand, and I thought you might be
able to help point them in the right direction of who might be best to talk to
at their stage.
Kristina and Andrew, please meet Addie, who runs the ecosystem programs for
Algorand foundation. If you can tell her a bit about what you're looking for,
she can help you find the best person to speak to at the foundation or Inc.
I'll let y'all take it from here,
Matthew

date: 2021-06-21
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 17a2ddcbca0e5995
---

Hi Brian,

Is there any reason I shouldn’t just go ahead and set up the company now? I
know quite a few UK residents who use a US company for all of their
freelancing/consulting, and at this point I have very few UK clients. From
my understanding, I am still paying more tax with a UK company than I would
with a US company, even as a UK resident.

Also, could you please let me know a ballpark figure for what the amount
due for my 2019 return will be so I am not completely blindsided by the
amount?

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-12
to: Cristina Vila <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Re: Looking for a Demand generation manager/head
msg_id: 17a0235f6edfaeee
---

Hi Cristina,
What's the salary range so I can think of this by seniority? The ones I know
around B2B SaaS are all a bit more experienced, but can keep an ear out either
way. Feel free to send along a job spec as well.
Also, I wanted to see if you could give me a discount code again for Cledara - I
wasn't going to ask when I thought it was $30/year, but realized it's $30/mo
after the second invoice, which is just a bit too pricey for my use case. Let me
know if that might be doable.
And of course, let me know if there's anything else I can help with :)
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-10
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 179f83aba8ee493c
---

Hi Brian,
Sounds good - I'll put some pressure on the Advisory filings in the next few
weeks, as everything else will be on my side.
The cryptocurrency reporting I have is all that's really available from the bank
I use for it (Revolut). Maybe they've updated their reporting in the past year,
but if not is there anything I can do to make this easier to process?
Good to hear on the 2019 return. Can you give me a ballpark of what the amount
due will be?
For the company incorporation, I would likely be planning to move all of my work
to a US company and shut down the UK consulting company (and would likely look
to do the same with the fund) as I plan to leave the UK. Would it make sense not
to do that if I have any clients at all in the UK/Europe/elsewhere? Does it only
make sense to do that once I've left the UK?
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-09
to: Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Final AEA Invoice
msg_id: 179f2a03e65b199c
---

Hey Nassim,
Hope all is well!
Please find attached a final invoice for the AEA program analysis and end of
program bonus fee. Let me know if you have any questions.
Also, let me know if there's anything I can help with around Graph / Origin (or
whichever the one was that started with an O) / etc.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-09
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 179f255bcdebb0f9
---

Hi Brian,
Just following up on this, when do you need these by to get filings in time so I
can chase Antonio?
Also 2 other questions -
1) Am I correct in thinking we haven't completed the filings (or at least I
haven't made payment for any pending balance) for the 2019 US tax year as well?
2) Should I be starting to incorporate a company in the US for my
advisory/consulting business so it isn't double taxed on profits as it sounds
like it will be in the UK (and beginning to think about how to do this with the
fund when it starts making profit in a year or two)?
Thanks!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-06-05
to: "Jonas Bøgh Larsen" <jonas@pento.io>
subject: Checking in and potential intro
msg_id: 179dc932abb625e4
---

Hey Jonas,

Hope all is well, and congrats on the recent funding round and growth the
past year - seems like quite some success from what I can see on LinkedIn :)

I wanted to reach out as 1) I’m going back into more consulting work and
wanted to see if there might be any analytics/automation/CRO/marketing
operations needs you might have, and 2) I recently ran an accelerator
program and have a company (ZTLment) I’m looking to help out - the founder
is Danish, ex-Deloitte, currently working on programmable payments (on
blockchain, but it’s quite hidden) and going through the sandbox there, and
was looking to connect with investors as he raises his seed round (I’ll
likely put in a bit as well), and though it might be an interesting
connection on a few fronts if you aren’t too swamped.

Let me know if either would be of interest.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-05-27
to: thomas@deep-ecosystems.com
subject: Re: Mentoring DEEP Ecosystem Accelerator
msg_id: 179accb4e5a6d15e
---

Hi Thomas,
Thanks for thinking of me, and happy to mentor
Ideally for me it'd be on Fridays, so let's say the 18th, 25th, 2nd, and 6th,
from say 2pm to 4pm UK time? Or could so more  like noon to 2 if that would be
better for participants time  zones.
For my bio, I'd say: Matthew started his career serving as the first marketing
hire at companies including Touch Surgery and Hackster.io. He then spent 2 years
managing the startup data and marketing teams across Startupbootcamp as they
grew from 8 to 20+ programs. After that, he built a consultancy that provides
growth services to A and B round startups, as well as has launched and managed
multiple accelerator programs (SportsTech Qatar, Algorand Europe Accelerator,
etc). He also runs an investment fund that has made 15 or so startup
investments, has mentored for 30+ accelerator programs (Google Launchpad, Tech
Nation, Austrian Trade Commission, Seedstars, etc), and manages due diligence
 and quant modelling for NTT DATA's startup partnership and strategic investment
programs in the UK.
Image attached.
Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-25
to: Regan Bozman <regan@dovemountain.co>, kristina@socialstack.co
subject: SocialStack/Dove Mountain Intro
msg_id: 179a3acc37ef2ae3
---

Hi Kristina,
Please meet Regan, from Dove Mountain. He formerly worked at Coinlist, and runs
a marketing and growth agency for blockchain companies based out of the western
US.
Regan, please meet a good friend of mine, Kristina, who has a long track record
of ops in tech companies and blockchain. Her and her cofounders recently
launched SocialStack, which I invested a bit in, and were looking for marketers
who might be able to help them gain traction.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-25
to: Victoria Entwistle <victoria@magnoliaadvisory.com>
subject: Re: Introducing Victoria and Matthew
msg_id: 179a3a0aa1ffc10b
---

Hi Victoria,

Good to hear from you!

I’ve been well on my side. Have been juggling quite a few projects
(managing my fund, running a blockchain accelerator, launching a digital
art marketplace, some SEO work, some startup advisory), on top of a full
time role managing due diligence and investment modelling for NTT DATA’s UK
R+D team (basically startup partnerships). So quite busy, but lockdown has
given me loads of time.

I have a close former colleague of mine, Nikita Thakrar, who used to work
with Imperial startups and very much enjoyed it - although not sure if
that’s the same program. Would love to hear more and how things are with
you :)

How’s sometime Thursday or Friday afternoon for you?

Best,

Matthew


On Tue, 25 May 2021 at 13:40, Victoria Entwistle <
victoria@magnoliaadvisory.com> wrote:

date: 2021-05-24

Hey Alex,
Find attached a doc with some ideas for what I think it might make sense for
Duha to focus on.
Let me know what you think/if there's anything I should shuffle or add, and I
can chat with her about it on my call with her tomorrow (before our all
marketing call). Or how you generally envision managing her time if you had
something else in mind.
I gave her the to do  of backlogging 5 or so loan posts, so hopefully she's made
some decent headway on that, but either way hopefully this doc can set some
clearer goals she can align to.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-05-21
to: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 1798ce6bfdba7408
---

Hi Kevin,
Just a heads up - I see you mention May 21 as the date you'd like to
receive payment. I'm very tight on cash right now and have a direct debit to
HMRC on the 23rd, but also about £6,000 in pending invoices I'm expecting to be
paid in the next week or so. Once one of those come  in, I should be able to
clear this payment.
I assume that there is no other unpaid work apart from the £900 you mention, and
I can count on future overtime costs to be communicated ahead of time from now
on.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-20
to: Connie Wright <connie.wright@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Vang Advisory Ltd - Correspondence
msg_id: 1798a9e2687466b4
---

Thanks Connie,

I’ve set up a VAT payment plan, and confirmed that the corporation tax
items you referenced are included in the payment plan I set up in March or
so.

Let me know if there are any pending balance I’m missing you can see or
other correspondence.

Best,

Matthew

connie.wright@shieldaccountancy.co.uk> wrote:

---
date: 2021-05-19
to: William Bainborough <william@doordeck.com>
subject: Re: Parking tickets
msg_id: 17985fc13c9271d8
---

Hi Will,

Ok, I will try to convince her not to pay, but likely won’t be able to so
will come out of my pocket. More generally, I feel this is inappropriate
for me and my girlfriend to be being used as leverage in what appears to be
a dispute between you and the property managers, caused by us following
your instructions related to parking. I have not heard an update on this
except for receiving a parking pass despite waiting patiently with
continued legal threats are made against us.

This is an extremely uncomfortable stressful and situation I would like to
resolve. I’d be happy to follow up directly with the property management
company - maybe having the person who they are actually threatening giving
them an earful will get them to stop making threats.

Best,

Matthew





On Wed, 19 May 2021 at 19:26, William Bainborough <william@doordeck.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2021-05-19
to: William Bainborough <william@doordeck.com>
subject: Parking tickets
msg_id: 17985da11e216119
---

Hi Will,

My girlfriend received two more fees/fines related to parking notices (not
new ones, just the previous ones). She is not willing to risk her credit
score waiting for these to be contested while she is being threatened
legally, so will be paying them in the coming day or two (and I don’t blame
her, as we’re nearly 5 months from when these communications started). Can
we deduct these from next months rent?

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-19
to: Kilian Ekamp <kilian@hey.com>
subject: Re: GrowthMentor Call
msg_id: 17985d234790fefe
---

Hi Kilian,

Terribly sorry - I’ve had a huge headache today so haven’t been able to
look at a screen. Any chance we could do sometime early next week? Again,
very sorry for not being able to give you a heads up ahead of time.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-19
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <Antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 17984f2435475760
---

Thanks Antonio. To clarify, when you say “not taxed as an individual in the
UK”, I’m assuming that’s not in reference to my personal tax liability as a
resident/for profits and dividends from my company/for my employment at NTT
but to of any tax liability to my companies as their owner?


Brian, to elaborate on/clarify a few points:

The idea of setting up a company in the US to receive payments for projects
would be so that those are only taxed at the US corporate tax rate (as
opposed to Uk revenue, which profits from would be taxed 19% UK corporate
tax and US corporate tax). My thought is it would effectively make the US a
revenue center. Or does it just make more sense to relocate the company
completely to the US at that point even?

The longer term question about raising a fund was more around the point of:
were I to raise an investment fund from external investors in the future,
it sounds like were it in the UK, I would be required to pay UK + US tax on
any profits before paying out to investors. This would likely make it very
difficult to secure investors having to outperform the double tax rate to
see the same returns (even if they are not US citizens and are limited
partners in the entity). Or would another option be to hold my ownership in
that company through another company/a US company, that would only be paid
out profit at the end of/in the last years of the fund?

How does reducing my liability with foreign tax credits work? Does this
essentially mean a deduction for tax paid in the UK? I imagine it’s not a
100% deduction?


Best,

Matthew



On Wed, 19 May 2021 at 14:38, Antonio Kapsamunov <
Antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk> wrote:

---
date: 2021-05-18
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 17981e7d2cd89bc0
---

Hi Brain,

Thanks for this.

To clarify, based on points 1, it does sound like it would be optimal for
me to set up a US company for my consulting work and route as much of my
business as possible through it (i.e. all non-UK clients) so that it is
only taxed by the US government (as opposed to the UK company which would
be taxed by both the US and UK), although I’m not sure I fully understand.
Is this correct?

It sounds like this would also be the case for the fund for any deals I
possibly can (I.e. anything where being a US entity doesn’t make the
investment process significantly more difficult)? It also sounds like this
would have major implications were I to look to set up an investment fund
with external limited partners (although that’s a secondary question at the
moment)?

@Antonio, any thoughts on how this would affect things on the UK side? Or
worth jumping on a call around? I’m guessing the UK would not tax profits
held in a US entity by a non-citizen tax resident?

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-17
to: andrey@zerion.io, Vadim Koleoshkin <vadim@zerion.io>
subject: Following up from call
msg_id: 1797c4cca4ea2edb
---

Hey Andrey and Vadim,

Was great to chat with you earlier and hear a bit more about Zerion and what
you're looking for in the role - I very much enjoyed our conversation, and am
keen to continue them.
Following up on a few points:
Writing Samples * Startupbootcamp analysis - I sent this to you while we were on
   the call, but just in case. it's was built out of the internal
   recruitment/portfolio performance reports that were a bit controversial at
   times, but after 6-12 months of running them and aggregating data - here
 * Incident gTar content - I produced these videos ages ago, the
   written form content has all been taken down (as the company went bust,
   relaunched under a different brand, and then went bust again), as well as all
   customer support logistics and comms (which was a nightmare given hardware
   delays and instability) - here  and here
 * Accelerator playbook - a whole writeup I did on structuring
   and running accelerator programs - here
   
 * My website blogs (template is broken so please don’t judge web
   tech) - here
 * My company websites - I mentioned I had been playing with webflow for a few
   sites - here  and here
 * TheFreshNew Facebook page - this was a blog I built with a
   friend and ran in Uni (website is down but you can get a feel for what we
   were doing on FB) - here
 * I’ve also built out a huge set of templated emails for
   basically managing the full lifecycle of startup, mentor, and event
   communications around an accelerator program (probably about 25 emails in
   total). I can share a few of these if they'd be of interest
 * I also manage all due diligence, internal model creation, and
   information deck production (i.e. a fair amount of writing on this point) to
   get management signoff of and organizational buy in into early stage
   technology partnerships for NTT DATA UK to strategically invest in (Glassbox,
   eXate, Weaviate, Lightico to name a few). These are sensitive materials that
   I unfortunately can’t share

Ideas * Drive more people to app (ASO (Me) - paid mobile (my friend Hannah
   Parvas is very good at this and consults)) - I imagine app download is a
   major aha point, but there’s no CTA for download on the core page of the site
 * Drive more interaction - I personally almost never interact with the
   Zerion UI except to occasionally check an individual token price, change the
   date filter (which adding a custom date input could also make this incredibly
   useful for accounting/tax purposes), or generally refresh it. I do think
   there’s a gap between more top of funnel tools for research that do a good
   job driving interaction but are often very limited in monetisation/narrow in
   view (dextools, coinmarketcap, coingecko) and tools that provide context of a
   users wider portfolio/actually can execute trading
 * Validation metrics - social proof of platform usage would be good.
   Number of wallets connected, number of people trading using platform per day,
   number of trades, amount transacted through the platform, etc would be useful
   to show people they CAN do that, as well as validate its safe
 * Platform gamification/contests/airdrop partnerships/etc
 * Marketing automation based on platform usage (notifications/ alerts/
   subscriptions/ watchlists/ etc)
   
 * Product Marketing Analytics
 * Rolling out new features (goal metrics for launch) - I think it's
   important to get in a habit of trying to predict a few metrics and set a few
   goals where we'd say "this is a good validation to roll out this feature". We
   may still roll out features anyways, but regardless it's good to start a
   feedback cycle here so we can get better at estimating input/output
   relationships for these types of releasesQualitative vs quantitative analysis
   (low hanging fruit/quick feedback vs statistically valid data driven
   insight). My approach is usually  start  with qual (easier to ask more
   questions at once, faster feedback cycles) and work way out to quant once
   questions have been narrowed a bit to confirm conclusions with stats that
   take a while to collect. To priotize quant tests, I usually use a vary  basic
   importance using basic prioritisation framework
 * Content
 * Cross-promotion centric partnerships (see above bullet) - I imagine
   there will be huge swatches of users that will onboard  if we can get
   Polygon, BSC, Fantom, Avax, etc to promote integration releases. This could
   be very easily turned into a turn-key process and scaled/streamlined (within
   reason to ensure legitimacy of integrations)Industry analysis/reports/data
   pages/etc - I really enjoy this type of data, as I get to learn while I play
   with it, it can provide very useful insight, it’s easy to tie in partners to
   help with data collection/production/distribution, and once it’s gone through
   a few cycles of iteration it’s very easy to set up as a data pipeline and
   turn into a live-updating resource (which can then create stronger pulls for
   data collection/drive for developers to plan for/adhere to our requirements
   for integration)


Questions: * How will you compete against Zapper? How is the
   vision/mission/team/approach different?
 * Any competitors apart from Zapper and Dappradar’s crappy portfolio
   tracker?
 * Any plan to build  NFT value tracking or partner with or integrate
   a platform that does?
 * I imagine you’re currently taking a small % of tx’s routed through
   the platform? Are there plans for new revenue steams  soon?
 * Is there an aim to introduce an API or monetise platform data?
 * Is there a token? When is it slated for launch?
 * Are there any goals/values/driving missions that align the company?
 * How many people is the team? What do you expect it to be by the end
   of the year? Basic department breakdown of headcount?
 * What does the current martech and analytics stack look like? How
   integrated is the data?
 * What user data is currently being collected? Is there any
   additional data at top of mind to collect? Or is it just what’s available on
   the blockchain/via wallet tx’s?
 * Any partners/partnerships in the works we can leverage for
   marketing?
 * How much funding at what valuation from who?
 * Marketing budgets and ad spends
 * What other types of agencies besides SEO are you using for
   marketing/commercial activities?
 * What does the product release roadmap look like? Any major upcoming
   launches?
 * Information about employee option terms


I imagine some of these points may be easier to go through on a call, so no rush
- but these are generally the types of things I'll hope to learn to figure out
if we're an ideal long term fit.
Let me know what next steps are, and have a good evening!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-17
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>, Antonio Kapsamunov <antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Looping together for US/UK cross border tax considerations
msg_id: 17979286d0e8255e
---

Hi Antonio and Brian,
Please meet each other - you each manage my taxes in respective countries (UK
and US). I'd mentioned I wanted to start a conversation around some changes to
US tax law, as well as generally  considerations I should take into account as I
start to reach the US double tax threshold. I've included them below, if either
of you have any initial thoughts so we can get the ball rolling, that'd be
great. Or if it would be better to set up a call, happy to do so.
Best,
Matthew



-It sounds like the US is taxing profits made by and held in any foreign
companies owned by individuals. Is this correct? Is this also true for foreign
held funds?
-If so, should I be looking to minimise the amount of profits I am holding in my
UK consulting company by either withdrawing to one of either my personal UK
accounts or personal US accounts? Should I set up a second consulting business
in the US to hold my profits in? Or even completely relocate it (the tax rate is
considerably lower now than when I initially set up, and I have more business
there than I expected, but I don't have a strong opinion)?
-In the context of the fund I am partnered with my dad on, there are some
benefits of being able to participate in investments that don’t want to deal
with the headache of US entities by having the fund in the UK. Currently, the
fund has not made a realized profit, but it will likely start to make some this
year, and probably significant amounts next year and the following as
investments mature. Is there anything I should be thinking about in the context
of this? Are there levels of profit withdrawn from the company that tax burden
will increase at, meaning I should look to reinvest profits instead and plan to
withdraw a certain amount per year? Are there any considerations I should take
into account (apart from normal financial regulation around investment
management) were I to look to set up a fund with external limited partners in
the future?
-I will likely be above the earned income exclusion tax threshold. Is there
anything I can do to reduce the amount of double tax I pay? 
-In the UK, there are a couple of tax relief programs for early stage high risk
startup investments (EIS and SEIS) that reduce tax liability by a portion of the
investment made into qualifying companies. Would making investments and
receiving relief through these programs mean that I simply pay back the tax to
the US? What about for programs like ISAs?

---
date: 2021-05-17
to: Alex Novosad <alextheukrainian@gmail.com>, Trevor Owens <trevor@leanstartupmachine.com>
subject: Following up on Stacks Accelerator
msg_id: 1797837a84bf4f3b
---

Hi Alex and Trevor,
Hope all is well. Just following up - I had a conversation with the partners at
Eterna Capital, who are willing to waive my non-compete.
However, they also mentioned (and I'll potentially help them depending on what
else I find in terms of jobs/projects in the coming months) that if you're
interested, they'd be happy to speak about running the first accelerator so you
can hit the ground running, while hiring out a long term team and building more
foundational process in parallel. It sounds like they're likely be  doing this
for 2-3 other top 100 market cap blockchains following the success of the
Algorand program.
Let me know if either of these might be of interest to continue or conversation
on.
Best,
Matthew
