---
date: 2021-05-16
to: Tariq Aris <tariq@motion-app.com>
subject: Re: Catch Up
msg_id: 179741c1499f33e2
---

Hey Tariq,

Sorry for the delay, a bit hectic but all good on my end. Found a bit of a
niche for my analytics/automation work with post-A round startups working
in through their VCs, as well as a lot of random projects, set up a small
fund, etc. More recently working full time for a big consultancy, but they
let me work on the side. How’s everything with you?

I’m actually hiring up a bunch of marketers and ops staff for a project I’m
working on, so if I find any that are more oriented towards fitness/healthy
living (I’m looking for more art/media/entertainment backgrounds), I’ll
send them your way.

Also, a bit random, but did Drinki (well, since rebranded to Dusk I think I
saw) raise funding? If I’m not mistaken, we had a clause in our contract
where we deferred a decent portion of the payment to that event. Let me
know!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-14
to: Regan Bozman <regan@dovemountain.co>
subject: Following up/potential intros
msg_id: 1796c444a8385782
---

Hi Regan,

Was great to catch up with you the other week and learn a bit more about
what you're working on. I had a few points I wanted to follow up on and see
if any might be of interest.

1. I have some friends who just launched a social coin platform earlier
this week, and are going into a fundraise and creator acquisition phase, as
well as looking to bring on some marketing team. They're more looking for a
CMO currently, but are very early in that process, and would be interested
in connecting with marketing/growth agencies in the interim if that's
interesting to you: https://socialstack.co/

2. A former employee of mine who has started his own agency looped me in on
a call with Polygon/Matic earlier this week where they were looking very
specifically for a PPC/paid marketing agency - which isn't really something
I do, and something he only does for non-crypto companies, so we're
basically passing on the RFP process. Not sure if that's too niche for you,
but let me know and I can send your intro to my contact.

3. I have a big project on right now that should be very interesting around
an NFT marketplace - backed by a major carbon-negative layer 1, big-name
founders/artists/brands/funding already committed pre launch (it will
actually be a serial launch of 5 or so different platforms over the next 6
months), etc. I'm hiring a bunch of internal ops and marketing people over
the coming weeks (which if you have any recommendations, would love to
speak to them), but we are in the process of looking for a PR agency to
handle our fundraising announcement - not sure if that's something you do
or you have an agency you'd recommend.

And of course, please let me know if there's anything I can help with :)

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-05-14
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>, andrea@vinapagroup.com
subject: Jonathan/Andrea Intro
msg_id: 1796c22497cd06c0
---

Hey Jonathan,
I've mentioned a bit about the NFT marketplace project to you and that we'd
likely want to get you involved for some design work. To that end, please meet
Andrea, who is one of the cofounders (and an artist, serial entrepreneur, and
fund manager).
Andrea, please meet Jonathan, who has done great work for me across a number of
projects (Startupbootcamp, my personal brand - and he also is the designer for
Radix).
I'll let you two find a time in the coming week or so to connect!
Best,
Matthew

This is very confusing. I already have a payment plan set up with HMRC that

come from? This sounds way way too high.

Matthew

Hey Nina,

Hope all is well with you! Are you enjoying Thread still? Still in London?

I was wondering if you might be/know any talented marketers  who are
looking for something new? Some friends of mine are trying to hire a bunch
marketers for a super well funded startup that’s going to be doing a ton of
product launches around art/music/entertainment with some huge names on
board this summer - I’ll likely be pretty involved and may even join up
full time.

Happy to tell you more, or would be great to catch up and hear more about
what you’re up to in general :)

Best,

Matthew

Hi Brian,

Very sorry for the delay on these - giving quite an earful about meeting
basic deadlines to my UK accountants. Will also loop you in on a thread
with Antonio to align on some multi-country operations/tax questions.

Please find attached filings for Vang Capital (renamed from Eisner
Capital). I’ll follow up with additional information on my own finances,
but I know you needed this for my dads and the deadline is tighter. Let me
know if you need anything else.

Best,

Matthew

Antonio

date: 2021-05-05

subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 1793d63ab44da916
---

Please let me know when I can expect these. I am under significant pressure
from my dad now to get these in, which is a really shitty position to be
in.

The most concerning part of all of this is that these filings don’t even
appear to have been on anyone’s radar until a week or two ago, despite me
raising pretty constantly that we need them. I’m really not sure how to
proceed at this point. This is really not in the spirit of our most recent
set of conversations that were spurred from this same issue last year.

Matthew


On Tue, 4 May 2021 at 14:53, Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
wrote:

Hi Antonio,

Anything I can do to help get the Vang Capital info ready?

on-chain portfolio value is zero by only including the crypto in exchanges
on my calculation. Does it make sense to declare a general estimate on
chain unrealized gains?

Feel free to give me a ring if there’s anything I can do,

Matthew

date: 2021-05-03
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: sitemap
msg_id: 17934a485a65fba5
---

Hi Victoria,
Sorry for the delay - bit of a hectic week last week with the accelerator
program I was running's demo  day on Thursday.
I took a scan through the site map and looks  like everything's populated
nicely :) - Google can often take a bit of time to read everything when there
are major restructurings. I did notice there were a few pages with noindex, but
I imagine these were mostly client portal pages, thank you pages, etc.
I also wanted to ask if you might be willing to write me a reference/mini case
study? I don't really have a framework for this (certainly open to
suggestions!), but happy to partially ghostwrite it or pose some questions if
either of those might make it easier. Just let me know!
I'll also follow up in a separate email re: the final invoice. Let me know if
there's anything else or you have any other questions!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-05-03
to: Andrew Berkowitz <andrew@socialstack.co>

Hey man,
Was great to chat with you the other day, and looking  forward to being a part
of the journey!
Kristina had mentioned there was a version of this for US citizens? I don't
think I would necessarily be disqualified with this one (as I invest through a
UK companywho's  purpose is general investment in startups and other sectors
(token presales make up about 10% of that)), but just wanted to make sure this
is the version you wanted me to sign.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-05-02
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Yummy April 2021 Update
msg_id: 1792f25db240e2e8
---

Not sure what happened - maybe the acquisition by Hugo (LATAM Weibo/WeChat
all in one app) fell through? But sounds like they’re still on as an
investor and seems good progress none the less

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Vicente Zavarce <vicente@pideyummy.com>
Date: Sun, 2 May 2021 at 22:58
Subject: Yummy April 2021 Update
To:
Cc: Diego Salas <diego@epakon.com>, Alejandro Sierra <
alejandro.sierra@pideyummy.com>


Hi everyone!

Wanted to provide a few exciting updates on Yummy since we last spoke:

   - We were admitted into Y Combinator's Summer 2021 Batch! Picture
   required:

[image: 0686DDDB-1D01-464E-A08C-912B1D080541 (1).jpg]


   - We just finished raising $1.2M in additional funding at $7M valuation
   caps from Hustle Fund, VitalMax, Mana Ventures and FIrst Check Ventures


   - Hugo is officially staying as an investor through a convertible note
   so we can continue our path independently


   - TED Caracas will have me as the headliner in their upcoming July event
   to tell Yummy's story, which will be great to have ahead of YC's Demo Day


   - Harvard Business School just approved writing a Case Study on Yummy.
   The main dilemma of the case is expected to be expanding geos vs. Verticals
   and if it's verticals, which ones and which ones to not do


   - We closed April with a $9M GMV Run Rate at $757,520 monthly GMV

[image: image.png]


   - We just hired a new Marketing Director that comes from 4 years as the
   Marketing Director of KFC Venezuela and will continue our growth trajectory
   while elevating the brand


   - We just hired a new Head of Product to focus on the Yummy Wallet and
   new vertical tech modules. He is a software engineer from Blackrock
   Financial moving from NYC to Caracas to work with us


   - @Diego T. Salas <diegotomassalas@gmail.com> is now a member of the
   board in addition to me. Diego has been instrumental to helping us since
   day 1 in fundraising efforts and key business decisions, and his impact
   outsizes his own investment


   - @Alejandro Sierra <alejandro.sierra@pideyummy.com> is one of my
   cofounders who recently moved back to Caracas from Miami to focus in the
   business and he is now the General Director managing several areas
   including Account Management, Business Development, Customer Service and
   Operations. Previously Alejandro was managing Customer Service and Ops and
   this new role is well deserved, and I am excited to see what he'll bring to
   the table

Thank you all for supporting us thus far, I am looking forward to the times
ahead!

Best,
Vicente

information on Vang Capital, so I’m unsure how the statements for it are
being prepared (unless I am overestimating the complexity since there 1) is
no revenue to report although that will likely change next year, and 2) all
that’s needed is an account summary/balance statement as I believe the
filing isn’t due in the Uk for Capital as it is for Advisory).

Let me know what the outstanding balance is so I can make a single payment
once outstanding work is completed.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-28
to: Trevor Owens <trevor@leanstartupmachine.com>, Alex Novosad <alextheukrainian@gmail.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 17916f3ddee0020a
---

Hi guys,
Was great to connect last week and hear a bit more about what you're working on
with the Stacks accelerator. I just wanted to follow up on a few points, as well
as see what you see next steps as.
First, a few questions about the accelerator you're looking to set up - some may
make more sense to chat through: * When you said the program is launching in 1
   month, does that mean recruitment or a cohort? Any particular reason for this
   deadline?
 * Will the programs be virtual/remote or
   in-person programs (or both/either)?
 * Who are partners/stakeholders?
 * What is the vague roadmap in terms of
   spinning up second and third programs?
 * Is the (I believe) 4M budget the investment
   fund, for accelerator operations, or both? Are there funds available to
   follow on?
 * What other ecosystem development initiatives
   are Stacks working on?
 * Do you have a salary range in mind for the
   role? Will there be carry in the cohorts? Budget for the team (if not the $4M
   above)? I'm certainly open to something full time (I recognize it's probably
   better return on effort to focus predominantly on building a single thing),
   but need good incentives and thorough understanding of expectations, goals,
   who I'll need to maintain alignment with, etc. in order to make sure there is
   a good long term fit if that's the case.


Second, a few other points we talked about: * I mentioned a lengthy write-up I
   did on various aspects of accelerators (and some recommendations for a retail
   corporate): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A0GjPc997jslTex09TpkO0c4MCuex3MBMNSxczibc1o/edit?usp=sharing
 * The Algorand Europe Accelerator
   Demo Day on Thursday - feel free to
   attend :) : https://www.eventbrite.co.uk/e/demo-day-algorand-europe-accelerator-2021-tickets-148561993923
 * I raised the
   non-compete/collaborating more generally with the partners at Eterna, who
   don't seem threatened and are keen to learn a bit more about if they could
   partner/contribute. I'll catch up in more detail with them on this after the
   Demo  Day, early next week.


Let me know if you have any thoughts or questions!
Best,
Matthew

Matthew

Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-23
to: Alex Novosad <alextheukrainian@gmail.com>
subject: Re: San Holo
msg_id: 1790010f9d3c3666
---

Will follow up with questions re: our conversation (very much enjoyed it), but
definitely - RAW is the closing track for S03E07 of Silicon Valley and I
literally Shazamed it earlier this week.- pretty sure I've seen him at least
once. And I actually interned in college at Ghostly International (you might
know Tycho, Mux Mool, Gold Panda, Shigeto) - similar but less hype vibe.
This was the soundcloud for the blog I ran back in the day (the site is long
since defunct), might be some stuff you dig there
- https://soundcloud.com/thefreshnew - and some of my photos from coverage of
festivals (mixed with some randoms, but Flosstradamus, Noisia, Russ Liquid,
Polish Ambassador, Griz x Grammatik x Big Gigantic, a few others in
there: https://www.flickr.com/photos/100369418@N05/
Two Fingers, Ivy Lab, Machinedrum, Rezz, Tsuruda, Kito, Tourist, KOAN Sound are
some of my recent go tos. Also if you're into more UK Garage, I actually ran
this crowdfund for Asa like 6 or 7 years ago - the amount of work they (those
Bristol garage guys like Joker, KOAN, Cultprate, Asa, Sorrow) put into their
music is obscene - years on a single
track: https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/be-a-part-of-asa-s-debut-album-and-live-band#/

date: 2021-04-22

subject: Andrea/Hannah Intro
msg_id: 178f9a10d27e275c
---

Hi Andrea,

Please meet Hannah, who I’ve collaborated with on a project a while ago,
and is one of the most talented people I know around PPC and mobile
marketing (as well as digital marketing in general). She also has quite a
bit of experience in media-entertainment, so I imagine might have some
useful ideas or potentially be able to help you a little more hands on.

Hannah, please meet Andrea, who is an artist and serial founder (most
notably of a prominent blockchain VC). He recently had some success with an
art sale he held, and wanted to explore ways to reinvest in his digital
presence, and I thought you might have some ideas.

Hopefully you two can find a time to catch up!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-19
to: Sophie Donkin <sophie.donkin@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Good to see you in TG :)
msg_id: 178e9660a454bdd1
---

All good on my end - just workaholicing until lockdown ends, as might be
expected from me. How's everything with you?
SeedLegals was interesting - sounds like they're 5xing the size of their
marketing team, which could be fun - but I just don't know how they get past
what I imagine is a rough problem with retention/repeat customers. I'll likely
have a second interview with them it sounds though and can pick into it a bit
more. But probably not for me ultimately. Hopefully this wasn't insensitive to
bring up - I assumed you and Matthew are together? Sorry if I'm just being
socially awkward now!
I'm vaguely interested in the role, although part of the reason I was delayed in
getting back to you (and sorry for that) was that I wanted to see if the project
I mentioned was looking for a COO was solidifying first, and it has, which makes
me way less likely to take something full time (and this might be it) so maybe
not worth it.
Well if you might be interested at all, happy to tell you more - I can't say too
much in text but they have a ton of funding/partnerships lined up, a
crypto-parallel-but-not-core platform around
arts/music/entertainment/collectibles, and effectively need to spin out 5
sub-companies/teams/product lines in the next 6-9 months - but also do get the
desire for something a bit more stable. My department's kind of getting gutted
at NTT so trying to make sure I land softly
Scheduled this email so as to arrive in socially acceptable hours :)

---

msg_id: 178da91750e16bde
---

Hi Kevin,

Thanks for this. I will need to verify on my side over the weekend.

I also need to stress that we are rapidly approaching the deadline for US
filings. While we are moving forward on the Advisory filings, I have
indicated multiple times that the Capital filings (and financial statements
through the end of the calendar year) are more urgent as my father must
file them as well and is not given as much leniency for extension as a US
resident, and have not really heard any signs of progress on this (although
I imagine it is simpler than the Advisory accounting). I really can’t
stress how essential this is, and how disappointing it would be for this to
turn into a high stress situation for the second year in a row.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-14
to: ermias@ledgy.com
subject: Following up on scooping of potential engagement
msg_id: 178cee95775dd5c4
---

Hi ,
Hope you're doing well!
As per your message on Whatsapp, I've put together a more tailored outline for
some of the areas that we might potentially focus on in our work together.
Please find linked here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1z7CDe0c0xqbtEhc_VxmOTxk6sKbtai_D4nm1CHo8niU/edit?usp=sharing

However, I did have 2 small concerns in doing this:
1) I've only really gotten to talk to you about your business for 20 minutes or
so (as part of our conversation was me telling you about me). While I have tried
to create a version similar to the Codility notes, it does feel a little
premature - at the point I produced that proposal document for Codility, we had
spent more than 2 hours in discovery to understand the state of their commercial
efforts, tools and processes.
2) The initial notes I sent you about Codility were an example of some of the
low level deliveries I can execute, and to point you in the direction of the
work I did for them. My proposal for them is effectively their line item list of
tasks to be performed (which I also use for tracking of project completion),
broken down by the hour, for the course of our rigid short term (4-5 month)
task-based engagement (although they did add-on a few additional tasks during
the project). My impression was that we wanted a more flexible long-term
advisory relationship, which would suggest we may want to leave these areas a
bit broader as opposed to pre-plan the majority of the engagement. We would then
need to have a periodic alignment meeting every week or two (or at least once a
month) to decide what to focus on. This would give us the ability to more easily
reprioritize areas that shift in urgency or unexpectedly arise. However, we
could certainly discuss either an advisory or a project-based relationship,
I might be a little more comfortable with this if I were to be on the call to
answer questions about what some of these tasks are and the benefit of them, to
come to the conclusion together that some aren't worth prioritizing at the
moment, or just to communicate that in general this is meant to be a rough,
by-no-means complete menu of options that could probably benefit significantly
from from an additional hour or two of discovery and strategic planning.
Let me know if you have any thoughts or questions, or when might make sense to
meet some of the other team members to explain my approach and do some
additional discovery. Looking forward to speaking more!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-13
to: Terese Hougaard <terese@atomico.com>
subject: Re: Following up
msg_id: 178cd7555ead31a1
---

Hi Terese,
More than happy to help anytime, and let me know re: Y42.
On Salesroom, Daria is quite impressive professionally, and one of my better
friends in London tech :)

On Cledara, they've really been having some insane customer growth the past few
months, although I probably can't say more than that. I do personally use
Cledara and while it's a little finicky to set some of the payment limits, it's
pretty great for centralizing and managing my digital tool spend (and would be a
great help if I ever had a SaaS billing horror story I needed to deal with).

On Plutio, I was actually in touch with Leo over the weekend around his launch.
He can go quiet for a few days at a time with a big product release upcoming
(and I know he was also traveling earlier this week), but I dropped him a note
and will look to intro  you in the next week or two. 
On steering Plutio  away from angels, I did that once in terms of investment in
general, but that was probably about 4 or 5 years ago when all he had was a very
rough MVP. He would have had a lot  of trouble getting any serious interest, and
certainly not at any attractive valuation for him to build long term. Frankly
now, I think he is definitely at the stage where an infusion of some capital
could dramatically accelerate his growth and allow him to make a few hires to
build much faster/more efficiently. However, the main investors who have gotten
in contact with him so far want very significant positions for not incredibly
attractive terms and little value-add, so haven't really shown him the benefit
of outside money apart from the money itself (but I think I've successfully
gotten him to hold off on taking their money until at least after launch).
I've actually been talking to Leo a bit more tangibly the past 6 months or so
about helping him move toward pulling together some decks and basic
P+L/forecasts/growth metrics to do his platform justice, meeting some investors
(Daria from Salesroom was actually keen to meet him when she was still at Oxx),
and getting the ball rolling on a raise (partially so I can invest a small
amount as well). From the sound of it, once the new product is live and he's
made sure everything's working, he wants to really focus on that and commercial
aspects for a while. So all to say, zero issue making that intro as I think your
input could be very useful for him (and would help to have another voice of
reason giving him some advice to navigate his first raise).

I'll pull together a few other companies that might be interesting based on the
above as well in the coming weeks, but let me know if there's anything else I
can help with! And on my side, I am starting to look at what's next for me (not
urgently, but with Mark leaving and the focus within NTT shifting, I'm starting
the process) - if you know any funds hiring or high-growth mid-stage companies
looking for CMO/Head of Growth/Head of Analytics/even potentially senior
marketing/analytics roles, I'd love to potentially talk with them.
Best,
Matthew

Matthew

date: 2021-04-13
to: Mitali Rakhit <mitali.rakhit@guardara.com>
subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: [EXTERNAL] Matthew Eisner <> Mitali Rakhit

Subject: 2 new citations to your articles
To: <MattEisn@gmail.com>


[PDF]  ASSESSING AND IMPROVING ROBUSTNESS OF PSYCHOLOGICAL RESEARCH FINDINGS IN
FOUR STEPS.
MB Nuijten - 2021Increasing evidence indicates that many published findings in
psychology may be 
overestimated or even false. An often-heard response to this “replication
crisis” is to 
replicate more: replication studies should weed out false positives over time
and 
increase robustness of psychological science. However, replications take time
and 
money–resources that are often scarce. In this chapter, I propose an efficient 
alternative strategy: a four-step robustness check that first focuses on
verifying …•Cites: ‪Investigating variation in replicability‬
[PDF]  STEREOTYPE UND VORURTEILE GEGENÜBER ROM* NJA-STUDENT* INNEN: EIN
ÜBERBLICK ZU DEN URSACHEN, ZU MASSNAHMEN UND BEWÄHRTEN PRAKTIKEN
S Civitillo, F Ialuna, P JugertTrotz kontinuierlicher Bemühungen, die
Bildungsnachteile, denen Rom* nja-
Schüler* innen in ihrer Schullaufbahn begegnen, zu verringern, bleibt die 
Ausgrenzung dieser Gruppe in ganz Europa weitestgehend unverändert. In unserem 
Kapitel werden wir die Mechanismen der Stereotypisierung und Vorurteile 
untersuchen, die den Kern der schulischen Ausgrenzung von Rom* nja bilden, aber 
auch einige Empfehlungen dazu geben, wie diesen präventiv entgegengewirkt …•
Cites: ‪Investigating variation in replicability‬

---
date: 2021-04-13
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Following up from last week
msg_id: 178c9ed2a09a02ed
---

Hi Victoria,
Following up on a few points from last week:
Keyword research update  - I’ve added some keywords around Blogs and Content
Production to a tab of the Keyword Research spreadsheet - 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NQVcN3RQS7UNGma3CQ2yOUVAFg0nMpUt3kcfxt9ZjUA/edit#gid=1537711009 
 - I used “blog post” and “content production” as the trigger phrases, so
combinations of words in the list below should prove fruitful for building up on
topics around those phrases. I did take a slightly leaner approach and used a
few less data sources for this batch of keyword research, but it shouldn’t be an
issue given I’m using about 10% of the amount of trigger phrases as I was in the
first batch
Goal tracking setup  - I’ve also made a few more tweaks to the Google Analytics
and Data Studio dashboards. Happy to do a bit of a deeper dive into these as a
separate project, but the analytics side was always a bit above and beyond what
I typically do for SEO projects (and not something I would usually add on for an
already a project I’ve already given my “Wordpress discount” to). Google
Analytics especially is one of my most commonly used tools, so I can’t speak
enough to the benefits of getting a bit comfortable with it (for me YouTube
tutorials are the best way, coupled with searching Google for specific support
documentation). Google Data Studio seems even more so, but again, its  a bit new
for me (although I’m planning to set up some dashboards for my own sites soon,
so hopefully might be able to share something a bit nicer with you then - and
the benefit with GDS is you can just plug your data in using the dropdown).
Sitemap instructions  - it looks like some of the sitemap may have just needed
time to update - I think we were having some issues with the homepage not
showing up in the sitemap? It looks like it is now in this one:
https://100poundsocial.com/sitemap-misc.html * For the sitemap, it appears that
   there is likely some PHP template level that is preventing the sitemap from
   being read properly in a few ways.
 * Pages in the case studies and
   testimonials site maps have an extra “/“ before the final URL slugs -
   https://100poundsocial.com/sitemap-pt-testimonials-2021-03.htmlIt looks like
   generally the sitemap has some trouble reading redirects and certain custom
   post/page types.It also looks like there is an error in the construction of
   the post sitemaps where they are missing and XLM tag - see sitemaps starting
   with https://100poundsocial.com/sitemap-pt-post here
   https://search.google.com/search-console/sitemaps/sitemap-index-drilldown?resource_id=sc-domain%3A100poundsocial.com&sitemap_index=https%3A%2F%2F100poundsocial.com%2Fsitemap.xml&hl=en
   A general debug/testing of the sitemap might be good to ensure there are no
   other issues that might be affecting sitemap accuracy that we haven't realize 
    yet (and to find the root issue with the above points)


CMSs  - Apart from getting lucky and finding an awesome Wordpress dev (or hiring
a front end dev in house to plug away at the site) and headless CMSs/static
sites (Ghost/Gatsby/Craft/etc), I’ve been using Webflow recently for my site,
and have been quite happy with it (although it’s a little bit harder than the
WordPress drag and drop builders, its SEO is considerably improved since the
last time

Let me know if there's anything I'm forgetting!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-04-10
to: crypto@chapterone.vc
subject: Reach out about Medium job posting
msg_id: 178bb0966c08aec3
---

Hi,
I saw your post on Medium, and wanted to reach out to enquire about the role.
I currently am  the Program Director for Algorand Europe Accelerator, an
initiative by Algorand to support projects building on it's chain, which will
end in a month. I also manage the quant analytics, market research, and
modelling fo NTT DATA's R+D arm to make 6 figure strategic investments in
enterprise startups to resell to their UK clients, but will likely leave this
role soon due to a huge organizational shakeup within our 150+ company
conglomerate.`And I also run a tiny hybrid startup/crypto fund on the side
(notable investments include Cledara, Yummy (acquired by Hugo), Omenics/
Predicoin, ECOMI presale, Sovrin/verim presale, FLOW presale (I have a good
friend who works at Dapper :) ) a bunch of metaverse plots).
Before these roles, I consulted for startups on a range of projects. Some
notable ones include: * Building Coinfirm's headless website, managing their
   SEO, and helping them with marketing around launch
 * Helping Radix with technical marketing, user data, and
   planning their token sale
 * Configuring CRM systems, configuring analytics systems,
   building marketing teams and operations, and automating communications for
   scale up startups including Codility, Vortexa, Jobbio, etc
 * Innovation projects for Siemens Bosch, citizen M, Tech
   Nation (UK gov't startup support vehicle), Rainmaking, Finnovista


Before that, I ran the global marketing and all application and portfolio data
for the Startupbootcamp accelerators as we grew from 8 to 20 programs. I drove a
3x increase in applications to our accelerators by applying data and insights in
the oversight of our 20+ program marketing staff. To do this, I stood up a
Postgres data system to deliver reports to each program, and created marketing
templates for our most effective executions. I also created an annual report to
help guide our recruitment efforts and optimize for finding the best startups
available to bring into the programs.
I'm looking for what's next in my career, and hope to speak to you more about
the role you're hiring for to see if it might be a good fit! Please find my CV
attached
Best,
Matthew

P.S. I'm currently based in London, but not very bullish on the UK's mid-term
economic outlook. My mom lives in Santa Monica, so LA would be a natural place
to eventtually relocate to.

---
date: 2021-04-10
to: jason@calacanis.com
subject: I work hard, learn fast, & take ownership
msg_id: 178bae43f2a006ec
---

Please find attached a CV
Instead of a cover letter, I've elaborated on the 3 points in the subject line
below.
I hope to learn more about what working for you might entail and if we might be
a good fit!
Best,
Matthew


I work hard: * I spent my Saturday morning writing this email 
 * I’ve been working 80-100 hour weeks in tech for about 8 years now
 * In college I worked in multiple marketing and psych research
   labs, interned for a record label, did SEO (and was thrown the social media
   channel as the youngest team member) for Car and Driver Magazine, built a
   music blog on WordPress that I grew to a couple thousand daily users, managed
   bookkeeping and paid digital ads for the big campus t-shirt printing company,
   and worked for a market research agency doing sentiment analysis on press
   coverage of the J.P. Morgan Whale Trades and the Boeing 787 Dreamliner
   issues. 
 * I currently work leading the analytics and modelling for NTT
   DATA’s R+D arm in the UK, where we make 6 figure + strategic investments in
   startups. However, even while contributing in areas of the business outside
   of my core focus (such as helping design our customer data and experience
   solution consulting offerings), I found my work was not challenging enough,
   so took another role in addition designing and serving as the Program
   Director for Algorand’s European accelerator - I oversaw a team of 5 to drive
   190 applications and deliver a mentorship curriculum, and our startups have
   already raised more than $6 million since the start of the program (and we
   expect this to increase dramatically at Demo Day).


I learn fast: * I’m a self taught musician
 * For the first 5 years of my career, I did not care about salary
   - I cared about where I could grow, learn, and surround myself with people
   that were smarter than me. At age 25, I turned down a 6 figure head of
   marketing role at a generic learn-to-code platform to work at Startupbootcamp
   for 1/4 of the pay, as I saw more valuable learning in starting to understand
   the wider startup ecosystem and to be a part of the growth from 8 to 20
   accelerator programs. Somewhere I can learn is still one of the bigger factor
   in where I am looking for my next role, although I would like to continue to
   balance that a little bit more with financial success
 * At Startupbootcamp, I was tired of working with spreadsheets to
   manage all of our application and portfolio data, so I learned Postgres SQL
   to stand up a more scalable solution. Once I hit the boundaries of what I
   could reasonably do on my own in terms of engineering, we hired a data
   engineer part time to work with me to solve some of the more technically
   challenging problems and teach me a bit of Python
 * At Touch Surgery, I was frustrated with the inability to
   attribute lifetime value of users to acquisition channels, so I worked
   extensively with a data engineer outside of work to stand up multitouch
   attribution, which allowed us to 15x our growth rate (and was not as
   ubiquitous of a concept in 2014-2015). This became a cornerstone of my
   consulting work, where I have delivered analytics/marketing automation/sales
   ops solutions to scale ups (side note: it’s been an interesting learning to
   see how some European startups at A/B round stage aren’t doing metric
   benchmarking outside of financial P+L, something I’m grateful was drilled
   into me in my first 2 startup jobs in SF)
 * in my consulting work, I constantly jump at the chance to take
   research projects, or projects that are significantly outside of my comfort
   zone, so that I can learn new skills. I am also almost completely industry
   agnostic, as I find there is incredible learning to be had from working
   across a multitude of varied but similar problems (although I certainly have
   some specialties)
 * i  also very much enjoy teaching, which I firmly believe as Richard Feynman
   says is the best way to learn. This is why I mentor for accelerators, lecture
   at universities, and generally mentor startups (It’d be tricky to estimate,
   but I probably have done 1/3 or so of my mentoring through Growthmentor.com
   over the past few years, and have done 100+ sessions there with a 4.8+
   rating)
 * I generally strive for efficiency and improvement (I used to
   model my progress towards reverse engineering the grades I needed in school
   so I knew exactly what scores I needed on tests to plan my studying, and
   sincerely enjoy tracking things and analyzing the resulting data)


I take ownership: * It’s almost hard to address this one, as the idea of not
   wanting ownership is so odd to me.
 * However, I almost always jump at the opportunity for more
   responsibility
 * I very much enjoy picking a problem and working with cross
   functional teams to design and implement an elegant and powerful solution to
   solve it - for some of my clients, this has been hiring/onboarding for
   entirely new teams, managing 6 figure ad spends, and overseeing the builds of
   complex systems. These activities require significant alignment and process
   in order to own in a way that is in line with an organization's vision, and
   it’s a very fun problem in my eyes
 * I built my own business, and hired a handful of full time
   staff (which I then had the difficult task of letting go)
 * When I was 19 and the housing market crashed, I realised
   there was an opportunity for me to make solid investment returns as well as
   get to have one of the nicest houses in Ann Arbor. I built a business case
   and convinced my dad to loan me enough money to buy a house with 5X leverage,
   managed rental and maintenance, and sold the house for a 50% ROI in 2 years.
   I did something similar when I was helping 2 friends set up a crypto
   advisory/investment company and consulting for some startups that wanted to
   pay me in equity, and built another case convinced my dad to (somewhat
   disproportionately) match me in a fund. That fund is on track to return 5x in
   5 years (very conservative estimate), and notable investments include
   Cledara, Yummy (acquired by Hugo), Relayto/, FLOW (protocol of NBA Topshots),
   and many others. And while I am certainly extremely lucky for these
   opportunities and the knowledge I have been able to gain from my dad’s
   experience in institutional and early stage investment, this has probably led
   me to have a chip on my shoulder to make the most I can of them, as opposed
   to become complacent. It also likely made me very wary of a job in finance
   (as his career was much more corporate finance than VC/angel investing) and
   nudged me towards marketing and early stage startups.

Hi Victoria,
I'm still learning the ropes a bit with Google Data Studio, so do want to set
expectations that this is more of an experiment, and the bulk of the
dashboarding/reporting is going to be in Google Analytics. I think where Google
Data Studio is especially useful, apart from just looking nicer and being a bit
more flexible/powerful than the GA dashboards, is pulling metrics from
different tools (GA, email, social media) into one dashboard, but I haven't even
looked at that yet unfortunately :/
Do you have this yellow dropdown at the top of the dashboard? Not sure if maybe
it's only visible to me as the creator.
​

The dropdown will let you link this dashboard into any Google Analytics property
you have access to. I'm sure there has to be a way to set this permanently to a
single site, but I haven't looked for it yet as I still need to add the new
Goals in. Should have both done by tonight (if the former is possible).
Let me know if you have any questions, or we can go through it on the call
tomorrow.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-04-06
to: Terese Hougaard <terese@atomico.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 178a92af5568c9ab
---

Hi Terese,
Was great to catch up with you today and hear a bit more about how Atomico works
with their pipeline/portfolio.
As per your request, please find below a blurb about me: Matthew helps startups
and technology companies more efficiently and effectively engage their customers
by leveraging digital tools, data analysis, and marketing/sales automation. He's
guided early-stage startups to gain traction and scale marketing operations and
acquisition for B2B, B2C, and marketplace companies across a wide range
of industries. He also manages a small angel investment fund, has mentored for
20+ accelerator programs, and is a peer review published Social Psychology
researcher.
And my Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/matteisn/
In terms of startups that might be interesting to talk to, a few of my personal
portfolio companies that might be of interest are: * Cledara  - probably a little too late stage for you, but a good example of
   the using proprietary data for thought leadership content I describe below
 * TechTree  - long term view of using data to optimize technical hiring
   process, often run showcase events for VC portfolio companies, will likely
   raise soon
 * Brydg  - BBB CBILS lender, bootstrapped so far, lots of data-heavy solutions
   for real estate finance, nearly £1bn in loans financed
 * Versus  - market research solution for African market
 * Knack  - tutoring marketplace for higher education students


And a few more I've done some advising for that are also nearing fundraise: * 
   Plutio - bootstrapped design-background founder with not a ton of idea of
   what he's doing on commercial side/no idea how to fundraise, but at least
   15-20k MRR and starting to get interest from somewhat predatory angels,
   trying to steer him away (told him 5 years ago not to take money and keep 
   boootstrapping)
 * 
   Salesroom - very early stage, my friend just left Oxo Ventures to start this,
   basically a virtual environment for long-sales cycle sales demos


Some companies I would check out to contextualize the one we were talking about
today: * Keboola
 * Domo
 * Exponea
 * Tray.io
 * Hull
 * Glassbox
 * Heap



One other quick point about why I love marketing with data companies - the
proprietary data these companies collect can often be a sort of flywheel
marketing effect. We did a very good job of this at Jobbio, where our industry
reports basically took our internal data that was inherently unique, packaged it
for public consumption as thought leadership, tied in external partners to help
us distribute the content and provide moore data, and collected even more
industry data through forms as a continuing data collection initiative (which
was really just lead collection). We did similar at Startupbootcamp with our 
Startup Ecosystem Analysis  (which was an aggregation and repackaging of the
per-program reports I used to run), and actually I've always thought Atomico did
a particularly good job of this for a VC with their little globe visualization
(and I actually tried to create a similarly inspired open source "Startup Map"
at one point). I'd point to App Annie as maybe one of the best examples of these
types of marketing efforts with their annual State of Apps report (but also
SendGrid, Hubspot, and quite a few other more technical marketing platforms).
Let me know if there's anything else that would be useful to add/elaborate on,
and would love to catch up again soon!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-04-06
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Sitemap solution
msg_id: 178a8b92a1ed0b9d
---

Hi Victoria,
Sorry for the delay on this, just wanted to make sure I had a solid handle on
everything before I came back to you. I believe what we're seeing is a
limitation of the free Yoast SEO plan (in that it can only handle non-custom
post and page types in the sitemap), but really there are not many incredibly
useful features of the paid version, so I'd recommend a workaround.
I tested a few other automatic sitemap generators, and believe I found a good
one that seems to be generating a correct sitemap and has a lot of users/active
support. However, it seems I don't have the access to remove the old sitemap
from Google Search Console (see the top right corner of the image below). If
it's okay, we can spend 2-3 minutes walking through this together and setting
the new sitemap URL on our call this week, as I think it may need to be done by
a verified owner (i.e. the account that verified domain ownership in Google
Search Console).
​

Please find the new sitemap here:https://100poundsocial.com/sitemap.xml
Once this is in place in GSC, I'll turn off the Yoast sitemap in the plugin and
monitor to ensure the new sitemap is working properly.
Let me know if you have any questions!

---
date: 2021-03-31
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: Info / questions from call
msg_id: 1788a5f559ffba46
---

Hey Victoria,
To answer the questions in your email:
Sitemap issues - looks like something weird has happened here, as there weren’t
issues before and I didn’t change anything. 2 things worth looking into - 1) why
are there 25 errors in the posts sitemap (suggests an issue with PHP templates,
2) what are the URLs that are missing from the sitemap/do they have in common.
It seems like a lot of them might be the drag and drop built pages? Once we find
the pattern, we can figure out how to fix it. The Sitemaps tab in Google Search
Console is usually my go-to for this type of monitoring




Topic clusters - so I’ve sort of been hinting at this - topic clusters naturally
emerge from having a good URL hierarchy (we’ve established some with the
category folders in the blog and in industry sections of the site. It’s also why
I de-emphasise the importance of specific key phrases in favour of using
combinations and permutations of sets of keywords, as this helps cover more
variations of keywords that comprise topic clusters. It’s also why I suggest
having a bit of a theme across content/marketing/sales for 4-8 week increments,
so you can build up content that supports this topic.




Keyword research updates - I typically update keywords about once every 6-18
months (depending on how important SEO is as a business driver and how rapidly
trending topics in the space are changing - so for you I’d probably recommend 12
months). I do however use tools like Buzzsumo to find social-trending
keywords/phrases for fresh content ideas (as these are a usually a bit ahead of
search indexing) and use Google Trends to do quick keyword comparisons as needed
when I’m on the fence around keyword variations to use




Keywords Victoria added to spreadsheet - look good, very much on right track,
think about how to make more specific/iterative/"able to make a series from"




Tags - it’s generally good practice to add these. It depends on your visual
template setting if and how they are used, but I do think your site has a “tags”
search results page, and maybe includes the tag as a visual element on the pages
that are tagged with it, which is good for being able to somewhat clunky just
attach keywords to various posts and pages. If you get into more advanced
searching and filtering of content or RSS feeds, these could become quite
important, but you’d have to be dealing with pretty high volume of content for
that to make sense (probably daily or at least 2x weekly releases)




No index pages - I would actually recommend you have your dev do this, as it
requires changing the header HTML code on this pages (I can do it, but I’d be a
little nervous I might not be making the changes properly in the site template)
- All that needs to be done is to add this: <meta name="robots"
content="noindex">  : to the relevant page headers - 
https://developers.google.com/search/docs/advanced/crawling/block-indexing#:~:text=You%20can%20prevent%20a%20page,header%20in%20the%20HTTP%20request
.




Performance Goals: The amount of content you publish really has a much greater
impact on traffic than SEO. However, were you to publish articles at least twice
per month, and assuming no new issues emerge, and we can clean up any remaining
redirect and sitemap issues, I would expect at least a 50% increase in traffic
after about 6 months. If you are able to shift most of the site away from
no-code/drag-and-drop builders, I would expect this to be about 100% increase. I
would expect this traffic to be higher converting than the existing traffic on
your site (although as conversion goals we only just set up, I do not have
historical data for this so don’t have a benchmark to work with to make a
prediction).

I'll get some more documentation for you on the backlink/redirect and Google
Analytics questions for tomorrow.
Talk to you then!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-31
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: error
msg_id: 1788a5cbd6d2386e
---

Hey Victoria,
This shouldn't be an issue when, as the WP Smush plugin only runs when you
upload an image file. That being said, I did find a fair amount of uncompressed
images that WP Smush was able to reduce the size of, so I'm not sure how well
the other plugin is working (or if maybe images in certain sections of the site
have been excluded). If you do notice any issues, you can just deactivate the WP
Smush plugin, and then reactivate it periodically to compress whatever images
you've uploaded recently.
Hope this answers your question, but let me know if there's anything I can 
clarify!
Best,
Matthew

<https://email.opensea.io/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnUFNtZ1RTbWdRREFBRjRoQUpXblJfWllfcmpmRjBPRFlrPSIsImhyZWYiOiJodHRwczovL29wZW5zZWEuaW8vYmxvZy9hbm5vdW5jZW1lbnRzL29wZW5zZWEtcmFpc2VzLTIzbS10by1zY2FsZS10aGUtbGFyZ2VzdC1tYXJrZXRwbGFjZS1mb3ItbmZ0cy8_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5ld3NsZXR0ZXIrTWFyY2grMjAyMVx1MDAyNnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFcdTAwMjZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsX2FjdGlvblx1MDAyNnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9Y3VzdG9tZXIuaW8iLCJsaW5rX2lkIjoxMDUyNiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjF9/f0bb730a1d26c53fa619379ee23da9ec2e22ab2d001f890635d3f7b48a762ead>
with Andreessen Horowitz, Mark Cuban, Tim Ferriss, Naval Ravikant, 3LAU,
RAC, the founders of Figma and Behance, and other creators and NFT

Rob Gronkowski

to create limited-edition trading cards
<https://email.opensea.io/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnUFNtZ1RTbWdRREFBRjRoQUpXblJfWllfcmpmRjBPRFlrPSIsImhyZWYiOiJodHRwczovL29wZW5zZWEuaW8vY29sbGVjdGlvbi9yb2ItZ3Jvbmtvd3NraS1jaGFtcGlvbnNoaXAtc2VyaWVzLW5mdHM_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5ld3NsZXR0ZXIrTWFyY2grMjAyMVx1MDAyNnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFcdTAwMjZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsX2FjdGlvblx1MDAyNnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9Y3VzdG9tZXIuaW8iLCJsaW5rX2lkIjoxMDUyNywicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9/94dca154b87bf520007f88beff1eb884cb7ba89be4c6d2227bb08e3c07a946aa>
to celebrate his 4 championships. The sale marked the first time a leading
athlete has distributed NFTs of their own, and more top-level sports stars
are dropping with us soon. Gronk's auction
<https://email.opensea.>
set the all time record for 1/1 NFT trading cards, finishing at $433k!

*Kings of Leon*

Kings of Leon celebrated their latest album release with an OpenSea sale in
collaboration with YellowHeart. The NFT Yourself collection included
collectibles with unlockable content such as album downloads and front row
tickets for life. Check out what's available on the secondary market.
<https://email.opensea.>

*Kevin Abosch*
[image: Kevin Abosch]
<https://email.opensea.>

Kevin Abosch is one of the pioneers of the crypto art movement. While
active in the space for the past three years, his recently-dropped 1111
collection
<https://email.opensea.>
is the first time he's presented work on a public platform. The final few
auctions are running for another couple of days, so move fast if you want
to get involved
<https://email.opensea.>.
Read about Kevin and the sale here
<https://email.opensea.>
.

*Spotlight*

*Valentina Loffredo | The Way In*
[image: Valentina Loffredo]
<https://email.opensea.>

Hello, my name is Valentina Loffredo, I am an Italian artist based in Hong
Kong. My work is minimal, conceptual and has its very own sense of humour.
Keeping things visually clean and simple works for me not only for the
aesthetics, but also to leave space to the imagination, to keep the
conversation open. Humour, on the other hand, is often my entry point for a
deeper conversation.

I have been a full time artist since 2017. My projects have been exhibited
with solo shows in Hong Kong and Milan, featured in “Personal Structures”
(collateral event of the Venice Art Biennale) and in several group
exhibitions and art fairs in Hong Kong, Shanghai and London, including an
auction by Sotheby’s. My first photography book 'Reality, Curated' was
published by NHP in 2020.

In March 2021, I minted my first NFTs collection on OpenSea
<https://email.opensea.>
!

*Lisa Odette | The Ladies*
[image: Lisa Odette]
<https://email.opensea.>

I'm a 3D and 2D artist who focuses on exploring the female essence through
shapes and colors. My style is recognizable for the very stylized forms,
bright colors, pattern design, and an emphasis in the overall geometrical
composition of the scenes.



In my OpenSea collection
<https://email.opensea.>
'The Ladies', I compile my personal explorations of woman figures, fashion,
pattern design and landscape illustrations, all very geometrical and
stylized, always playing with filling the space, even exaggerating the
forms of the ladies to achieve this. Among portraits and full body
illustrations, some of them are looped short animations that gives life to
the scenes and invites the viewer to stare at them longer. Each piece is a
1/1 NFT of my personal works created between 2020 and 2021.

*SuperNfty*
[image: SuperNfty]
<https://email.opensea.>

Hi, I’m SuperNfty, a 25-year-old, European 3D-artist. I’ve loved collecting
things from a very young age: Pokémon cards, Flippo’s, marbles, anything.
Heavily inspired by designer toys, pop surrealism and hype culture, I’ve
been quietly designing my own virtual toys for the last 5 years, some of
them brought to life through virtual reality and 3D printing.



The Super Nfty Floating Heads
<https://email.opensea.>
are high quality, animated 3D characters unburdened by gravity. Only 264
will ever be made and every single one will be completely unique (1/1). A
fun fact is that I actually make the 3D models for these in VR, in a
program called Gravity Sketch. I’d like to view the floating heads as the
first project in a long lasting career in collectibles. My number one goal
is to keep creating and adding value for my community. It’s been so much
fun talking and collaborating with collectors and other artists on Discord,
Twitter and Instagram.



I never dared to pursue a career in art until I heard about NFTs a few
weeks ago. Now, I’m completely obsessed with Cryptoart!

*New projects*

*Ai Art House*

*Richie Hawtin*

Techno pioneer Richie Hawtin released the Acid Drop collection
<https://email.opensea.>
on OpenSea to mark 303 day, a day that celebrates the synthesizer at the
heart of his trademark Plastikman sound!

*EulerBeats*

Speaking of music NFTs, the EulerBeats team is auctioning 1/1's from their
newly-launched Enigma collection
<https://email.opensea.>,
and there are just a few hours to get your bids in! Check out the guest
blog post
<https://email.opensea.>
for
details: "Imagine music as mathematical art on an NFT substrate. This is
EulerBeats..."

Hi Janice, Josette, and Kelly,
It appears that when you sent me my returns for the 2018 tax year, certain pages
were not scanned properly. My new accountant needs to verify that these previous
filings were filled in correctly/how they were filled in for the upcoming
filing, and I need them for my record keeping.
Can you please resend the document with pages 39-42, 47-50, 55-60, 81-84, and 86
 in tact  (as they are currently cut off and prevent the full page from being
read) as soon as possible?
Please let me know if there are any issues getting this to me before the end of
this week,
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-03-28
to: Brian Leidel <BLeidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Tax info
msg_id: 1787b274e4eb809a
---

Hi Brian,
Very sorry for the delay on this - it seems this slipped through my inbox around
the holidays.
If it's still useful, please find attached my UK personal tax returns for
2019/2020. I also have my most recent round of filings for the 2 companies, and
will be filing UK personal filings soon.
I've also attached the 1044 file I think you're looking for, but if it's not
there, it might be in thiis
 folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/12LdJGGgmSu04GptDelPZATWUcCxUEVJO?usp=sharing.
- if that's not what you're looking for, just let me know. And please do let me
know if this was filed incorrectly, as the previous company that did my filings
before you blindsided me with a bill that was 10x what they initially quoted, so
if they didn't perform the work properly that is a big issue.

I did want to see what you would need for me for the next set of filings (and
for my dad in terms of his ownership stake in the investment company) - sorry
I'm a bit late to initiate this conversation. I also wanted to see if we could
pick up a conversation around my incorporation strategy, and if it might make
more sense for me to have a company in the US that I hold any profits in (or to
shift all my companies to the US), as well as if certain tax credit programs in
the UK make sense for me to try and take advantage of.
Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-03-24
to: jonathan saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Update
msg_id: 178643a71dc654c5
---

These look good to me, and agree even a single rope that way is too intense (I
had a subtler effect in mind, but I have a feeling it would probably be a lot of
work for something 99% of people wouldn't even notice if it was subtler).
Just to clarify, is the difference between the two logos that one is square? If
so, I like this and think we can move forward (or if not just let me know what
my untrained eyes should look for). One touch up for the square logos is it
would be good to have a square version with just the V, and another with "Vang
Capital" and "Vang Advisory" under the V (in addition to the horizontal version
which is good as is, and I like the center alignment as opposed to bottom
alignment with the V that I think was in previous versions). Hopefully that all
made sense?
For the deck, I think I'd rather Google Slides - but do you have a strong
preference towards PPT?
Thanks very much!
Matthew

date: 2021-03-23
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Paying freelancer with Crypto & US/UK Tax
msg_id: 1786112e74c95898
---

Hey Antonio,
Hope all is well! Wanted to touch base on 2 points.
First, I'm starting to set up branding/etc for my companies (starting with
Capital). I wanted to ask how complex it would be to pay freelancers with crypto
(or if it's much easier to do it from the Revolut account from an accounting
standpoint).
Also, I have a US tax filing coming up (although the deadline has been extended
to early May). Can we get statements for both companies (more urgently Capital,
as my dad also has to file that, and does not have as much flexibility as I do,
as he has to file state taxes in addition to federal, which are not as lenient
with extending deadlines).
Also, I did want to try and sync up (ideally also with my US accountant) to see
if we could start to figure out an incorporation strategy that reduces my double
tax.
Thanks!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-23
to: jonathan saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Update
msg_id: 178610c35352b984
---

Hey Jon,
Thanks for this :)
So I think the colour scheme here is good.
For the "V", I think it's a little bit too plain now - is there maybe a mid
point? Still 3D but with harder edges, or rounded edges but still 2D (I do like
the 3D aspect though). Just some thoughts, I'll leave for you to decide if/how
it makes sense to iterate, but as is I would probably
Also, I know I didn't flag it before, but can I get white versions of the logo
as well for use on dark backgrounds? And a square icon version for various
social pages/favicons/etc?
I like the lines design - I agree it probably won't be heavily as a main element
on the Capital site, but I think it will be very helpful as a placeholder and
maybe more used as an element in decks (or as an accent on pages like
blog posts, case studies, etc - as I'm sort of thinking of the MVP version of my
Advisory site, which will basically an effort to build out a few templated pages
that we'll likely go back and restyle and apply to content on the Capital site).
But I like these and the "rope" connection. If it's not significant effort, it
could be cool to add a braided-rope effect to each line, but not sure if that's
a headache or something that's a quick filter/texture add-on.
In terms of visuals, actually I had started to set up a company with a couple of
friends, one of whom was a designer, and we came up with this start to design -
also has a bit of a nautical/boat theme, but something I can probably play on
(and I'm linking here just for reference/potential inspiration) - although the
motivation/feeling/theme was more about adventure into the unknown than
optimization/performance: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/16fCrcDNctANHsknW-0KWXSlWGwiv7MBIdzLFHvptpvM/edit?usp=sharing
Let me know if I can clarify or elaborate on anything, or if you have any
thoughts/feedback.
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-22
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Update
msg_id: 1785b6a1845251b1
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Mar 2021 at 17:27
Subject: Re: Update
To: Alicia Burke <aliciaevelynburke@gmail.com>


Hey man,

Generally I really like this. I do have a few thoughts that might be more
in the realm of more small incremental changes, or aesthetics visuals to
keep in mind for graphics/textures/future logos as opposed to this
iteration.

In no particular order (and please don’t take offense to any word choice -
just throwing out adjectives to try and frame my thinking):

-I really like this brand for a tech company, but the consulting/investor
angle that I’m coming from makes me feel that something a bit more
stable/secure/experienced/data-driven/insightful/wise/mature as opposed to
light/playful/childish/fun. I’m okay with (and really actually like)
cutting edge/new wave/etc, but not at the expense of
reliability/trust/smart/experience/credible/knowledgable and not douche-ily
hip like some media/digital agencies

-I think the colours may be a little bit too light. It’d also be helpful to
see the secondary colours used in a layout to see how they fit together.
Also maybe the block V is rotated a bit too much (really not sure though)

-I like the block logo, but it does feel a little more like a tech company
(and even potentially blockchain, which I’m not opposed to and don’t want
to exclude, but don’t really want to align myself with as blockchain has
some trust stigma around it). I do like the parallel to the bar chart of my
personal logo, and the rising bar chart graphic that it is now (as my
personal one wasn’t necessarily going up so to speak). And generally I like
this logo - I think I would love it if I was founding a software company
around data analytics

-Random side - I like that the logo has a line going through a block - a
vang is technically a line (rope) that runs through a block through a block
(pulley system) to make it manageable to pull the sail and pin it to change
the shape. I could see more visual play on this idea potentially. Maybe
more for backgrounds/textures/the future


Let me know what you think - think we’re definitely not far off :)

Best,

Matthew




On Fri, 19 Mar 2021 at 13:13, Alicia Burke <aliciaevelynburke@gmail.com>
wrote:

date: 2021-03-21
to: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@vortexa.com>
subject: Re: A note on Rhys
msg_id: 1785615454077313
---

Sorry for the delay - the past few weeks have been really hectic.
Huge congrats! :) I'll drop you a message next week and maybe we can find a time
for a catch up in the coming week or two - my numbers 07523506891
Sounds like very valuable experience (and a role that seems to often become more
and more important as sales-driven tech companies really hit scale :) )
Hope all is well!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-21
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Catching up!
msg_id: 178560f562aea9b6
---

Most of the fundraising/IR is covered by a few of the other partners, and
there's a VC that's a significant stakeholder in the program that can pretty
easily fill out their rounds what with how much money is flying around in
crypto, but happy to chat and put something in front of them, and would be great
to catch up more generally. Thursday is pretty flexible for me, and Friday could
work.
Where are you based these days? Hope all is well!

---
date: 2021-03-21
to: Scott Cowley <scott@thesalesmastermind.com>
subject: Re: Invitation: Algorand Europe Accelerator - Sales Workshop @ Wed 24 Mar 2021 10am - 11am (GMT) (scott@thesalesmastermind.com)
msg_id: 178560c8bbffd14f
---

I'd say maybe 3-4 will do direct sales on the phone, but the rest will have a
fair amount of biz dev and partnerships calls (some with very significant
amounts)
Looking forward to the session :)

---
date: 2021-03-21
to: Alicia Burke <aliciaevelynburke@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Update
msg_id: 17855d37d4849c41
---

Hey man,

Generally I really like this. I do have a few thoughts that might be more
in the realm of more small incremental changes, or aesthetics visuals to
keep in mind for graphics/textures/future logos as opposed to this
iteration.

In no particular order (and please don’t take offense to any word choice -
just throwing out adjectives to try and frame my thinking):

-I really like this brand for a tech company, but the consulting/investor
angle that I’m coming from makes me feel that something a bit more
stable/secure/experienced/data-driven/insightful/wise/mature as opposed to
light/playful/childish/fun. I’m okay with (and really actually like)
cutting edge/new wave/etc, but not at the expense of
reliability/trust/smart/experience/credible/knowledgable and not douche-ily
hip like some media/digital agencies

-I think the colours may be a little bit too light. It’d also be helpful to
see the secondary colours used in a layout to see how they fit together.
Also maybe the block V is rotated a bit too much (really not sure though)

-I like the block logo, but it does feel a little more like a tech company
(and even potentially blockchain, which I’m not opposed to and don’t want
to exclude, but don’t really want to align myself with as blockchain has
some trust stigma around it). I do like the parallel to the bar chart of my
personal logo, and the rising bar chart graphic that it is now (as my
personal one wasn’t necessarily going up so to speak). And generally I like
this logo - I think I would love it if I was founding a software company
around data analytics

-Random side - I like that the logo has a line going through a block - a
vang is technically a line (rope) that runs through a block through a block
(pulley system) to make it manageable to pull the sail and pin it to change
the shape. I could see more visual play on this idea potentially. Maybe
more for backgrounds/textures/the future


Let me know what you think - think we’re definitely not far off :)

Best,

Matthew




On Fri, 19 Mar 2021 at 13:13, Alicia Burke <aliciaevelynburke@gmail.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2021-03-18
to: Anastasia Lawes <anastasia@doordeck.com>
subject: Re: Washing machine
msg_id: 17845bcbd6b094a4
---

Hi Anastasia,

I have not heard anything about the washing machine or the delivery company
since there was a confirmation SMS on 10 March. I was under the impression
it was supposed to be installed today. Is there some reason why that is not
the case?

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-18
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: Project folder deleted | ATTENTION REQUIRED
msg_id: 17842bb628478de4
---

Hi Rhys,

I have never had any intention or desire to pursue legal action, and the
only situation in which I will is if I am being held accountable by one of
our clients for work, accesses, or files that went wrong/were breached that
you are responsible for. However, I have certainly not waived the right of
non-circumvent/non-compete for all our clients, so you not contacting
clients covered under the NDA is not up for negotiation/your acceptance,
and you have been notified to cease these communications. I do believe I
had waived this for Vortexa, but I am not aware of having done it for any
other clients.

As it stands, I do not think you’ve done any of the actions that would
necessitate me pursuing further action. It sounds like you’ve done a
relatively thorough job of removing accesses, but should it eventually
emerge that you still do have access to confidential
information/tools/files/etc that you have been asked to delete and
disclose/breach/etc it, you would certainly be liable in said scenario.

I had already taken the action you mentioned in regards to the password
sheet. However, you have been notified to remove accesses to any and all
relevant accounts and files, and the burden of ensuring this is done and
you do not access these again is on you. I don’t think any issue would ever
be made about it unless something went horribly wrong with a system and you
had logged in recently (Depending on the activity if visible, or actively
altered the tool or system). However it is my obligation to inform you that
you need to remove these accesses. From the sound of it, it sounds like you
largely have, although again I cannot confirm this.

Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-17
to: Chris Fung <chris.fung@outlook.com>
subject: Fwd: Mood Foods
msg_id: 1784269c821dd899
---

Hey Chris,

Hope all is well! Are you still working on the corporate lunch program
startup, or did that value proposition get hit by companies going remote?

I wanted to reach out as I had a friend reach out to me asking if I knew
any investors in food and health, and I thought of you.

Let me know if you might be interested in connecting re: their round. Also
not sure if Alex would be interested in this sort of thing at all (congrats
to you both on the acquisition!)?

Would love to catch up sometime soon as well :)

Best,

Matthew



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Nick Janssen <nick@ralston.co>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2021 at 15:44
Subject: Mood Foods :)
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hey hey my good sir,

So here's the deck for the Company I'm joining. We're raising £500k  (SEIS,
etc.) with guidance at @£5M pre-money. We've got APX, a German VC on board
which came in early with a convertible and is now taking 30% of the round,
so now it's a race to the rest so we can get to market asap. Aim is to
close end of May, and launch in July to get on shelf up to get to the 1k
store order book as quickly as we can.

Any people you think you'd be interested in this would be very much welcome.

Let me know when youre free next week to catch up in any case.

Cheers,

Nick

---
date: 2021-03-17
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Buying Bitcoin (Banking)
msg_id: 17841ed0a1b1905c
---

So this will be a little bit of a process.


Step 1 is to download this and set up an account:
https://www.ally.com

Step 2 will be to set up a kraken account

Step 3 will be take $50 from suntrust and transfer to ally, then transfer
from Ally to kraken


If that works, we can transfer a larger amount and do full KYC on Kraken

---
date: 2021-03-17
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Buying Bitcoin (Banking)
msg_id: 1784198730368201
---

Many banks will shut down your accounts if they see you interact with
cryptocurrency products. So the steps to figure out are:
1) Will Suntrust let you send AND withdraw funds with Kraken.com? If yes, this
is pretty simple - we can set up an account together on a call.
2) If Suntrust WONT  let you send/withdraw funds with Kraken, there are a few
options * 2a - Set up an account on Ally Bank (online only bank - another
   alternative is Marcus by Goldman Sachs), send funds there, send funds from
   there to Kraken
 * 2b. - I can talk to my UK accountant as to how to manage the cash
   flows through my accounts/Vang Capital
 * 2c - There may be some ways to purchase with a credit card (so we'd
   just want to make sure to use a credit card you aren't particularly attached
   to in case it gets blocked/flagged)

date: 2021-03-17
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Catching up!
msg_id: 178403a6586d92d0
---

Hi Yohann,

I’m in London.

Yup, I remember you telling me about it. I’m running an accelerator, so
certainly know startups in fundraising mode, but we have a pretty strong
investor network so will probably handle it in a more personal way. I’ll
keep an ear out though. But congrats, sounds like some nice growth. Any
notable clients?

Apart from running (actually 2) accelerators, I’m likely raising another
fund, as well as working on some other growth, research, and strategy
projects.

Any renewed life in Omenics in the huge bull run? Would seem like a prime
time if there ever was one.

Happy to catch up whenever makes sense.

Hope all is well!

Matthew

date: 2021-03-15

subject: Fwd: Book a Project
msg_id: 178367336e515cb8
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Voxel Architects <voxelarchitects.info@gmail.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2020 at 10:06
Subject: Re: Book a Project
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Dear Matthew,

Thanks for reaching out and for having interest in building your dream CV
gallery with us!
I proceed to explain you in detail how we work:

*Our rate *

We charge based on an hourly base. The number of hours required will depend
on the parcel size and complexity of the project, but the average project
is completed in 5 to 7 days of work (40 to 56 hours). And we can properly
quote it once the Design loop is completed. *Our hourly rate is 0.16 eth*.
If you have a specific budget in mind, we are open to discuss it and adjust
the project to it.


*Our process of work is divided into 3 stages.*

   - *Design and planning* (mood board, layout, asset list, concept design,
   planning, feedback loop)
   - *Asset modeling* (Magica voxel modeling)
   - *Construction in CV* (CryptoVoxels modeling, assets placement,
   texturing, animations if needed)


Attached I send you an example of a build we developed for a project called
Real Estate. The build is an Auction House built in modern style with
unique architectural features. The time for completion from the initial
iteration to finish was 7 working days (56 hs total).

One or two days were reserved for the development design concept, presented
like in the pdf attached to this e-mail, and the other five were for the
actual building and asset creation. This was a relatively small build so
time may vary given the scale of the parcel/s.

*The final result of Auction House:*
https://www.cryptovoxels.com/parcels/2657

[image: image.png]



*Here you can also find other builds we did in cryptovoxels:*
CryptoMotors Showroom  https://www.cryptovoxels.com/parcels/19
One BC HQ  https://www.cryptovoxels.com/parcels/2796
One BC Galleries https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=N@468W,291S
Token Smart amphitheater  https://www.cryptovoxels.com/parcels/2908
Token Smart Lounge https://www.cryptovoxels.com/parcels/2333
E1337 https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=W@433W,342S
Whale Fridge https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=S@198W,72N
House of money https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=S@443W,2U,311S
Pagoda project https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=W@442W,197S
Metalympics https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=W@348E,-1U,356N
Ghost extension https://www.cryptovoxels.com/play?coords=N@186E,428S

*Also you can check our DCL and Hubs builds*

NFT42 DCL HQ:
https://play.decentraland.org/?position=35%2C-29&realm=fenrir-amber
TokenSmart Hubs HQ:  https://hubs.mozilla.com/T8j9mdG
CryptoMotors Hubs HQ:
https://hubs.mozilla.com/LAugsK5/thoughtful-this-place


Attached you will find a questionnaire and the design book pdf from this
build as an example.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Kind Regards,

Leandro Bellone
CEO VoxelArchitects
https://www.voxelarchitects.com/ <https://www.voxelarchitects.com/portfolio>

date: 2021-03-12
to: Christine Chang <christine@alumni.ie.edu>
subject: Re: AWS for fintech
msg_id: 17827c45f575feb2
---

Yeah, fortunately the team I’m in in NTT is very new/entrepreneurial, so
they’re quite open to me working on the side as long as I get everything
done I need to. Although I’m a bit swamped until demo day. Actually you may
find it interesting - as my role with AEA is largely to say someone from an
accelerator background is running the program, and to try and keep my
ongoing hours down, we actually split the startups between me and 3 of the
senior Eterna Capital partners so we each manage 2-3 each - it seems to be
a decent model to avoid lots of 1-to-1s but still be able to be very hands
on, but maybe only works in a remote setting.

Sounds cool! Also might be worth checking out the startup programs Stripe
has been setting up - they’re hiring for a bunch of roles to basically seed
usage of/develop startup clients

---
date: 2021-03-12
to: jessiah.straw@gmail.com, Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Jesse / Jonathan Intro
msg_id: 1782786c4b94533e
---

Hi Jesse,
Please meet Jonathan, who has worked with me on a number of projects for design
(my personal brand, Startupbootcamp, etc). He has a lot of experience across
many industries and types of design, including product interfaces, so might be a
good fit for you.
Jonathan, Jesse is launching a company/product that will service large
investment firms. His main focus right now is standing up a product, including
the interface design that goes into that, and I thought you'd be good for him to
talk to.
I'll let you two take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-12
to: Christine Chang <christine@alumni.ie.edu>
subject: Re: AWS for fintech
msg_id: 178277f424ff6be0
---

Hi Christine,
Very sorry for the delay - I've been a bit swamped the past few weeks.
I haven't personally built on AWS, but know a lot of people that have, and the
feedback is generally very positive (although comparatively, Google Cloud
Platform also seems to be becoming a more liked product in the space). Also,
none of the tools really provide built in financial-oriented products (Stripe,
Plaid, etc), although I imagine they're quite easy to plug in. AWS used to have
a bit of a dominance because they were the first to really popularize scaling up
and down servers that priced  based on used  as opposed to reserving a monthly
usage allocation.
But generally outside of GCP and AWS (and maybe Azure), there aren't many viable
solutions for startups for these type  of infrastructure tools.
That being said, from what I can see from external signals (mainly the dramatic
reduction in credits provided to startups), it seems these companies might be
moving to increase profitability of their business models, which could mean up
and comers can undercut their price.
Just top of head thoughts - happy to chat a bit more on anything in there that's
interesting. And the Algorand role is interesting - quite low touch for what it
is (please excuse the intern-sent form introduction emails :) ). But getting to
do lots of other projects and still balancing with my full time role at NTT, so
can't complain.
Let me know how things with Amazon (or in general) go, and good luck!
Best,
Matthew
