---
date: 2021-03-09
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Some projects
msg_id: 1781470983cece9f
---

Hey Jon,
Hope you're doing well!
So I've finally got my shit together and with Alicia's help have come up with an
umbrella brand name - Vang - which currently I have Vang Capital and Vang
Advisory. For reference, vangs are the ropes on a sail boatthat designed  to
pull and hold a sail in various directions to control its shape and ensure it's
capturing as much wind in the most efficient as way  possible.
As I told you way back when, I'd love to contract you to do a few things
building on the work you did for my personal brand (ranked in order of
priority): * Solidify colours and fonts (Alicia picked some, but if you have
   strong opinions they should change) - find some initial brand guidelines here
    for a v1 site we've been mocking up on Webflow we can also share
   
 * Create a logo for the umbrella brand / 2 sub brands
 * Take 2-3 slide decks I have, polish them to create slide deck
   template, collection of brand elements/patterns/graphics


Secondary: * Business cards
 * Create some visual graphic templates for newsletter and social
   media
 * Look at refreshing personal brand
 * Website v2 (this is likely when I'd look at doing a full custom
   website)



Also, I have a friend in the US who is launching an interesting company
(targeting mid-stage/late-stage investors), and wants someone to help him put
together some product designs and eventually likely branding. Any interest in
connecting?
Let me know if either of the above might be interesting, and how you would see
next steps going.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: Sync
msg_id: 17803227027591f1
---

My only agenda was to have your NDA termination not have knock on effects.
I had hoped to do this collaboratively with you, but you were not willing
to do so, so I had to get legal assurance that you would be liable for
relevant data breaches or issues, which I have, and you have been notified
of.

Then when I informed you we would not be working together further and to
delete files and accesses, you made slanderous and libellous accusations
about the nature of my relationship with my employer. Only then did I raise
your past conduct, as an indication that while have can make untrue
accusations about me, the reasons why we are not working together are your
actions past and present which you have shared with me.

I have certainly not disclosed any of this information to people we
performed work for, and never suggested I would, apart from notifying them
that you terminated your NDA with me and that I subsequently served a
notice to you to delete any relevant files and accesses to protect their
proprietary or confidential information.

Again, I did not expect these files and accesses to become an issue - it
was only your unwillingness to come to an agreement to continue to take
responsibility for the work you performed and accesses you had that
necessitated serving that notice.

I come from a world where people take responsibility for their work, where
legal responsibilities are enforceable, and where a level of maturity and
composure is expected in business conduct. This has nothing to do with
money - the person I know in the world who is best at this was literally
born to crackheads and raised by his neighbour. And the only thing my
parents money ever got me was an education, which I have taken upon myself
to share with others, including you, and maybe connections, which I largely
rejected in favour of finding my own, and again take upon myself to share,
including with you.

You have turned this from a conversation about the need for a very small
and reasonable liability shield for an extremely unlikely scenario into
personal attacks, and brought it outside of you and I into our clients.
These are just no go’s in any business relationship (or really any
relationship), and the types of fixed pie mentality people I don’t waste
time on personally.

And at this point all I’m trying to do is part ways.

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: LAVINIA <lavinia.andreea.stoian@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Sync
msg_id: 17802ab12037d487
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2021 at 12:40
Subject: Re: Sync
To: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>


Rhys, you are coming off as deranged and unprofessional.

In some cultures, business deals cannot proceed until you get plastered
drunk with your potential business partner, as it is said this is when a
persons true colors show.

I have no desire to “use this” - just making it clear why I can not
associate myself professionally after your repeated unprofessional
interactions in and out of the workplace. I certainly have not mentioned
that information to anyone, so not sure why you think I’ve been sharing
this about. But I’m sorry, there is no world in which groping a woman is
acceptable. And if you don’t stop this harassment immediately, I will share
it to allow my clients to protect myself from future conduct from you, as
it seems like you have now taken an approach of slandering me publicly,
whereas I have not done this.

I have not tried to do anything but cover basic legal concerns (and started
with a very collaborative approach, which you were not willing to engage
in) so we can end this relationship - your hostility in response has done
nothing but deteriorate this situation, and prevented us from parting ways.
It actually seems YOU are the one who wants to sabatoge BOTH of us - stuff
like that just isn’t worth my time. Fortunately, I am now protected.

Goodbye Rhys. I have no desire to sabatoge you, pursue legal action against
you (although I will if you continue to hurt my business and violate our
agreements, and your messages here are only strengthening my case), hurt
your business, or associate or talk to you in any way really. It’s just a
waste of time.

But keep digging your hole deeper if you want

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@vortexa.com>
subject: Re: A note on Rhys
msg_id: 1780293c1c942b40
---

Had a feeling that might be the case if they were in our age bracket, but
glad to hear. Also maybe a blessing of being a relatively new business
without too much attachment to “the way things have always been done”.

Do let me know if you ever get to a more logistics use case - it’s the sort
of thing we’d probably be keen in reselling at NTT. Or if you ever want to
do something with blockchain (Algorand has sort of tried to position itself
as the enterprise blockchain - there's a interesting aerospace
manufacturing component marketplace being built on it at the moment), happy
to set up an intro there as well.

Wow that’s impressive! Hopefully overseeing all the marketing in addition
to design has been more fun than annoying - I imagine it’s interesting the
way design is interesting to me as being another piece of a more wholistic
UX/CX puzzle.

Would be great to grab a coffee and catch up when normalcy resumes (would
be amazing if that’s as soon as summer :) ), but hope all is well with you
in the meantime!

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>

---

date: 2021-03-05
to: Russell Glenister <russell@curationzone.com>
subject: Re: Request for rights removal
msg_id: 17802658fc5155d5
---

Sorry for the double emails - I should actually mention that Rhys’s NDA
still has a 5 year period in which he is required to not disclose any
information after termination of the NDA.

Moreover, I think the only systems or data he really ever had access to
would have been Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, and Hubspot. I’d just
check to make sure he’s not on any of those if you can (but I don’t
anticipate him doing anything malicious, and he is liable if he does).

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@vortexa.com>
subject: Re: A note on Rhys
msg_id: 178024efcd2958af
---

Hi Sophie,

Yeah, I didn’t think it would really be an issue, but just want to be
absolutely sure I cover all our bases. On the personal conduct, frankly I’m
grateful I found out before we progressed any further. And I do have faith
in the work he did - I still think he’s quite good at the technical setup
he does, but just too much of a liability outside of work.

I’ve been doing very well :) I’m running a cloud enterprise SaaS
accelerator for NTT DATA where we basically set up reselling agreements
with startups to bring them into new enterprise verticals, and running a
blockchain accelerator for a company called Algorand (and in general got
into blockchain a bit more over the past year, which seems to have been
very good timing). And still doing some more advisory work with various
clients (grateful NTT lets me work in the side :) ). How’s everything with
you? Vortexa? Did your friends find a way to keep their pub/bar alive
through pandemic?

Also, I wanted to ask if there had been any progress on the
shipping/logistics use cases for Vortexa (as opposed to energy market
data)? I could see it potentially being interesting for NTT’s clients.

Hope all is well!

Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Sophie Hart <sophie.hart@vortexa.com>
subject: A note on Rhys
msg_id: 178023b1baaf9b58
---

Hi Sophie,

Sorry this email came out of nowhere.

Just for some more information, I found out some very disturbing
information about Rhys’s personal conduct, and made the decision I couldn’t
work with him in the future.

When I did, he decided to terminate our NDA, and refused to sign anything
that would replace it due to a fear of NDA he has. At this point, i
consulted with a lawyer, and then I sent him a legal notice asking him to
delete all files and remove all accesses he has with clients, and making it
very clear he will be held liable for any breaches or any other issues that
he causes should this happen. I was hoping to get him to respond that “yes,
he had deleted them” at which point I was planning to give you a little
update on the situation.

Rhys is still obligated to protect any information for a 5 year
non-disclosure period after the termination of our NDA, as stipulated in
the NDA, which he is aware of.

Happy to talk to you about this and answer any questions you have :) sorry
for any drama or headache this has caused.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Russell Glenister <russell@curationzone.com>
subject: Re: Request for rights removal
msg_id: 178022e54b2350e1
---

Hi Russell,

Rhys was a freelancer I worked with briefly, who was covered by an NDA
during our project. He has decided to terminate his NDA (really for no
reason other than being uncooperative), so I have sent him legal notice
threatening action against him should he breach any data, or create any
other issue, and demanding he immediately remove all accesses and delete
all files he has related to your and any other project.

Best,

Matthew





On Fri, 5 Mar 2021 at 10:26, Russell Glenister <russell@curationzone.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2021-03-05
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: Sync
msg_id: 178016e55d8eba91
---

Haha, check your open/readreceipts - I don't really feel the need to rapidly
read your emails or respond to you at this point, and frankly I'm a bit swamped
with emails at the moment anyways, so most people have to wait a day or two.
Lol I've been selling CRM configs FOR  my company, and some pretty exciting huge
direct partnerships/joint R+D I'm getting to work on to that end :) - my
personal work is all pretty much advisory and strategic at this point, got my
head out of the low-margin execution/SI weeds ages ago. And I have my employer's
permission to work on side projects - it was a major condition of my joining. So
maybe stop talking about things you have zero understanding of and think twice
before making slanderous accusations.
If you really want to get personal (and in this case, not slanderous), part of
the reason I was never comfortable partnering up with you was your confession to
me of your previous charges for sexual related crimes. That conduct is just a
non-starter in the circles I work in. But I did appreciate your transparency,
which is why I tried to figure out some sort of working relationship. But the
deterioration of this relationship has been entirely your decision - I gave you
3 months to man up and clean it up, which you were too cowardly, scared (gonna
have to get over your NDA fear if you want to get into later stage or better
funded companies), lazy, or inconsiderate to do so. I tried multiple times to
accommodate your changes, requests, and repeated delays, but ultimately you were
never willing to come to the table. And the fact that you still don't understand
why this is important or simply the right thing to do in a business relationship
just shows you aren't really at a necessary level of professionalism or growth
mentality for us to work together. Client success is much more than driving
metric growth, especially for companies that plan to grow into large scale
businesses operating across multiple regulatory jurisdictions - and for already
large businesses, metric growth is actually often secondary.
This type of bad faith (or at best, naive, although I do believe you are smarter
than that) behaviour is a habit with you from what I have heard over the past
years from multiple of your past collaborators, so I would really reconsider
your approach.
Please immediately delete (if you have not already deleted) the previously
mentioned files and accesses which you are not permitted to have access to
any longer, as previously requested multiple times and which you have had ample
time to comply with. If it is discovered that you have not deleted these files
and accesses, and there is a breach or otherwise related issue of some kind, you
may be held liable (and will be held liable if you were in any way, shape, or
form actively the cause of said breach or issue). I do not expect this to occur,
but I need to make this abundantly clear, as I will not be providing notice of
this again.
At this point I'd like to part ways (except for if you would like to confirm you
have in fact deleted relevant files and removed said accesses). I don't see any
benefit for either of us from continuing this conversation.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-03-05
to: customerservice@tracerecovery.co.uk
subject: Re: Payment Due - TRACE Recovery Reference 105358857
msg_id: 177ffc6d7f012d04
---

This is absolutely unacceptable. I am contesting this parking fine with the
issuer, which was given to me on private property that I was parked on with the
permission of the owner. This parking fine was issued ILLEGALLY, and I have very
 substantial evidence to back this up from the property owner and property
manager. Moreover, it was not even ME who was parked there.
Please note I will pursue legal action against you if you ping my credit score
or continue to harass me. This is absolutely ridiculous that this has been
forwarded to you, and I will recoup all costs I incur related to this matter
through court if you continue to harass me. I'm sure relevant consumer
protection oversight bodies would find the handling of this matter by you and
your client completely unacceptable as well.

Hi John,

---
date: 2021-03-01
to: Alexander Guy <alexanderwguy19@gmail.com>
subject: Random potential intros
msg_id: 177ee70e9502c9ab
---

Hey Alex,
Hope you're doing well - I think last I saw on socials, you're in France (and
maybe had a kid if I'm not mistaken, at least married? Congrats on either/both!)
I was talking to two people recently who were both looking for senior marketers
in France, and was curious if you might be interested in connecting.
The first is Smart Chain, a startup in an accelerator I'm sort of running
- https://smart-chain.fr/
And the second is Relayto - I'm an investor in them and the flounder is a
personal mentor of mine and has been a part of some pretty major enterprise SaaS
products, and the marketing they need is more on the creative side than is
really my wheelhouse - http://relayto.com/
Let me know if you'd be interested in talking to either, and hope all is well!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-03-01
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: Sync
msg_id: 177ecab38c18de8d
---

Hi Rhys,
Doing well.
You were never willing to move forward on resolving the NDA issue, so you sort
of already made the decision that we can't move forward. But more importantly
and as I clearly stated last fall, I cannot align myself with someone that
creates hold-up scenarios that create additional cost and liability for my
business and my clients' businesses, and who I can't trust to act in good faith.
Also, please take this as a formal notice to immediately and permanently delete
all files, assets, data, or other materials received, and delete all account and
system accesses related to the CurationZone and Vortexa projects we worked on,
which were only sent to you under the assumption you would be under NDA, and for
which you elected to end the aforementioned NDA covering these after receiving
these files, assets, data, materials, account accesses, and system access.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-25
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: 100 Pound Social SEO and Potential Client
msg_id: 177db9429ad9b466
---

Hi Victoria,
On backlinking, there are a lot of ways you can go about doing this, including
some extremely creative or technical ways.
However, I've started to put together the recap deck, and slides 10-14 get at
this a bit (although I'm expecting to be adjusting them slightly over the coming
week or two). Please find linked
here: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1odMnUQDYb-PZA-B2RYVOUsU5LNQ-x3FoGWvbWsTr4is/edit?usp=sharing
In general, the best linkbuilding is scalable, involves some relationship
building or productization, and takes a little while to really get going, Good
content can make this a lot easier. A lot of my strategies for linkbuilding 
involve: * getting partners involved in producing content (or at least highlight
   them so they have some stake)
 * getting quotes from somewhat influential people on the topic I'm
   writing about in hopes they then share
 * finding niche press outlets that will print anything I send them
   (these outlets are usually just a level above syndication in terms of
   quality, but are a good way to ensure you get links on each major piece of
   content you release)
 * one of the more advanced tactics I've done involved basically
   figuring out how to get our content academically indexed (it was very niche
   surgical/medical content). Definitely not open to all businesses, but this
   was very powerful for us, and there may be parallels for you (think pay to
   play content listing sites with high quality of content)
 * At a basic level, building profiles on major social networks,
   business directory sites, etc and making sure they link back to your main
   site is always good practice (although often these links do not have SEO
   impact)
 * While it doesnt have direct SEO impact, traffic to your website is an
   indirect SEO factor (Google likes pages with more traffic and can pick this
   data up off GA), and you may find valuable audience from posts into social
   media groups - I found this very effective in driving startups to apply to
   Startupbootcamp accelerators and events

There are a few linkbuilding  tactics that are more technical based on looking
at where your competitors are getting links from (still worth setting up a free
trial of SEMrush or similar and pulling a competitor link report to see if
you're missing any major opportunities), or finding sites that are linking to
similar topics you're talking about but where the links are dead and reaching
out to them.
This is also a pretty solid article on linkbuilding
 tactics: https://www.growthmanifesto.com/link-building-tactics
For Brydg, let me circle back with them, and let me walk you through our basic
social process on our next call. Basic copywriting and social media management
has been a pretty big pain point for us, as I have a for-equity advisory role
with them, and while we took some time to pilot some social media tactics that
seem like they could work, don't have the capacity to actually execute them.
Hope the above helps re: linkbuilding, and happy to answer any questions you
might have
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-02-22
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: 100 Pound Social SEO and Potential Client
msg_id: 177c8c3c059f2f56
---

Hi Victoria,
Hope all is well. I know I owe you some follow up on a few points in the
meantime, but I wanted to get a call on the diary for end  of this
week/beginning of next. Are there some times that might be ideal on your end?
Also, I wanted to enquire as to if the copywriting and social media management
for this company might be something your team would generally take on -
http://brydg.com/. I oversee their general digital marketing, and we have some
templated social posts and graphics we can produce relatively easily on an
ongoing basis and that's fed by the company's core operation of brokering loans
through their platform. We do have an in house graphic designer who does pretty
solid work, there is very little thought going in to  how to make our content
better on social media as we really don't have much resource there. Let me know!
Looking forward to catching up,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-19
to: Alex Kay <alex.kay@brydg.com>
subject: latitiude email draft
msg_id: 177b86ce437b2d12
---

Hi ______,
We’re excited to announce the launch of Lattitude, the Local Area Transactional
Intelligence Ticker from Brydg Labs. Lattttude  helps real estate investors,
developers or just home owners  better understand a property’s value in real
time by providing a detailed, customisable, and up-to-date price per square foot
ticker.
Try it for yourself here: {LINKLINKLINK}.
Our official beta launch will be on Monday of next week, but as a friend of
Brydg, we wanted to share access ahead of time. Do let us know if you see any
bugs, as it’s a brand new product. Or if you have any ideas for how we could
make it even better or more useful for you, please don’t hesitate to reach out.
Thanks for joining us on our journey to bring better data to real estate
finance!
Best,

date: 2021-02-17

subject: Re: Design Mentoring for an accelerator

Hi Ashley,

Thanks for the heads up, I’ll find someone else. We probably won’t be
running a cohort in the summer, so a couple of months from now probably
doesn’t make sense in terms of timing, but I can let you know if we need
mentors for the next cohort we run.

Hope all is well!

Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-16
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: TechTree update #3 2020 wrap up; 20k GBP in MRR, pre-seed round with top angels and more
msg_id: 177ab878a8b9b8fa
---

FYI

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Pawel Tomczuk - TechTree <pawel@mytechtree.io>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2021 at 08:59
Subject: TechTree update #3 2020 wrap up; 20k GBP in MRR, pre-seed round
with top angels and more
To: Matthew <matteisn@gmail.com>


H2'2020 has been very busy - we have steadily grown our MRR and got backed
by ex founders & execs from Monzo, Uber Eats & StackOverflow and more.
Dear investors and friends,
It has been a while since we last shared an update. I hope you'll find this
summary of 2020 a good overview of what has happened at TechTree. In a
word, it was *a lot* - you can find the full update here
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=36a4318fec&e=4464ef8ea9>.
I hope you're going to enjoy reading about it.
Download our 2020 Investor & Friends summary
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=0719835f2f&e=4464ef8ea9>
*The TLDR; *
- we have grown our MRR to 20k GBP (see below)
- we continue to increase all metrics of our alpha version - (see below)
- we've continued to grow our key initiative Show the Code - where we've
already partnered with 10+ VCs, including the likes of Northzone, Local
Globe, EQT Ventures, Seedcamp, etc. (more details here
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=f762030f37&e=4464ef8ea9>
)
- we've piloted our new initiative, "Breaking Bias" (more details here
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=e252e20094&e=4464ef8ea9>
)
- we started to pilot our mentoring feature and got some fantastic feedback
from early users (more details here
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=6adec1504a&e=4464ef8ea9>
)
- we've signed up over 100 community partners
- companies on our platform now include likes of Klarna, Peakon, Wayflyer,
Personio, Trustpilot, Tessian, and many more (over 50)
*TLDR; continued - We closed our pre-seed round*
Some amazing angel investors have trusted us and are already proving to be
amazingly valuable with their advice & introductions:

- Gary Dolman
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=97d33943cd&e=4464ef8ea9>,
Co-Founder
of Monzo (their ex-CFO)
- Jambu Palaniappan
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=6dd99b9710&e=4464ef8ea9>,
Managing Partner at Omers Ventures, one of the first 100 employees at Uber
and ex-Head of Uber Eats for EMEA
- Ben Kizltug
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=2810a22640&e=4464ef8ea9>,
Ex-Head of EMEA Sales at Stackoverflow (and their first salesperson in
EMEA)
- Aaron Neale
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=7bde1e5e7a&e=4464ef8ea9>,
VP of Talent at Improbable

**What's quite cool is that one of our clients and one of our communities
has also invested in us after using our product. *

*Our commercial traction*

While we're still very much in our Alpha version - we've managed to work
with a small group of clients, who have enabled us to increase the revenue
while also giving us excellent product feedback that has driven our
roadmap.

Last year helped us to understand who our key customer segment is - post
Series A / B scale-up companies rather than startups.We're incredibly proud
that we could make these few clients happy, as you can see from the
references above.
*Our user growth*
Despite still being in an Alpha phase, we're observing growing engagement
in the users, who after being recommended by the communities or
participating in the events hosted on our platform are joining our waitlist
- we already have over 1000 users who signed up in the last 6 months with
zero promotion.

We were also able to bring a lot of great companies to post content and
jobs on our platform thanks to our partnerships with VCs.

We've also started to pilot our mentoring feature - we have over 30 mentors
who have signed up and some amazing feedback from mentees who took part in
it.
*But most importantly - We have built an amazing team!*

We grew from *1 to 11 people in the last 12 months* building an amazing and
diverse foundation for our further growth:
- 5 people on the engineering and 5 on the commercial side without me
- 7 nationalities, 4 ethnical backgrounds,
- parents to 7 kids and having one grandchild
- 20 years of age difference between the youngest and oldest employee
- one weightlifting national champion

We still need to do better, especially when it comes to gender diversity
and it's something we're very focused on.
Lastly: as you can see above 2020 has been a very exciting year and if
you're receiving this newsletter it means you had a part in by spending
time with us on calls, sharing your feedback, giving us a chance or
introducing to other people. While this is just a beginning of our journey,
we would not be where we are if not you, so I wanted to say big

*Thank you! *

P.S. Reach out to us if you want to know what we're planning for 2021 -
we'll keep you posted on the progress.
View this email in your browser
<https://mailchi.mp/2de5806bcef8/techtree-update-3-2020-wrap-up-20k-gbp-in-mrr-pre-seed-round-with-top-angels-and-more?e=4464ef8ea9>
[image: Twitter]
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=cf2db3024c&e=4464ef8ea9>
[image: Facebook]
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=98014eb803&e=4464ef8ea9>
[image: Website]
<https://mytechtree.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4135e2ef2145fe862c7d0c864&id=e7ddb63804&e=4464ef8ea9>
*Copyright © 2021 TechTree, All rights reserved.*
We're sending you this email as we have been in touch about TechTree and I
thought it might interesting for you to receive this updates.

*Our mailing address is:*
TechTree
23 Byfeld Gardens
<https://www.google.com/maps/search/23+Byfeld+Gardens+London?entry=gmail&source=g>
London
<https://www.google.com/maps/search/23+Byfeld+Gardens+London?entry=gmail&source=g>,

date: 2021-02-15
to: Kevin Gardner <Kevin@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Shield Accountancy - Payment plan for Eisner Capital & Ansible Advisory
msg_id: 177a482d35106afa
---

Hi Kevin,
That generally sounds fine, and appreciate the patience, it's been a very hectic
period.
For Ansible Advisory, I'm hoping to finalize a payment plan for outstanding
bills with HMRC (I wasted about 20 hours on hold with them in Dec and Jan), so
it would help to have that nailed down to know what my monthly obligations will
be under that before. If we could lower to 600 per month, I could probably agree
to that now.
For Eisner Capital, I can pay this immediately. is there any way to consolidate
the outstanding balance into a single invoice (or just send me the invoice
numbers so I can easily look them up)?
Best,
Matthew

Sorry for the double emails - realized I misread my calendar - I do gave a

---
date: 2021-02-14
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: Shuffling call
msg_id: 177a224dc7aa1c30
---

Hi Victoria,
Thanks for the well-wishes - feeling a bit better, although still under the
weather and awaiting test results to come back.
Looking forward to chatting through all of that tomorrow. I did have a
conflicting call come up for my day job at NTT, and wanted to see if we could 
shuffle to either sometime between 11am and 1pm or anytime after 3pm? Let me
know if that might be possible, and sorry for the late notice.
Hope all is well!
Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-12
to: Idan Beck <idanbeck@gmail.com>
subject: Mentoring startup for AEA
msg_id: 1779693f53d482c3
---

Hey Idan,
Hope you're doing well!
I have a startup for the Algorand accelerator I thought might be interesting for
you to mentor - they're based in California (I think one of the two founders is
in the bay, if not both), and basically working on infrastructure for chain
interoperability/cross-platform assets, starting with a PoC use case around
NFTs.
https://ockams.com/

Let me know if you might be open to talking to their technical founder. Also as
a heads up, we've decided to take a more free-form approach with mentoring -
really just providing an initial introduction to set up a call, and leaving it
to you/the startup to decide if there's ongoing interest to keep in touch (but
we would certainly encourage it, and it would be good to hear your thoughts and
feedback after the call either way).
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-02-11

subject: Re: Design Mentoring for an accelerator
msg_id: 1778eba86b6addd1

Hi Ashley,
Just sent along a calendar invite. Let me know if it doesn't work for some
reason, and looking  forward to it!
We certainly can't force our startups to use anything, but we'd be more than
happy to offer a galaxy to them and to pin it in our slack chats/etc (as well as
refer any startups interested in setting up their own to you should there be
any).
Looking forward to the session
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-02-09
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: Shuffling call
msg_id: 1778909aa5bafe86

Sorry for the double emails - also added comments on the blog
here : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-OccqoAVB05NYioFFCgVn4JcHVv-FhibqKx1NPo7WMM/edit

---
date: 2021-02-09
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Re: Shuffling call
msg_id: 17788fe191f7f724
---

Hi Victoria,
Taking a quick look through the case studies page and a randomly selected
individual case study:
Case Studies Page: * How SaaS Business ContractsWise Reaches New Prospects Month
   on Month
   
 * I would change this to: How SaaS Business ContractsWise
   Grows New Sales Month on Month
 * HR Recruitment Agency The Talent Crossing Sees
   “Exponential” Audience Growth with Professional Social Media
   
 * I would change this to: HR Recruitment Agency The Talent
   Crossing Sees Exponential Digital Growth with Expert Social Media
 * In general, I'm not doing much here apart from trying to
   remove filler words (if, and, the, if, with, of, for, etc) and replace any
   words that I can with words from the list
 * It also might be a good idea to add a line or two of
   subheading text at the top of the page


On the individual case studies pages - using ContractsWise as an example: * I
   would make the "Case Study" label at the top left corner of the page 
   clickable back to the main case study hub page (or consider having
   breadcrumbs to categories of case studies if there are enough)
 * I
   like the structure data at the top - especially the hyperlink to the plan
   page :) - one potential improvement here - the data label (i.e. Industry,
   Website, Plan) are in a heading format CSS class (h4 tagged) signifying they
   are important text, but the actual data (i.e. SaaS, www.contractswise.com,
   the plan) are in unemphasized paragraph CSS class (p tagged) - ideally, the
   data is actually more important than the data labels, but the best solution
   is probably to have them both as h4 text, but bold the labels and unbold the
   actual data (let me know if you'd like me to clarify this a bit)
 * I
   would potentially try to use more descriptive section headings - currently
   Case Study, Onboarding, Working With are good for readibility, but not very 
   discriptive. Consider using a 2 part section header to allow for consistency
   across case studies but more keyword rich heading tags on the site (i.e.
   Onboarding: Defining a SaaS Social Strategy - completely pulled that out of
   nowhere, but hopefully conveys the idea of using longer more descriptive
   words in these big emphasized text blocks)
 * 
   Generally this looks good. I imagine there might not be total flexibility
   with the quotes, but there (and otherwise) I'd say there are some sentences
   that could be more concise/keyword rich. That being said, if it would hurt 
   readibility or clash with how you like to write, I would say that there is
   probably enough keyword density in here to be effective. That being said,
   trying to replace any words possible with more targeted words from the
   keyword research list we have is always good where possible (I'd say the
   introduction section is actually pretty good about using rich keywords, but
   this falls of a  little in the later sections). It will likely feel a little
   repetitive (and as mentioned, if you're using the exact same phrase more than
   5 or 10 times, it might be worth trying to permutate  it a little bit into a
   slightly different form or synonym).
 * 
   Also, ideally the case study pages will be in the case studies subdirectory -
   i.e. change from https://100poundsocial.com/case-study-contractswise/
   to https://100poundsocial.com/case-studies/contractswise/


Let me know if there's anything in particular in these you'd like additional
feedback on, and hope this helps!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-09

subject: Re: Brad <> Matthew

---

hope that’s okay (or do you want to plan to start at 5.15?)

Best,

date: 2021-02-08

subject: Fwd: DOGE and Binance Smart Chain new pool

---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Binance <do_not_reply@mailer.binance.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Feb 2021 at 16:49
Subject: DOGE and Binance Smart Chain new pool
To: <matteisn@gmail.com>


On BakerySwap
*Binance Smart Chain & DOGE 150% APY on BakerySwap*



*Today we are presenting you an AMM (Automated Market Maker) called
BakerySwap <https://www.bakeryswap.org/#/bakery>which allows you to earn
free tokens for providing liquidity on Binance Smart Chain.*

*Binance Smart Chain in numbers*

   - To-date BSC features *100+ projects*, 60 of which process large
   numbers of users and transaction volumes.
   - BSC processed *771,000 transactions* in its recent ATH, i.e. almost *60%
   of Ethereum’s numbers.*
   - *The average gas fee per transaction is as low as $0.07*, which is
   about 2-3% the gas fee of Ethereum.
   - As of January 2021, BSC supports 42 cryptocurrencies across the chain
   to BSC.
   - The total value of cross-chain assets has reached *$1.37 billion*, and
   the total market value of Binance BTokens has reached $556 million.

BSC offers a *high-performance and low-fee blockchain network that’s
compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine*.


New to Binance Smart Chain? Click Here
<https://academy.binance.com/en/articles/an-introduction-to-binance-smart-chain-bsc>
<https://www.binance.com/fr/support-center>

*Connect your Binance Smart Chain wallet to BakerySwap and start earning
free tokens*


*Bakery Swap an AMM on Binance has recently launched a pool where investors
can stake their DOGE coin to get free tokens.*

*How to participate?*

   - Transfer DOGE to Binance Smart Chain network (you can use Binance
   Bridge <https://www.binance.org/en/bridge>)
   - *Go to Bakery Swap *
   - *Connect your wallet*
   - *Get LP tokens BAKE-DOGE BLP*
   - *Approve BAKE-DOGE BLP*
   - *Stake BAKE-DOGE BLP*
   - Harvest the tokens you are earning by providing liquidity

But be careful BakerySwap is an innovative and new project. Invest at your
own risk.


Go to BakerySwap <https://www.bakeryswap.org/#/bakery>
<https://trustwallet.com/>

*To start discovering the Binance Smart Chain you need a Binance Smart
Chain Wallet*


*Please find 2 guides that will help you connect to the Binance Smart Chain
blockchain network:*

   - *How to Set Up and Use Trust Wallet for Binance Smart Chain
   <https://www.binance.com/en/blog/421499824684901157/How-to-Set-Up-and-Use-Trust-Wallet-for-Binance-Smart-Chain>*
   - *Connecting MetaMask to Binance Smart Chain
   <https://academy.binance.com/en/articles/connecting-metamask-to-binance-smart-chain>*



*Struggling transferring DOGE to Binance Smart Chain?*


Our Telegram chat has more than 14 800 people who discuss about Binance
Smart Chain, blockchain and cryptocurrencies every day.

Inside those groups you can chat with the *Binances Angels,* some
volunteers crypto expert  who will support you in discovering
cryptocurrencies and decentralized finance.


Ask your questions to Binance Chain Community
<https://t.me/BinanceDEXchange>

Thanks again for your support,


Binance Team

PS: If you have more questions about Doge and BakerySwap you can *ask them
directly  <https://t.me/bakeryswap>*

*Join us on our social media *
[image: Telegram] <https://t.me/BinanceDEXchange>

*Risk warning : *Cryptocurrency trading is subject to high market risk.
Binance will make best efforts to choose high-quality coins, but will not
be responsible for your trading losses. Please trade with caution.

*Note:* Please be aware of phishing sites and always make sure you are
visiting the official Binance.com <http://binance.com/> website when
entering sensitive data: www.binance.com.

If you no longer wish to receive these emails, unsubscribe
<https://etrack01.com/track/unsubscribe.do?%3D>
.
© 2017 - 2021 Binance.com Tous droits réservés
URL：www.binance.com E-mail：support@binance.zendesk.com

date: 2021-02-08
to: brad@cledara.com
subject: Re: Brad <> Matthew
msg_id: 17782aa59b7f91a8
---

Hi Brad,
Terribly sorry - have been having some internet issues today and only was able
to get on via my phone now - sorry to have missed you.
Could do  same  time  tomorrow or Friday? Or feel free to suggest some other
times.
Or ultimately, if we're looking specifically at reselling data to NTT, what
might be useful is an understanding of what you might be able to provide. You
had sent some charts previously that were nice, but not really enough detail for
our appetite. We'd be looking for benchmarks like % of spend by software
category, churn rate by software category, potentially those same metrics split
by client industry and geography, etc (and ideally the larger the companies in
the sample, the better). Happy to chat through those ideas in more detail if
that jogs some thoughts, or if it's not really within the current analytics
capabilities, we can pick up the conversation when it might be.
Best,
Matthew

(hopefully not COVID, but taking a test today). Could we move our call to the
same time next Monday?
Sorry for the late notice - hopefully should have everything wrapped up by then
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-02-05
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: time sensitive... ready (and needing) to transition from My Tribe
msg_id: 17771f9cb1b4e378
---

I’ve talked to Mike, he has said he’s happy to help you migrate your
contacts - however, changing the domain and hosting is something controlled
by you - to change the domain there is no code required, you just follow
the instructions in the link you sent me. I’m not sure how wix handles
hosting, it’s probably free but I really don’t know

As I told you, a refund is unlikely. However, I did talk to mike and he was
open to it. To be 100% clear, I will effectively be paying for your refund.
It is not really reasonable to expect a refund in this scenario - if you
were my client I would tell you to fuck off (although I probably would have
also had slightly better lines of communication), and this is why I told
you multiple times to get a refund in the fall (and why I never ever said
you should use Mike for your website - just that you should talk to him and
see what’s out there)

Matthew

date: 2021-02-03
to: Gene Teare <gene@crunchbase.com>, Tina Ou <tinaou@crunchbase.com>
subject: License purchasing (and data sharing) with NTT DATA accelerator
msg_id: 17769e8800b49011
---

Hi Gene and Tina,
Thanks for your help getting set up with the Algorand Europe Accelerator. We've
just picked our first cohort, and will send along a list with some basic
information as soon as we finish onboarding them.
I had another question - one of my other roles is running the
analytics/modeling/back offices for NTT DATA UK's "startup accelerator" program
(it's really more of a non-equity sales acceleration program for enterprise SaaS
and Cloud software startups). We wanted to purchase 2-3 licenses for Crunchbase
Pro for the program, as well as see if there's any potential for discount,
collaboration, cross-promotion, etc as we move towards the public launch of the
program.
Let me know who would be best to talk to on that front!
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

Best,

Matthew

date: 2021-02-02
to: Alex Kay <alex.kay@brydg.com>
subject: Brydg Monthly Digital Marketing Budget Ideas
msg_id: 17762f1d46ad3471
---

The below is EXCLUDING retargeting budgets, and assuming no cost for design,
web development, email tools, etc

100: * $15/month on Buffer (social scheduling)
 * $30 per month on LinkedIn boosts
 * $30 per month on Twitter boosts
 * $30 per month on Instagram boosts



250 * All of the above +
 * $100 on small targeted ad buys (event sponsorships, pay-to-play
   journalists, press wires, etc)
   
 * Additional $50 for post boosting across channels



500 * All of the above +
 * Additional $100 ($50 per channel) on social media boosts
 * $150 for 100 Pound Social production and channel management



Others to consider: * Mail merge/bulk sending tools
 * Zapier
 * Airtable

---
date: 2021-02-02
to: Cristina Vila Vives <cristina@cledara.com>
subject: Recruitment firm for CFO
msg_id: 17762bd86751f0a6
---

Hi Cristina,
Hope all is well, and congratulations on the continued traction and growth.
2 quick questions:
1) my friend  (who could eventually be an interesting mid-to-senior marketing
hire for you) does some work for a boutique executive search agency  that runs a
CFO community, and noticed the CFO job post on LinkedIn and offered to intro 
you. Although with all the inbound applications, I imagine this might not be an
issue :)
2) I'm doing our annual POs for my department at NTT, and wanted to follow up on
our conversation around data sharing. Basically, we probably want some
statistics on churn rate by company type, software sector, a few other things
like that, to help support some of our models and presentations. We definitely
don't have a monster budget, but I thought it could be a nice validation of use 
case  for data-as-a-product if you wanted to show a big client on it.
Hope all is well, and of course let me know if there's anything I can help
with :)
Matthew

---
date: 2021-02-01
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Cledara January 2021 Update
msg_id: 1775f4bdec489c5d
---

FYI

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Cristina Vila <cristina@cledara.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Feb 2021 at 19:10
Subject: Cledara January 2021 Update
To: Founders @ Cledara <founders@cledara.com>


Dear Friends of Cledara,

🚀🚀🚀 Highlights:

   -

   Team – we’re delighted to have received more than 700 applicants for our
   open positions. We expect to extend offers for Customer Success, Product,
   Marketing and Support roles in the coming days to candidates who will be
   joining us from companies like Wefox, Booking.com and Travelperk. They will
   join our newly hired Head of Sales (who’s already killing it - 4 demos
   booked today) and 3 new engineers that all joined Cledara in January.
   -

   Growth – We closed January up 18% month-on-month. 20 January was our
   largest GMV day ever (£171,346) and 7 the top 20 GMV days in Cledara’s
   history happened during the month.
   -

   Logos –  Havaianas’ EMEA business became a customer during the month as
   they look to implement processes and tools to manage SaaS ahead of a
   digital transformation project that will take them to the cloud. As the
   first global brand using Cledara, we will be releasing testimonials and
   case studies shortly.


🙏🙏🙏 Thanks

   -

   Thanks to Jordi and Ari for opening your network to us to help with
   referencing of our new team members. Also thanks to JeeYoon for helping
   us interview product managers.
   -

   Thanks to Todd for a game changing discussion about positioning and
   product marketing and double thanks for agreeing to making it a monthly
   occurrence.
   -

   Thanks to Pedro for reaching out to help us with our migration to Zendesk


📦📦📦 Product

   -

   We’re getting ready to launch our new sign up flow, replacing the
   existing one that has been with us almost since the MVP. Soon our customers
   will be able to get into the product and experience many of our non-payment
   functionality before completing KYC. This will reduce our time to value for
   the customer and help them see much of the product in action before
   uploading company documents to us.


📡📡📡 Coverage

   -

   Cledara (and Cristina!) was listed by the Spanish print edition of
   Forbes Magazine as one of their 21 changemakers in 2021
   <https://forbes.es/empresas/85464/21-change-makers-para-2021/>
   -

   Continuing our Spanish-language coverage, Cristina was interviewed on
   the Itnig podcast <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lujP_xrtn1A> by the
   founder of Factorial.
   -

   Moving to the other side of the Atlantic, Brad made an appearance on the For
   Fintech’s Sake podcast
   <https://www.forfintechsake.com/p/ending-saas-madness-with-brad-van> to
   talk about the origin story of Cledara (and pizza).



Until next month!

Cristina & Brad

---
date: 2021-01-29
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Company Name Changes
msg_id: 177509082290b905
---

Hey Antonio,
Thanks for your time earlier today and helping me start to get more on top of
things.
I've finally gotten my shit together and come up with a company name : Vang.
Can I change: 1. Eisner Capital Limited to Vang Capital Limited
 2. Ansible Advisory Limited to Vang Advisory Limited

Let me know if you need anything from me to do this.
Also, if you're interested in the Georgia voting backstory
- https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2018/nov/10/georgia-election-recount-stacey-abrams-brian-kemp
- it's just common knowledge that if you live in the urban areas of Atlanta,
you're waiting at least an hour to vote - it's why I didn't vote in 2008. And if
you're in a heavily minority district, you're lucky if the polling center even
ends up being open (and you haven't been purged - Brian Kemp literally managed
to purge himself for the 2016 election ahaha, and those tactics did not stop,
even despite judge  orders). COVID probably helped the Dems in the election this
year by allowing urban districts to vote by mail and spread early voting out
over time, coupled with the fact that probably about 10% of the people I know in
Georgia were directly volunteering driving people to polls, helping them fill
out voter registrations, and young and female voter turnout was up huge b/c
georgia is trying to restrict abortion and gay rights (and Atlanta has a huge
and surprisingly, at least historically but not the last decade or so, not
extremely liberal gay population), etc. But the GOP has been in charge of the
election infrastructure and the districting  (which has helped them keep
stranglehold  on the election infrastructure) for decades there now, and each
state manages its own election systems and processes. But without all of that,
there's been a trend left in Goergia  for years, and slide 8-10 and 17 say a lot
why (the other piece is that the influx of population is about 75% college
educated young singles and families). Anwyays, i know way too much about this
(also Jon Ossoff was 3 years above me at my school, so it's all just so fucking
stupid to me when the GOP candidates there try to peg a jewish, extremely
popular, and well adjusted kid from my pacifist mpntessori-ish high school and
is literally one of the most straight laced person I've ever met and made
corruption expose documentaries for years as somehow assosciated with ISIS). Not
to mention the GOP candidates in GA did themselves no favours by doing millions
in insider trading off their Senate COVID briefing last Feb. Anyways, rant over,
but thought you'd find it interesting.
Have a good weekend!
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

subject: Design Mentoring for an accelerator
msg_id: 1772774a8dca3ec6
---

Hi Ashley,
Hope all is well with you and building Orbital!
I'm running an accelerator program this winter/spring, and was wondering if you
might be available to give an hour or so workshop on design (probably with more
of an emphasis on product/UI) the week of 22 February.
You can learn a little more about the program here - we're just in the final
stages of picking the cohort.
Let me know if you might be open to it, or if you have any questions. You can
learn more about the program here: https://www.algorand-europe-accelerator.com/
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-01-20
to: Amr Bakr <amr.bakr@7keema.com>
subject: Re: Ending our Menrtorship
msg_id: 1771f96d7de8aabd
---

Hi Amr,
It does not seem like you have a grasp of the events that occurred over
timelines we are looking at.
I never received any message from you yesterday, nor did I the previous 2 weeks.
The week before that, you cancelled at the last minute, and the week before
that, I cancelled for Christmas.
It has been 5 weeks since our last call, and given your lack of communication
and engagement, I believe my time would be better spent on founders who value it
more.
Not communicating with your stakeholders is a big issue in healthcare (one that
I watched prevent one of my investments with a great product from breaking into
unicorn valuation before they exited) - hopefully you can take this as a
learning experience.
Best of luck,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-20
to: Amr Bakr <amr.bakr@7keema.com>, tamer.shawer@7keema.com, karim.yehia@7keema.com
subject: Ending our Menrtorship
msg_id: 1771f629e7db5c57
---

Hi,
I just wanted to give you a heads up that I will be ending our mentorship
engagement, as you have bailed on our call the past 3 weeks.
Best of luck to you in the future.
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <Antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: VAT Returns - Completed
msg_id: 1771c9c7393eb3b5
---

Hi Antonio,
Sorry for the delay on my side - have been quite snowed under.
Crypto has been good the past few months - hoping I can play altseason for a bit
and then get out before the next drop but well we'll see.
Re: time for a call, are you free some times Thursday or Friday?
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Robert Stineman <robert@explorersurgical.com>
subject: Re: Great chatting with you yesterday!
msg_id: 1771bfe80fb16863
---

Hi Rob,
No worries at all - how's Thursday at 8am CST?
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Freddie Smith <fs@foro.dev>
subject: Re: Intro from Rishi
msg_id: 1771ab3eaf8a7479
---

Hi Freddie,

That works for me - what’s the best way to reach you?

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Adaeze Zita Edokwe <adaeze@seedstars.com>
subject: Seedstars mentoring
msg_id: 1771aaab9a865d96
---

Hi Adaeze,
Due to 7keema's lack of engagement and respect for my time, I must ask to end my
mentorship assignment with them. I'm happy to mentor another startup that will
respond to messages and attend meetings.
This is unfortunate, as 7keema has a lot of work to do on their KPI tracking and
analytics (they're only tracking Facebook acquisitions, they don't measure ROAS
and their acquisition strategy is heavily paid, they don't track LTV), and I
have a great deal of experience scaling medical platforms, which would have been
fun to collaborate on. However, it's not a good use of my time to attend calls
they do not attend and provide no notice that they will not be attending.
I have had 3 major exits in the healthcare space, and was even considering
seeking putting  some money in to 7keema when we first connected based on the
idea. However, regulatory-intensive and relationship-oriented industries like
healthcare do not like flakey founders, so that's not really of interest to me
anymore.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Freddie Smith <fs@foro.dev>
subject: Re: Intro from Rishi
msg_id: 17718a43226ecb22
---

I'm London, so GMT. i'm pretty flexible on Thursday, if there are some times
then that would work for you.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-19
to: Connie Wright <connie.wright@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Your company's annual Confirmation Statement is due
msg_id: 177189fb8baab4dc
---

Thanks Connie - I have paid this from Ansible Advisory. Do I need to pay it
once each from both of my companies?
Best,
Matthew

date: 2021-01-12
to: Ben Davis <ben@trynow.io>
subject: Fwd: Ecom acquisitions
msg_id: 176f7833fec41209
---

Hey man - hope all is well and your year is off to a good start. Things
seem to be growing nicely at TryNow!

I have couple of friends who are looking to purchase one (or a couple) of
mid-sized e-commerce businesses. You’re closer in that space than most I
know, so shot in the dark, but if any of your customers are looking for an
exit, maybe interesting? See details below.

Would love to catch up some time. Best of luck til then!

Matthew





Introducing Growth Shop <http://growth.shop>, we build, launch and scale
direct-to-consumer brands that we own or hold major equity stakes in. This
encompasses client businesses (via our agency side), co-owned ventures and
self-owned consumer brands (via our accelerator side).

We're looking to acquire consumer brands with revenues of $100-300k MRR,
with funds available to do so. Things are moving very quickly, with one of
our brands that we acquired up from $110k to $6m last month. Our previous
brand portfolio scaled from $10MM - $300MM in 18 months back in 2018.
Hence, we're looking to extend our portfolio of brands via acquisition.
Types of businesses we’re looking for

   -

   Brands with existing products and established supply chain
   -

   Proof of concept with existing customer base and repeat buyers
   -

   Run by a small group or 1-5 people with low levels of sophistication
   -

   Likely they haven’t taken on external capital at a high level like VC/PE
   -

   We're pretty flexible on consumer brands. Looking for a brand ideally
   with fewer SKU’s where we sell a solution, vs 1000's of products and
   products where we can scale using paid social/YouTube etc. Re AOV, around
   $150 minimum is what we're after, if the AOV lower than $500 we’ll need a
   clear repeat purchase model.

---
date: 2021-01-12
to: Chana Greenberg <chanagreenberg@ymail.com>
subject: Fwd: TechTree funding round update - last 50-100k GBP available
msg_id: 176f7525f215114a
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Pawel Tomczuk <pawel@mytechtree.io>
Date: Sun, 6 Dec 2020 at 10:05
Subject: TechTree funding round update - last 50-100k GBP available
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hi Matt,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to share a brief update on our round -
you can see some of the angels who have already invested alongside you. If
you know any great people who could come onboard - let us know - we plan to
close in the next several weeks.

----

Re our round - we're finishing the raise - having approx. 300k GBP
confirmed, we still have 50-100k GBP (and even about 50k GBP in SEIS) and
we have discussions with various angels to fill it. Some of the angels
already involved are:
Gary Dolman co-founder of Monzo (their ex-CFO)
https://www.linkedin.com/in/gary-dolman-0085957/
Jambu Palaniappan, Managing Partner at Omers Ventures, one of the first 100
employees at Uber and ex-Head of Uber Eats for EMEA
https://www.linkedin.com/in/jambu-palaniappan-7358211a/
Ben Kizltug, ex Head of EMEA Sales at Stackoverflow (and their first
salesperson in EMEA), https://www.linkedin.com/in/benkiziltug/
Francesco Petricari, founder of Silicon Roundabout Ventures and
Siliconroundabout community (15k+ senior engineers in the UK)
https://www.linkedin.com/in/fperticarari/

Advisor
Aaron Neale, VP of Talent at Improbable,
https://www.linkedin.com/in/aaronneale/

-----

*About TechTree*
At TechTree, we're creating a mentoring and community building platform for
devs designed to break bias in tech. By connecting devs with mentors to
hack their careers, while helping devs tell their story rather than polish
CVs, we believe we can make tech a fairer more open place to work.

We're a team of 10, 3 founders, 2 of whom has exited the business in a
similar field before (marketplace, employer branding for devs), and one who
was an early employee in a Recruitment Tech startup which raised over 20m
GBP from investors like Balderton. 5 people on the engineering team.

Our deck is here: https://docsend.com/view/zdmmxrjhk6jeybyp

*The problem we're solving:*

Sense of nonsense - We thought “engineering should be a space where only
merit - your tangible coding and communications skills - matters. Things
like network, brands in which you’ve worked before etc. are not important
(unlike in sales, marketing and a number of other areas). This should be
the perfect ground for a very diverse workforce - and yet it’s the least
diverse space of all professional areas.

Such inaccessibility of jobs for devs from diverse backgrounds is even more
shocking if you consider how big the shortage of the supply is - 1 000 000
unfilled tech roles in US and approx. 600 000 in Western Europe.

We believe this is absurd - and we hope that TechTree can make things
normal.

*Commercial summary:*

   -

   Signed up 80+ community partners enabling reach to 400k engineers (150k
   engineers coming from diversity-focused communities)
   -

   30+ mentors who are successful engineers from top tech companies
   (Google, Palantir, Skype), top startups (Hopin, Zego), or exited technical
   founders
   -

   Partnering with top VCs (Seedcamp, Local Globe, Northzone, EQT Ventures,
   and several more) who are hosting “Show & Tell” events on our platform -
   engineers from their portfolio companies are showing what they’re building
   and discussing it with the engineering audience. Clients showcasing on our
   events include likes of Hopin, Tessian, Peakon, Cleo, TrustPilot or Personio
   -

   We’ve helped our clients such as Hopin, Advent International, hire great
   engineers and generated over 180k GBP in revenue, 20k GBP average monthly
   revenue from June.
   -

   In November we have started to pilot our subscription offering and
   already have 2 customers paying 1000 GBP a month.


*Fundraising summary:*

   -

   We're post termsheet and completed DD with a pre-seed fund started by an
   experienced executive who was the CEO in two companies with 1bn USD+
   marketcap ( media and HR space), he started his career as one of the first
   30 employees at NYTimes.com and is an ex- Marines.
   -

   We're also backed by a group of angels including co-founder of Monzo
   (their ex-CFO), ex-Head of Uber Eats for EMEA, ex Head of EMEA Sales at
   Stackoverflow (and their first sales person in EMEA), VP of Talent at
   Improbable and people from likes of Amazon, Startupbootcamp or Permira
   -

   We’re looking for additional 100-150k GBP from angel investors that can
   add value beyond just money. The total round size is approximately 400k
   GBP. The terms of the round are here:
   https://docsend.com/view/zdmmxrjhk6jeybyp - it's a convertible loan with
   5m GBP cap, 20% discount and 4m GBP long stop conversion date (which is 6
   months if you want to use SEIS / EIS and 18 months if you don't)


Looking forward to hearing from you.

All the best,

Pawel TomczukCo-founder & CEO
+44 7484146499 <+44%201632%20960511>
pawel@mytechtree.io
https://mytechtree.io/
ᐧ

date: 2021-01-08
to: Victoria Lee <victoria@100poundsocial.com>
subject: Rescheduling Friday Call
msg_id: 176df5ee95fdc432
---

Hi Victoria,
Hope all is well with you and your new year is off to a good start.
I've had a bit of a hectic week this week, and unfortunately haven't gotten as
far as I'd hope with the SEO meta tags and titles. Could we reschedule our call
for Tuesday or Wednesday of next week so I can wrap them up over the weekend?
Best,
Matthew

12:30 UK time?
Best,
Matthew

---

---
date: 2021-01-06
to: Robert Stineman <robert@explorersurgical.com>
subject: Re: Rob from ExplORer Surgical
msg_id: 176d88eb8f27c7db
---

Hi Robert,
3:30 CST works fine for me, thanks for the heads up.
Sounds like good timing - I am relatively happy and working on a large project
for the next quarter that I want to see through, but will likely start exploring
what's next for me in summer with an eye of moving back to the states.
Plus always interesting to hear about scaling medtech companies.
Looking forward,
Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-05
to: Amr Bakr <amr.bakr@7keema.com>, tamer.shawer@7keema.com, karim.yehia@7keema.com

Matthew

---
date: 2021-01-04
to: Antonio Kapsamunov <Antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: VAT Returns - Completed
msg_id: 176cce7a37615c43
---

Hey Antonio,

Thanks for this. It’d also be good to catch up to plan the next round of
filings when you get a chance (as US ones will be due again soon, and it
seems that new regulations put in place effective last year mean I actually
was already being double taxed on any profits Ansible Advisory made, which
throws a major wrench in how I have my companies structured).

Also, I’ve been trying to set up a payment plan with HMRC, but have spent
at least 10 hours on hold with them over the past few weeks and still
haven’t been able to talk to anyone. Any suggestions? Is the Covid loan
program you mentioned still available, as maybe it would make sense to do
that and pay any balance I have instead of continuing to try to reach
someone at HMRC.

I do also want to check - I’ve been very bad about keeping my Xero up to
date - will this affect the VAT filings you just submitted?

Best,

Matthew





On Thu, 24 Dec 2020 at 11:37, Antonio Kapsamunov <
Antonio@shieldaccountancy.co.uk> wrote:

Hi Robert,
Sorry for the delay on this - I was a bit off the grid this holiday break on
vacation.
I'm quite flexible Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday of next week between noon and
4pm Chicago time if that works - let me know when might be ideal for you.
Looking forward to hearing more,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner

LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/in/matteisnTwitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisn
UK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583  





On Tue, Dec 22, 2020 10:49 PM, Robert Stineman robert@explorersurgical.com 
wrote:
Hi Matt,
Hello from email! Thanks so much for the quick response via LinkedIn - I'm
really looking forward to chatting with you and determining if there might be a
fit here!
I am actually taking some extended time off for the holidays, but perhaps we can
connect the first week of January? I am based in Chicago and am free Monday the
4th any time after 1pm CST, but I realize that is your evening. I am also free
any time in the morning CST of the 5th or the 7th of January (between 1pm and
6pm UK time).
Let me know what works best for you. Very excited to chat!
Rob
